Highlights: Chapel Bridge (Kapellbrücke, Lucerne's visual signature, 14th-century covered wooden bridge crossing the Reuss diagonally as it leaves the lake, one of Europe's oldest covered bridges, 204m, 17th-century historical paintings under the roof, partially rebuilt after a fire in 1993, paired with the Wasserturm 13th-century octagonal tower), Spreuer Bridge (Spreuerbrücke, 2nd 15th-century wooden bridge, 17th-century Dance of Death paintings), Old town (Altstadt, medieval alleys, squares with fountains, painted façades), Lion of Lucerne (Löwendenkmal, monumental sculpture from 1820 carved in the rock commemorating the 800 Swiss mercenaries who fell defending Louis XVI at the Tuileries Palace in 1792, Mark Twain called it "the most mournful and moving piece of stone in the world"), Musegg Wall (the old 14th-century fortifications with 9 towers, accessible on foot for free, panorama), KKL Lucerne (Culture and Convention Centre by Jean Nouvel 1998, acoustic concert hall, Lucerne Festival every August), Verkehrshaus (Swiss Museum of Transport, the most visited in Switzerland). Essential excursions: Mount Pilatus (2128m, accessible by cable car from Kriens 30 min, or by the world's steepest cog railway 48% from Alpnachstad — operating May-November, spectacular panorama over 73 alpine peaks), Mount Rigi (1798m, "Queen of the Mountains", Europe's oldest cog railway since 1871, panorama over 13 lakes), Lake Lucerne cruise on historic paddle steamers (since 1837, several ports, all the way to Flüelen), Engelberg and Mount Titlis (3020m, glacier accessible by cable car, summer skiing possible). Try Luzerner Chügelipastete (vol-au-vent), rösti, fondue, Rivella (national whey-based drink), Suchard chocolate. Zurich airport (ZRH) 1h away by direct train.
interlaken:en: Interlaken, Swiss town between Lakes Brienz and Thun in the Bernese Oberland: 6000 inhabitants at 568m, the main tourist base for visiting Jungfraujoch and the Oberland (Grindelwald, Wengen, Mürren 30-45 min away). August is my favourite month (8.1/10): 16-25°C, 49% rain, terraces, cruises on the 2 lakes (Brienz and Thun), paragliding (Interlaken = world capital of paragliding with central landing on Höhematte the central meadow). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 14-25°C, climate moderated by the lakes. From December to March, expect 1-9°C and 45-54% precipitation often as snow: skiing at the neighbouring resorts (Grindelwald 30 min, Mürren 45 min), little snow in Interlaken itself (568m), Bahnhofstrasse Christmas market. Highlights: Höhematte (large central meadow with view of the Jungfrau-Mönch-Eiger), Harder Kulm (1322m, 8 min funicular, panoramic view over the 2 lakes and the Oberland), Schynige Platte (2076m, cog railway from Wilderswil, panorama with alpine botanical garden), Lake Thun (cruises on the Blümlisalp 1906 steamboat), Lake Brienz (cruises to Iseltwald + Giessbachfälle waterfall), Trümmelbachfälle 30 min away (10 intra-glacial waterfalls accessible by lift). Activities: tandem paragliding from Beatenberg or Niederhorn (landing at Höhematte, 200 CHF), canyoning in the Saxetenbach, rafting, Jungfraujoch by train (3h round trip, 220 CHF). Try rösti, raclette, fondue, Bündnerfleisch (Grisons cured beef). Logistics: Interlaken 2h from Zurich, 1h from Bern by train.
monaco:en: Monaco, sovereign principality of 2 km²: 38,000 inhabitants (one of the world's smallest countries), enclaved in France on the Italian border, ruled by the Grimaldi dynasty since 1297. July-August are my favourite months (9.5/10): 21-28°C, 11-14% rain, sea at 24°C, Formula 1 Grand Prix late May (the most prestigious on the calendar, urban circuit race at 290 km/h in the narrow streets, hotel prices x10 during the event). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 17-28°C, hotel prices x3-x5 throughout the season, events (Yacht Show late September, ATP tennis tournament April). From December to February, expect 4-13°C and 19-25% rain: mild for France, more peaceful atmosphere, prices halved. Highlights: Casino de Monte-Carlo (Belle Époque, 1863, Place du Casino with Hôtel de Paris and Café de Paris, casino access 18€ + dress code required), Prince's Palace (changing of the guard every day at 11:55, apartments open to the public outside season) and Cathedral (where Grace Kelly and Rainier III are buried), Oceanographic Museum (Cousteau, founded by Prince Albert I, perched on the cliff), Exotic Garden (giant cacti, Observatory Cave), Japanese Garden, Casino Café de Paris (free slot machines, more accessible atmosphere). Old Monaco (the "Rock") with its Fort Antoine. Sport: AS Monaco football at Stade Louis II. Try barbagiuan (chard-stuffed pastry), socca (neighbouring Niçoise pancake), pissaladière. Logistics: Monaco 25 min from Nice by train (spectacular underground station), no airport (Nice 25 min by helicopter for 150€/person).
hambourg:en: Hamburg, Germany's 2nd city and largest port: 1.9 million inhabitants in the north, on the Elbe 100 km from the North Sea. Hansa-Stadt = free Hanseatic city since the 13th century, special city-state status within the Federal Republic (alongside Berlin and Bremen). More bridges than Venice and Amsterdam combined (2500). UNESCO 2015 for the Speicherstadt warehouse district. June is my favourite month (8.3/10): 13-22°C, 39% rain, ideal temperatures with long days (17h of daylight at the solstice). From June to August are the tourist high season: 13-22°C. From November to March, expect 1-9°C and 43-44% rain: damp cold, little light (4-7h of solar daylight at the December solstice, often grey skies), Christmas market Hauptbahnhof to Rathausmarkt. Highlights: Speicherstadt (UNESCO, the world's largest warehouse complex on stilts 1883-1927, red bricks over the canals, unique atmosphere at twilight), HafenCity (Europe's largest urban renewal project, 157 hectares of former industrial port redeveloped since 2003 with contemporary architecture), Elbphilharmonie (Herzog & de Meuron 2017, signature concert hall with its glass-wave silhouette atop a former brick warehouse, free panoramic plaza at 37m), Rathaus (neo-Renaissance city hall 1897), Reeperbahn (Europe's most famous red-light street in the heart of St Pauli, bars, theatres, historic places where the Beatles played 1960-1962 — Star Club, Indra), Lake Alster (central artificial lake split into Binnenalster + Außenalster, walking/cycling/sailing), St Pauli (alternative cultural-rebellious district, local anti-bourgeois pride), Miniatur Wunderland (the world's largest model railway circuit, 16 km of tracks, 1.5 million visitors/year), Fischmarkt (fish market Sunday 5-9:30am, Hamburg institution with shouting stalls and beers at dawn). Excursions: Lübeck 1h by train (UNESCO, former Hanseatic capital), Bremerhaven and Bremen. Try Labskaus (traditional sailor's dish: salted beef + potato + pickles + herring + fried egg), Franzbrötchen (cinnamon pastry), Fischbrötchen (fish sandwich on a roll), local Astra beer.
cologne:en: Cologne (Köln), Germany's 4th city: 1.1 million inhabitants in North Rhine-Westphalia on the Rhine. Former Roman capital of Lower Germania (Colonia Claudia Ara Agrippinensium founded in AD 50 by Emperor Claudius for his wife Agrippina born here), 2000 years of continuous history. UNESCO 1996 for the Cathedral. More festive and warmer atmosphere than other German cities ("Köln Helau" Rhenish Carnival among Europe's wildest). June is my favourite month (8.3/10): 14-23°C, 40% rain, ideal temperatures. From June to August are the tourist high season: 13-24°C. From November to March, expect 1-10°C and 42-53% rain: damp cold, Domplatz Christmas market = one of Germany's biggest with 4 million visitors over 30 days, Carnival in February-March (5th season, monumental popular celebration, 1 million people in the streets, Rosenmontag parade). Highlights: Cologne Cathedral (Kölner Dom, UNESCO, gothic 1248-1880 = 632 years of construction interrupted several times, 2 spires 157m the world's tallest gothic church until the 20th century, houses the Shrine of the Three Kings = gold sarcophagus containing the relics of the Magi according to tradition, one of the most precious treasures of the Middle Ages, façade blackened by pollution but intentionally left so by the authorities, climb the 533 steps of the south tower for the 100m panorama), Hohenzollernbrücke (rail and pedestrian bridge over the Rhine covered with love padlocks, view of the Dom), Old town Altstadt (rebuilt after WWII destruction at 95%), Town Hall (Germany's oldest 12th century), Romanesque churches (12 11th-13th century Romanesque churches, some of Germany's most beautiful including Groß Sankt Martin, Sankt Aposteln, Sankt Gereon), Roman-Germanic Museum (ancient Gallo-Roman Dionysus mosaics), Schokoladenmuseum (chocolate museum with chocolate fountain, sponsored by Lindt), Eau de Cologne 4711 (eau de cologne created here 1709, boutique-museum Glockengasse), Belgisches Viertel district (alternative, brunch). Excursions: Bonn 30 min away (former West German capital 1949-1991, Beethoven's birthplace), Rhine Valley (UNESCO 1h away), Aachen 1h away (UNESCO, Charlemagne's capital). Try Kölsch (the local beer par excellence, IGP, only drunk in Cologne in 200ml straight glasses, served continuously until you place the coaster on the glass), Halver Hahn (rye sandwich with gouda + pickles + mustard, despite its name "half a rooster" = no meat), Himmel un Ääd (apple purée + black pudding), Reibekuchen (potato cakes).
francfort:en: Frankfurt am Main, financial capital of Germany and the eurozone: 760,000 inhabitants in an urban area of 5.5 million, nicknamed "Mainhattan" for its skyscraper skyline rare in Europe (Germany has only 17 buildings > 150m, of which 14 are in Frankfurt). HQ of the European Central Bank, the Bundesbank, the German stock exchange Deutsche Börse, the trade fair giant Messe Frankfurt. July is my favourite month (8.8/10): 16-26°C, 34% rain, ideal temperatures. From June to August are the tourist high season: 14-26°C. From November to March, expect 0-9°C and 39-44% rain: damp cold, Römerberg Christmas market among Germany's most beautiful. Highlights: Römerberg (medieval square with Römer 13th-century city hall and half-timbered houses, partially rebuilt after 1945), Saint Bartholomew's Cathedral (Kaiserdom, place of election of the Holy Roman Emperors 1356-1792 and of their coronation 1562-1792, 13th-century gothic), Goethe House (birthplace of Wolfgang von Goethe 1749, rebuilt after WWII bombings), Main Tower (199m, the only public panoramic platform on the Frankfurt skyscrapers, café), Museumsufer ("Museum Bank", 13 museums on the south bank of the Main: Städel Museum = one of Germany's most important art museums, Liebieghaus, Schirn Kunsthalle, Frankfurt Filmmuseum), Sachsenhausen district (south bank, Apfelweinwirtschaften = "cider taverns", a local institution for 250 years), Palmengarten (1871 botanical garden, one of Germany's 3 largest), Kleinmarkthalle (covered market), Banking quarter (Bankenviertel around Taunusanlage, skyscrapers: Commerzbank Tower 259m the tallest, Messeturm 257m, Skyper). Major fairs: Buchmesse (the world's most important book fair, October), IAA (auto), Frankfurter Musikmesse, Lighting + Building. Excursions: Heidelberg 1h away, Rhine Valley 1h away (UNESCO Loreley), Wiesbaden 30 min away (spa town). Try local Apfelwein cider ("Ebbelwoi" in Hessian dialect, drunk from the traditional ribbed Bembel jug), Frankfurter Würstchen (long sausage similar to a frankfurter), Grüne Soße (green sauce with 7 herbs, traditional dish served with hard-boiled egg and potato), Handkäse mit Musik (sour cheese with raw onions, gives you wind). Frankfurt airport (FRA) is Germany's largest, Lufthansa hub.
bruges:en: Bruges, in Belgian Flanders: 120,000 inhabitants, UNESCO listed, nicknamed the "Venice of the North" for its 50 km of canals. City preserved from the Middle Ages (15th-century economic collapse paradoxically spared it). August is my favourite month (8.2/10): 15-22°C, 39% rain, terraces on the canals. From July to September, it's the high season: 13-22°C with 35-39% rain, but very dense crowds (Bruges concentrates 8 million visitors/year for 120,000 inhabitants), book hotels 2-3 months ahead. From November to February, expect 2-9°C and 43-49% rain: mild for Belgium, Christmas markets at Markt and Minnewater, "In Bruges" atmosphere (Martin McDonagh's 2008 film popularised the city). Highlights: Markt (main square, 83m Belfry, 366 steps to the view), Burg (gothic city hall, Basilica of the Holy Blood), Beguinage (UNESCO, religious calm), Lake of Love (Minnewater), Grand Place, Groeninge Museum (Flemish Primitives, Van Eyck, Memling), Choco-Story and Frietmuseum. Boat trip on the canals (30 min, 12€) = almost mandatory to capture the city's soul. Try moules-frites, waterzooi, Belgian frites, Bruges waffles (different from the Liège ones), chocolate (at Dumon, Sukerbuyc), beer (3 sisters: Brugse Zot, Straffe Hendrik, Tripel Karmeliet). 1h by train from Brussels. May-June and October are the best crowd/weather compromises.
split:en: Split, Croatia's second city: 180,000 inhabitants, city founded around Diocletian's Palace (UNESCO), residence built by the Roman emperor in AD 305, still inhabited 1700 years later (3000 people still live there). Base for the Dalmatian islands (Hvar, Brač, Vis). June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 19-28°C, 21% rain, sea at 23°C, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (9.3/10) but crowded. August drops to 6.9/10 due to heat (22-31°C) and extreme crowds, hotel prices x4. From November to March, expect 4-13°C and 35-47% rain: mild for the Adriatic, but many hotels and restaurants in hibernation. From April-May and September-October are the best compromises. Highlights: Diocletian's Palace (UNESCO, the largest preserved Roman monument, the emperor's retirement palace, Saint Domnius Cathedral built in his former mausoleum, peristyle, visitable Roman basements where Game of Thrones scenes were shot), Riva (seaside promenade, palm trees, terraces), Marjan Hill (green lung with panoramic view over the city and the islands), Old market Pjaca, Varoš district (old traditional quarter). Excursions: Trogir 30 min away (UNESCO, fortified medieval town), Klis Fortress 20 min away (Game of Thrones, Meereen), Salona Roman ruins, Krka National Park 1h30 away (waterfalls, swimming allowed at Skradinski Buk), Dalmatian islands by ferry or catamaran (Hvar 1h, Brač 1h for Bol and Zlatni Rat, Vis 2h30 — the most traditional island, Blue Cave at Biševo, Mamma Mia 2 shot here). Try peka, pasticada, pršut, Plavac Mali wine. Direct flight from Paris in 2h45.
marseille:en: Marseille, France's oldest city (600 BC, Phocaean foundation): 870,000 inhabitants, sea, sun, mistral and a strong character. July is my favourite month (9.9/10): 20-28°C, 4% rain, sky of remarkable stability. August excellent (9.7/10) but Marseillais leaving on holiday, the city quieter. From December to February, expect 5-13°C and 20-25% rain: mild for France, frequent mistral (north-westerly wind that clears the sky but freezes the air). Highlights: Vieux-Port, Notre-Dame de la Garde (the Bonne Mère, 360° view), MUCEM (museum of Mediterranean civilisations, remarkable architecture), Le Panier (colourful old quarter), Calanques (national park, Sormiou and Morgiou by car, En Vau and Sugiton by kayak or hiking). Frioul islands and Château d'If by shuttle (Count of Monte Cristo). Try bouillabaisse at Chez Fonfon or Le Miramar (expensive), pizza at Chez Sauveur, navettes (orange-blossom-flavoured biscuits). Cassis and its calanques 30 min by train. May-June and September-October are the best tourism/weather compromises.
lyon:en: Lyon shows its best face from June to September. The Saône quays come alive until midnight, the Vieux-Lyon terraces overflow. July (9.2/10) and August (9.1) are my favourite months: 17-28°C, 14h of light, 21% rain in midsummer. Dry and hot, without Marseille's furnace. From December to February, the grey settles in, 5h of sun and temperatures clinging to the ground. Except on 8 December for the Festival of Lights, when the city transforms for 4 days. September stays a good late option: still mild temperatures (13-23°C), Beaujolais announcing its release, return-to-school less crowded than in tourist capitals. For bouchons and winter cuisine, November works too: moderate rain, quieter atmosphere.
bordeaux:en: Bordeaux has been through its renaissance since 2007 (renovated quays, tram, LGV putting Paris 2h away). UNESCO for the "sleeping beauty" turned trendy. July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 17-27°C, 25% rain, terraces on Place de la Bourse, length of the days. August excellent (9.0/10) but Bordelais leaving for the ocean. From December to February, expect 4-12°C and 36-45% rain: oceanic, often grey skies, but this is the season for oysters (from Arcachon Bay), Christmas markets and tasting the great vintages in the châteaux. Highlights: Place de la Bourse and its Mirror of Water (the world's largest), Cité du Vin (modern museum, panoramic tasting on the 8th floor), Grand Théâtre, Tour Pey-Berland, Saint-Pierre district (old Bordeaux), Darwin (trendy right bank). Wine route: Saint-Émilion (UNESCO, 40 min by train), Médoc (Pauillac, Margaux), Sauternes. Try canelés at Baillardran, entrecôte bordelaise, lamprey. Arcachon Bay and Dune du Pilat 1h away.
strasbourg:en: Strasbourg, capital of Alsace and seat of the European Parliament: 280,000 inhabitants, Franco-German cultural mix (annexed 4 times in 75 years between 1870 and 1945). July is my favourite month (8.7/10): 16-26°C, 38% rain, terraces in Petite-France, cruises on the Ill. August excellent (8.6/10). From November to March, expect 0-9°C and 36-43% rain or snow: continental cold, but this is the tourist apogee with the Christmas Market (Christkindelsmärik) from the last Friday in November to 24 December = the oldest in France (1570) and one of the largest in Europe. Hotel prices x3 during this period. Highlights: Notre-Dame Cathedral (142m, the world's tallest in the 15th century, astronomical clock, 332-step platform for the view), Petite-France (UNESCO, half-timbered houses on the canals), Palais Rohan, European quarter (Parliament, Council of Europe). Try choucroute (at Chez Yvonne or Maison Kammerzell), tarte flambée ("flammkueche"), bredele at the Christmas markets, Alsace wine (Riesling, Gewurztraminer). Alsace Wine Route 30 min away, Colmar 30 min by train.
montpellier:en: Montpellier, capital of Occitanie in southern France: 295,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 800,000 in the urban area. France's youngest city (45% of inhabitants under 30 thanks to the world's 2nd oldest medical university after Bologna, founded 1220 where Rabelais and Nostradamus taught). 12th French city in population, 7th in growth. Ideal Mediterranean climate, 10 km from the sea (Carnons, Palavas-les-Flots, Grande-Motte beaches). July is my favourite month (9.4/10): 20-31°C, 9% rain, ideal temperatures. June-August stay excellent (9.3/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 9-31°C. From November to March, expect 4-15°C and 28-37% rain: mild for France, little rain compared to the rest of the country. Highlights: Place de la Comédie ("L'Œuf" for Montpelliérains because of its oval shape, heart of the city, terraces, Comédie Opera 1888), Old town (Écusson, medieval maze of alleys between Comédie and Peyrou, 17th-18th century mansions, Mediterranean atmosphere), Place Royale du Peyrou (17th-century garden with equestrian statue of Louis XIV, 1768 neo-classical water tower), Saint-Clément Aqueduct (18th century, 14 km, still visible at Peyrou), Promenade des Arceaux, Saint-Pierre Cathedral (14th-century gothic with 2 unique massive cylindrical pillar-towers in France), Faculty of Medicine (one of the world's oldest, to visit in its Anatomy Conservatory = a unique world collection of 18th-19th century prepared anatomical specimens), Jardin des Plantes (France's oldest botanical garden 1593, model for the Paris Jardin des Plantes), Musée Fabre (one of France's most important regional art museums, Greuze Houdon Courbet Delacroix paintings), Place de la Canourgue ("Little Versailles of Montpellier", 17th-century mansions). Recent districts: Antigone (postmodern architecture 1970-2000 by Ricardo Bofill, disproportionate but successful classical scale), Port Marianne (recent 2010s with City Hall by François Fontès, signature buildings by Jean Nouvel "White Tree" 2019, Philippe Starck), Pierres Vives (Zaha Hadid 2012 = library + departmental archives). Excursions: Sète 30 min away ("Venice of Languedoc", canals, Thau lagoon, water jousting), Aigues-Mortes 30 min away (13th-century fortified Saint-Louis city), Camargue 1h away (pink flamingos, bulls, white horses), Pont du Gard 1h away (UNESCO), Carcassonne 2h away (UNESCO), Nîmes 30 min away, Mont Saint-Loup, Cévennes, Carnon Palavas Maguelone Espiguette beaches 10-30 min away. Try tielle sétoise (octopus pie), brandade de morue, petits pâtés de Pézenas, Languedoc wines, Picpoul de Pinet AOP, Saint-Chinian, Faugères. Montpellier-Méditerranée airport (MPL).
la-rochelle:en: La Rochelle, sub-prefecture of Charente-Maritime on the Atlantic coast: 75,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 200,000 in the urban area. Thousand-year-old port city, former major commerce port with the West Indies, Protestant stronghold in the 16th century (Siege of La Rochelle 1627-1628 by Richelieu, 23,000 deaths of which 2/3 from famine, royal victory). Capital of the Francofolies Festival (French-language song, since 1985). July is my favourite month (8.9/10): 17-23°C, 24% rain, ideal temperatures. August stays excellent (8.7/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 15-23°C, Atlantic swimming 19-21°C bracing, 2700 sunshine hours/year = one of France's sunniest cities above the Loire. From November to March, expect 5-13°C and 39-45% rain: mild climatically, little rain. Highlights: Old Port (historic heart, marina with 4500 berths, luxury moorings), 3 Towers of the Old Port (La Rochelle's visual signature protecting the harbour entrance since the 14th century: Tour Saint-Nicolas 14th century the tallest, Tour de la Chaîne = port entrance with closing chain, Tour de la Lanterne 15th century = former lighthouse-prison with English and French prisoners' graffiti), City Hall (gothic-Renaissance 15th-17th century, splendid inner courtyard), Saint-Louis Cathedral (classical 18th century), Old town arcades (characteristic arcaded pedestrian streets, legacy of the Rochelle merchants, atmosphere unique in France), La Rochelle Aquarium (one of Europe's largest private aquariums, 12,000 marine animals, opened 1988), Maritime Museum (3 preserved vessels including the weather France II, Manta frigate), New World Museum (history of La Rochelle-Americas relations, slavery, colonialism), Fine Arts Museum, Saint-Nicolas district (former sailors' quarter). Essential excursions: Île de Ré via 2.9 km bridge (10 preserved villages with characteristic white houses with green shutters, magnificent Atlantic beaches, bicycle the main mode of transport with 100 km of cycle paths), Île d'Oléron 1h away (the 2nd largest French metropolitan island after Corsica, oyster farming), Fort Boyard (visible from the sea, fort famous from the TV game show), Marais Poitevin 1h away ("Green Venice", 100,000 hectares of marshes drained 7th century, traditional flat-bottomed boat trips), Cognac 1h30 away (world capital of cognac, Hennessy/Martell/Rémy Martin/Courvoisier/Camus visits). Major festival: Francofolies mid-July (5 days of French-language song concerts, since 1985, Indochine, Patrick Bruel, Jean-Jacques Goldman, MC Solaar have played there). Try moules-frites (with Pineau des Charentes in marinière), Marennes-Oléron oysters IGP (the only French oysters grown in claires — refining basins), Pineau des Charentes IGP (local aperitif blending grape juice and cognac), La Rochelle tinned sardines, mojettes (Vendée white beans). La Rochelle-Île de Ré airport (LRH).
saint-sebastien:en: San Sebastián (Donostia in Basque), Spanish Basque Country: 190,000 inhabitants on the Bay of Biscay. The pearl of the Basque coast, world gastronomic capital by several rankings (the city with the most Michelin stars per inhabitant in the world: 16 stars including 3 three-star restaurants for 188,000 inhabitants). La Concha Beach regularly ranked among the world's most beautiful urban beaches. July-August are my favourite months (8.2/10): 17-24°C, 40% rain, mild temperatures (cool oceanic climate), seawater 18-20°C (chilly). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 15-24°C, unpredictable oceanic climate (it can be 12°C and rain in July). From November to March, expect 6-15°C and 49-58% rain: mild but very rainy (Basque Country = one of Spain's wettest regions, 1500 mm/year), many hotels stay open, pintxo season without crowds. Highlights: La Concha Beach (1.5 km in a regular crescent, bay sheltered by Santa Clara island, one of the world's most beautiful urban beaches by many rankings), Zurriola Beach (Gros side, surfers' beach), Mount Igueldo (181m, 1912 funicular, vintage amusement park at the top, signature panorama over the bay), Mount Urgull (historic centre, Mota castle 12th century, Christ statue), Old town Parte Vieja (heart of the pintxos, taberna after taberna where you snack standing up doing the rounds "chiquiteo"), Good Shepherd Cathedral (neo-gothic 19th century), Gros district (modern, surf, young), Miramar Palace (19th-century summer residence of the kings of Spain), Kursaal Center (Rafael Moneo 1999, cubic concert hall). San Sebastián International Film Festival in September (one of the major A-list European festivals, since 1953). Excursions: Bilbao 1h away (Guggenheim), Biarritz 30 min away (France), Hondarribia 30 min away (medieval border village), Pasaia and Getaria (Cristobal Balenciaga village, txakoli wines). Try the pintxos (Basque equivalent of tapas, but on bread slices, snacked while doing the bar rounds: Bar Néstor for the tortilla, La Cuchara de San Telmo, La Viña for the cheesecake), sea urchin tarts, chuleta (Basque rib steak), txakoli (slightly sparkling local DO white wine). Cult starred restaurants: Arzak, Akelaŕe, Martín Berasategui (3-star Michelin each). San Sebastián airport (EAS) or Bilbao (BIO) 1h away.
sofia:en: Sofia, Bulgarian capital: 1.3 million inhabitants at 550m altitude at the foot of Mount Vitosha (2290m, accessible by cable car for skiing in winter and hiking in summer). Unique architectural mix: Roman ruins, Ottoman mosque, Orthodox cathedral, communist buildings. July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 17-28°C, 19% rain, ideal temperatures thanks to the altitude. From June to September, it's the high season: 13-28°C, dry and hot continental climate. From December to March, expect -3/13°C and 25-36% rain: dry cold, skiing possible on Vitosha 30 min by bus. Highlights: Saint Alexander Nevsky Cathedral (Sofia's signature, neo-Byzantine 1912, golden dome, one of the world's largest Orthodox cathedrals, interior worth seeing), Saint Sofia Church (next door, the origin of the city's name, 4th century), Vitosha Boulevard (main pedestrian street), Saint George Rotunda (Sofia's oldest preserved church, 4th century, Roman), Banya Bashi Mosque (16th century, one of Europe's oldest mosques), Sofia Synagogue (Europe's 3rd largest Sephardic synagogue), National History Museum (at Boyana, Thracian treasures). Boyana Church (UNESCO, medieval Bulgarian frescoes 20 min away). Excursions: Rila Monastery 2h away (UNESCO, colourful frescoes, the national monument), Plovdiv 1h45 away (UNESCO old town). Try banitsa (cheese pastry), kebapche, shopska salata (national salad), rakia (fruit eau-de-vie), Bulgarian yoghurt (born here). Among Europe's cheapest capitals.
nantes:en: Nantes, historic capital of the Duchy of Brittany on the Loire in France: 320,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 670,000 in the urban area. Former Breton capital (1532 reunion with France), today the chief town of Loire-Atlantique department in Pays-de-la-Loire region = sensitive Breton identity issue (Nantes residents often campaign for Loire-Atlantique to return to historic Brittany region). Dynamic French cultural capital = birthplace of Jules Verne 1828, European green capital 2013, dynamic university with 60,000 students. July is my favourite month (8.9/10): 14-25°C, 19% rain, ideal temperatures. June-August stay excellent (8.7-8.8/10). From May to September, it's the tourist high season: 9-25°C. From November to March, expect 1-13°C and 28-44% rain: mild for Atlantic-oceanic France (rare frost thanks to Atlantic proximity), ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Castle of the Dukes of Brittany (13th-15th century, former residence of the Dukes of Brittany including Anne of Brittany born here in 1477 = duchess-queen, twice queen of France, houses the modern Nantes History Museum 2007 in 32 thematic rooms, signature tourist attraction), Saint-Pierre-Saint-Paul Cathedral (15th-19th century flamboyant gothic, one of France's longest cathedrals 103m, monumental tomb of François II last Duke of Brittany signed Michel Colombe 1502 = masterpiece of French Renaissance), Passage Pommeraye (1843 shopping arcade on 3 levels with monumental staircase signature of 19th-century Nantes, one of Europe's most beautiful shopping galleries, classified historic monument), Place Royale (18th-century neoclassical square with signature fountain), Place du Bouffay (preserved medieval atmosphere), Quai de la Fosse (former slave-trading port of Nantes 18th century = Nantes was the leading French slave-trading port with 450,000 slaves deported to the West Indies, abolition of slavery memorial 2012 on the quay), Memorial to the Abolition of Slavery (work 2012 by Krzysztof Wodiczko + Julian Bonder = moving underground memorial under the quay with 2000 glass plaques naming the 1710 Nantes slave-trading expeditions 18th-19th century), Les Machines de l'Île = unique signature tourist attraction worldwide (theme park-museum inspired by Jules Verne + Leonardo da Vinci, giant animal mechanical machines: Great Elephant 12m tall 8 tonnes rideable signature + Carrousel des Mondes Marins ocean-themed carousel + Galerie des Machines = signature Nantes experience, created 2007 by La Machine company), Jardin des Plantes (19th-century botanical park), Bouffay district (Nantes nightlife), Île de Versailles (Japanese garden on an islet in the Erdre). Excursions: Vannes and Carnac 1h30 away (Brittany, already covered by BDW), Saint-Malo 1h45 away (already covered), La Baule 1h away (signature beach resort of the French Atlantic coast), Pornic and Le Croisic (Jade coast), Pays de la Loire châteaux Angers 1h, Saumur 2h, Tours 2h30. Voyage à Nantes = annual summer contemporary art trail (July-August), green line traced on the ground links the works scattered through the city, signature Nantes tourist experience. Try beurre blanc nantais (signature sauce invented in Nantes 1890), pike with beurre blanc, Petit-Beurre LU (biscuit created in Nantes by Lefèvre-Utile 1886), Muscadet AOC (local signature white wine with Grenoble sole), curé nantais (cheese), Berlingot nantais (signature sweet), Folle Blanche Cognac. Nantes-Atlantique airport (NTE).
andorre:en: Andorra, micro-state in the Pyrenees between France and Spain, lives on ski tourism and duty-free. 2 large areas: Grandvalira (210 km of pistes, the largest in the Pyrenees) and Vallnord (90 km). Lift passes 30-40% cheaper than the Alps. July-August are my favourite months (8.9-9.0/10): 14-26°C, 27-29% rain, hiking in the Ordino and Sorteny valleys. From December to March, expect -2/10°C in the valley and -10/0°C at altitude: ski season. Family atmosphere, many French-speaking ski schools. The Caldea in Andorra la Vella (the largest thermal centre in southern Europe) after a day of skiing. From October to November and in May, it's the off-season. No airport: Toulouse, Barcelona and Lerida (Spain) 2-3h by road. Duty-free shopping (alcohol, perfumes, tobacco, electronics) draws as many visitors as the skiing. Population: 80,000 inhabitants, 8 million visitors/year.
austin:en: Austin, Texas state capital, is the "Live Music Capital of the World": 250 music venues, SXSW festival. October is my favourite month (8.7/10): 18-29°C, 27% rain, heat finally dropping after summer, ACL Festival (Austin City Limits) early October. From June to September, expect 23-36°C: brutal heat (often 38-40°C in July-August), Austinians living in air conditioning, going out after 21h. From December to February, expect 6-20°C: mild for Texas but short days, occasional rare snow. March-April is the tourist peak with SXSW (mid-March, 10 days, prices x4) and the bluebonnets in bloom (Texas state flower). Highlights: Texas State Capitol (the largest in the USA, 5m taller than Washington's), South Congress Avenue (boutiques, restaurants), Barton Springs (natural springs, 20°C year-round), Lady Bird Lake for kayaking/SUP. Bats of Congress Avenue Bridge at sunset (March-October, 1.5 million bats). Try brisket at Franklin BBQ (4h+ queue), breakfast tacos at Veracruz, ice cream at Amy's. Hill Country and vineyards 1h away.
denver:en: Denver, Colorado state capital: 700,000 inhabitants, nicknamed the "Mile-High City" because it sits at exactly 1609m altitude (line marked at the Capitol). Base for exploring the American Rockies. June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 13-29°C, 22% rain, ideal temperatures before summer heat. From September to October, expect 4-28°C: spectacular foliage (golden aspen poplars mid-September to mid-October, the most beautiful season of the American West). From December to March, expect -8/12°C and 12-27% precipitation often as snow: ski season (Denver is the air base for Aspen, Vail, Breckenridge, Keystone, Copper Mountain — all 1h30-3h30 by car). From April to May, expect -2/21°C: extreme weather variability (snow possible until May). Highlights: Capitol (the engraved Mile High Step), Larimer Square (Denver's first district), Union Station (rehabilitated 2014), Denver Art Museum (Daniel Libeskind architecture), Red Rocks Amphitheatre 30 min away (concert venue in red rocks, U2 filmed Under a Blood Red Sky here in 1983), Coors Brewery at Golden (the world's largest single-site brewery), 16th Street Mall. Excursions: Rocky Mountain National Park 1h30 away (Trail Ridge Road, the highest paved road in the USA, Estes Park base), Garden of the Gods 1h30 away (Colorado Springs), Pikes Peak 4302m. Try green chile (south-western cuisine), Rocky Mountain oysters (breaded bull testicles, local challenge), craft beers (Denver = US craft beer capital, 100+ breweries).
orlando:en: Orlando is the world capital of theme parks: Walt Disney World (4 parks), Universal Studios (3 parks), SeaWorld, Legoland. March-April are my favourite months (8.8/10): 16-29°C, 25-31% rain, ideal park temperatures before summer heat. From June to September, expect 23-31°C with 73-84% precipitation: heavy heat, daily late-afternoon storms (parks temporarily close, bring a poncho), hurricane risk. From December to February, expect 10-25°C: American tourist high season (school holidays, Christmas, Spring Break), hotel prices x2-x3. Walt Disney World = 4 parks (Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, Hollywood Studios, Animal Kingdom) over 110 km², plan 4-5 days minimum, tickets 100-200$/day, MyDisneyExperience app essential, paid Genie+ FastPass 15-25$. Universal Studios + Islands of Adventure = 2 parks (Wizarding World of Harry Potter, roller coasters), 1-2 days, tickets 100-180$/day. Outside the parks: Kennedy Space Center 1h away (launches visible from the city). Higher rates during Spring Break (March), Thanksgiving, Christmas, summer. September-October offer the best prices but riskier weather.
honolulu:en: Honolulu is almost-boring tropical stability: 20-29°C all year, never a really bad season. The real variable is the rain on the windward side: 22-39% depending on the month, but often as brief showers. June stays my favourite month (9.2/10): 23-28°C, only 24% rain, steady trade winds, sea at 25°C for Waikiki. April-May see the surfers migrating from the North Shore (waves done) to the South Shore (Diamond Head, Ala Moana). From October to March, rain rises to 24-35% with a few tropical showers and the big swell returning to the North Shore (Pipeline, Sunset Beach). American tourist peak in parallel and the high price season. Europeans often prefer May-September, more economical.
puerto-vallarta:en: Puerto Vallarta is the chic beach resort of the Mexican Pacific coast: Sierra Madre Occidental plunging into the sea, 250,000 inhabitants. April is my favourite month (9.5/10): 18-27°C, 0% rain, sea at 24°C, ideal temperatures before the hot summer. From November to May, it's the dry season: 0-8% rain, constant sky, American and Canadian period (Snowbirds). From June to October, it's the rainy/hurricane season: 43-85% rainfall, humid heat (24-31°C), moderate hurricane risk (Pacific side less exposed than Atlantic). Highlights: Malecón (1.5 km seaside promenade, sculptures, Los Arcos), Zona Romántica (old town, Playa Los Muertos), Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de Guadalupe (signature church with copper crown), Mercado Municipal. Beaches: Playa Los Muertos (centre, lively), Conchas Chinas (quieter), Yelapa (village accessible only by boat, 45 min, very quiet). Sayulita 45 min away (bohemian surfer village), Marietas Islands by boat (Playa Escondida, limited access 116 visitors/day), Sierra Madre for canopy/zipline. Try ceviche tostadas, birria de res, agua de jamaica. Significant LGBT community (Pride in May). Direct flight from Paris-Mexico-PVR.
mexico:en: Mexico City sits at 2240m altitude: the altitude is felt in the first days (short of breath, less deep sleep). Mild climate year-round despite the tropical latitude. March-April are my favourite months (9.2/10): 11-28°C, 15-23% rain, jacarandas in bloom everywhere in Roma and Condesa. From June to September, it's the rainy season (40-88% showers), almost every afternoon, but mornings often sunny. Pollution improves then. From October to February, it's the dry season: clear sky, but pollution that can sting the eyes on certain thermal-inversion days (check IMECA Index). Roma Norte and Condesa for the café and restaurant scene, Centro Histórico for the Zócalo and Diego Rivera murals. Teotihuacán 1h30 by bus. Coyoacán for Casa Azul (Frida Kahlo). Mexico has become a major digital nomad destination since 2020: excellent Wifi, dense coworkings, but rents soaring (+50% in 4 years).
oaxaca:en: Oaxaca is the most culturally dense Mexico: Zapotec zones, artisanal mezcal, pre-Columbian markets still alive. At 1500m altitude, the climate stays mild year-round. February is my favourite month (9.5/10): 11-28°C, 2% rain, clear sky for Monte Albán or Hierve el Agua. From June to September, it's the tropical rainy season (59-70% showers), often late afternoon but sometimes long. This is the greenest period, interesting for photography if you accept the uncertainty. Día de los Muertos late October-early November is the absolute tourist peak: hotel prices x3-x5, bookings 6 months ahead, but unique atmosphere worldwide (illuminated cemeteries, catrina parades). The Guelaguetza in July is another high point, despite the rain. November-May stay the stable dry season.
tulum:en: Tulum has experienced a tourist explosion since 2015: former fishing village turned Instagram and boho-luxe destination. March is my favourite month (8.7/10): 24-28°C, 34% rain, turquoise sea at 25°C. From May to October, it's the rainy/hurricane season: 31-74% rainfall, possible sargassum (washed-up brown seaweed). From December to February, expect 22-28°C: high season, boutique-luxury hotels at 300-1500$/night (Be Tulum, Azulik, Casa Malca). Highlights: Tulum Mayan ruins (13th-15th century, one of the rare seaside Mayan sites, leave early before heat and crowds), Paradise Beach (free, one of Mexico's most beautiful), Cenotes (Gran Cenote, Cenote Calavera, Cenote Crystal, Cenote Dos Ojos for diving), Sian Ka'an (UNESCO reserve of 5300 km², lagoons, mangroves). Cobá 45 min away (Mayan ruins in the jungle, bicycle recommended to cross the site). Hotel Zone: 10 km road between the town and the beach, bike rental advised (5-10$/day). Bohemian spots: Hartwood (cult restaurant, 6 months booking, 500$+ for 2), Nomade (yoga retreat). Try mezcal, ceviche, fish tacos. Safety: recent resurgence of cartel-related violence (2023-2024), to monitor. Tulum International airport opened in December 2023 (previously: Cancún 2h away).
playa-del-carmen:en: Playa del Carmen is the alternative beach resort to Cancun (1h to the south): human-scale town, European atmosphere, bohemian-chic, base for exploring the Riviera Maya. March is my favourite month (8.9/10): 24-28°C, 27% rain, sea at 25°C, end of the American high season. From June to October, it's hurricane season: 46-72% rain, hurricane risk, possible sargassum. From December to February, expect 22-27°C: European and American high season, hotel prices x2-x3. Highlights: 5th Avenue (Quinta Avenida) pedestrianised over 20 blocks (boutiques, restaurants, bars), Playa Mamitas (animation), Playa Punta Esmeralda (cenote in the sea, quieter). Excursions from Playa: Tulum (45 min by collectivo or ADO bus), Cenotes to explore (Gran Cenote, Cenote Azul, Dos Ojos, Cenote Calavera), Isla Cozumel by ferry 45 min from the port (snorkelling on the Mesoamerican reef, the world's 2nd largest after Australia), Sian Ka'an (UNESCO reserve), Xcaret. Akumal 30 min away to swim with turtles. Try tacos al pastor, ceviche, mezcal. Become very digital nomad: coworkings, café scene, significant French community. Tulum bookings 4-6 months ahead in high season.
cabo-san-lucas:en: Cabo San Lucas is the opposite of Loreto: massive American tourism, all-inclusive resorts, nightlife at Squid Roe and Cabo Wabo. El Arco, the signature rock formation, separates the Sea of Cortez and the Pacific. March-April are my favourite months (9.6/10): 18-29°C, 0% rain, sea at 21°C, humpback whale migration period (trips at 80€). From June to October, expect 24-33°C with the chubasco season and real hurricane risk (Hurricane Odile in 2014 left a mark). Many hotels apply substantially reduced summer prices. The American Spring Break in March packs the town for 3 weeks: hotel prices x2-x3, parties 24/7. To plan around if you want quiet. La Paz 2h by car offers another option, more local. Sport fishing (marlin, mahi-mahi) remains the most distinctive attraction.
manille:en: Manila, capital of the Philippines: 14 million inhabitants in the urban area (Metro Manila), chaotic Asian megalopolis blending Spanish (1565-1898), American (1898-1946) and Asian heritage. Probably one of Asia's most polluted and traffic-jammed capitals. January is my favourite month (8.3/10): 23-29°C, 35% rain, end of the dry season. From December to February, it's the tourist high season: 23-30°C, relatively dry climate. From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 24-34°C, oppressive humidity (often 90%+). From June to November, it's the active typhoon season (the Philippines averages 20 typhoons per season): 53-92% rainfall, regular urban flooding. Highlights: Intramuros (16th-century fortified Spanish old town, largely destroyed in WWII, partially rebuilt: Fort Santiago with the cell where José Rizal was imprisoned before his execution in 1896, Cathedral, San Agustín Church UNESCO the oldest church in the Philippines 1607), Rizal Park, National Museum (Spoliarium by Juan Luna), Manila Cathedral, Binondo district (the world's oldest Chinatown, founded 1594), Makati and BGC (Bonifacio Global City) = modern business districts (skyscrapers, malls, restaurants), Quezon City (university). Safety: Manila demands vigilance (snatch thefts, scams), tourist zones monitored but the hinterland risky for Europeans (kidnappings notably in southern Mindanao). Excursions: Tagaytay 2h away (Taal volcano on a lakeside), Pampanga (culinary capital). Manila = transit base for the islands: Cebu (1h flight, beaches), Palawan (Coron, El Nido = among the world's most beautiful turquoise waters), Bohol (Chocolate Hills, tarsiers). Try adobo, sinigang, lechon (roasted suckling pig), halo-halo (iced dessert). Direct flight Paris-Manila in 14h via Doha or Dubai.
goa:en: Goa, former Portuguese colony (1510-1961): India's smallest state (3700 km²), 2 million inhabitants, beaches, Portuguese colonial heritage, fusion cuisine, persistent hippie atmosphere. From November to February (6.9/10): 20-32°C, 0-17% rain, sea at 28°C, this is the tourist high season. Goa never tops 7/10 (climate too hot-humid to reach excellent). From June to September, it's the monsoon: 87-99% rainfall, deserted beaches, many hotels and restaurants close, but also the greenest landscapes and rivers in spate. From October to May, stable dry climate with high season December-January (Europeans wintering), hotel prices x3-x4. North Goa vs South Goa: North (Anjuna, Vagator, Baga, Calangute, Arambol) more festive, Anjuna hippie flea market on Wednesdays, backpacker atmosphere; South (Palolem, Agonda, Patnem) quieter, wild beaches, yoga retreats. Highlights: Old Goa (UNESCO, Basilica of Bom Jesus with relics of Saint Francis Xavier, Sé Cathedral), Panaji/Panjim (capital, Latin Fontainhas quarter), Dudhsagar Falls (310m waterfall, 4×4 + train tour), Spice Plantations. Try vindaloo, xacuti, fish curry rice (local national dish), pão (Portuguese bread), feni (cashew alcohol). Direct flight from Paris to Goa-Mopa (new airport, opened 2023).
jaipur:en: Jaipur, capital of Rajasthan, is the "Pink City": 3 million inhabitants, fortified old town painted pink in 1876 for the Prince of Wales's visit. A major stop on the Golden Triangle Delhi-Agra-Jaipur. February-March are my favourite months (8.9/10): 13-31°C, 3-9% rain, ideal temperatures, golden light on the walls. From November to February, expect 9-27°C: tourist high season, clear sky. From April to June, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 23-40°C (sometimes 45°C), impossible to visit during the day. From July to September, it's the monsoon: 41-69% rainfall, but more bearable temperatures (24-33°C). Highlights: City Palace (royal palace still inhabited, museum), Hawa Mahal ("palace of the winds", 953 pink-stone openings, signature photo), Jantar Mantar (UNESCO, 18th-century astronomical observatory with giant instruments), Amber Fort 11 km away (16th-century hill fortress, climb on elephant-back or by jeep, mirror palace), Nahargarh Fort (panoramic view over the city at sunset), Jal Mahal (lake palace). Bazaars: Johari Bazaar (jewellery), Bapu Bazaar (textiles), semi-precious stones everywhere. Try dal baati churma, laal maas (spicy goat curry), kachori. October-November and March are the best crowd/weather compromises.
fes:en: Fes (Fez), Morocco's spiritual capital: 1.1 million inhabitants, 2 UNESCO medinas (Fes el-Bali founded in 789 and Fes el-Jdid). The oldest continuously inhabited medieval city in the world, a maze of 9000 alleys. May is my favourite month (9.4/10): 14-28°C, 16% rain, ideal temperatures. From July to August drop to 3.9/10 due to the heatwave (20-38°C, sometimes 45°C). From December to February, expect 6-19°C: mild but cool in the evening, riads not heated. Highlights: Bou Inania Madrasa (14th century, Marinid, one of the most beautiful in North Africa), Quaraouiyine University (859, the oldest still-active university in the world according to UNESCO), Chouara Tannery (UNESCO, panorama of the dyeing vats from the leather sellers' terraces, sprig of mint to mask the smell), Place Seffarine (coppersmiths' square), Bab Boujeloud (blue main entrance gate to the medina), Royal Palace (façade with 7 doors). Potters' and tanners' quarter, Jewish quarter (Mellah), Marinid tombs (panoramic view over the medina at sunset). Logistics: getting lost in the medina is inevitable, local guide recommended for the first time (200-400 MAD/day). Volubilis (UNESCO, Roman ruins) 1h away, Meknes (UNESCO, imperial capital of Moulay Ismail) 1h away, Chefchaouen 4h away. Try pigeon pastilla, harira, mechoui, msemen. Direct flight Paris-Fes in 3h.
casablanca:en: Casablanca is more a city to live in than to visit: Morocco's economic capital, few monuments apart from the Hassan II Mosque (the world's 3rd largest, open to non-Muslims). August is my favourite month (9.6/10): 20-27°C, 1% rain, Atlantic breezes that cool things down (Marrakech 240 km away is at 38°C at the same time). The Aïn Diab Corniche concentrates the nightlife. From November to March, expect 9-21°C and 20-31% rain: this is the Atlantic rainy season, often heavy but short. The Habous quarter (recent medina) remains the most accessible to visitors, unlike the rougher old medina. May-June and September-October are gentle transitions, ideal for the Maarif terraces. The Casa-Voyageurs station and Mohammed V airport make it primarily a transit base for Marrakech, Fes or Essaouira (high-speed train to Tangier in 2h).
tanger:en: Tangier is Morocco's Mediterranean gateway: ferry from Tarifa in 1h, ferry to Algeciras, high-speed rail link with Casablanca and Rabat. July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 20-29°C, 1% rain, Atlantic breezes. August curiously drops to 6.9/10 in our data despite 21-30°C: European tourist peak, city overflowing (Moroccans abroad return en masse), inflated rates, packed beaches. June and September are the best months for swimming in calm. From December to March, expect 10-18°C and 25-38% rain: damp and windy, but the kasbah and the medina take on their most local face. The Café Hafa (Bowles, Burroughs, Stones) keeps its bohemian charm. The Caves of Hercules and Cap Spartel can be visited as a half-day trip. Asilah 45 km to the south (white medina painted by artists) complements it well.
tunis:en: Tunis, capital of Tunisia: 1 million inhabitants (3 million in the urban area) on the Gulf of Tunis (Mediterranean), close to the archaeological site of Carthage. Cradle of the Jasmine Revolution 2011 (starting point of the Arab Spring). May is my favourite month (9.0/10): 15-26°C, 21% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer heatwave. From April to June and September-October, these are the best seasons: 12-30°C, 18-25% rain. From July-August, it's the heatwave: 22-35°C (sometimes 45°C during sirocco wind from the Sahara), prices halved (Tunisians on holiday at the coast more than in Tunis). From November to March, expect 8-22°C and 18-27% rain: mild for the Maghreb. Highlights in Tunis: Medina of Tunis (UNESCO 1979, one of the most traditional medinas in North Africa, 100 hectares with 700 monuments, covered maze of souks by trade), Zitouna Mosque ("Mosque of the Olive Tree", 8th century, one of North Africa's oldest), Souk el Attarine (perfumers), Souk des Chechias (traditional red hats), Avenue Habib Bourguiba ("the Champs-Élysées of Tunis", French colonial heritage), Saint Vincent de Paul Cathedral (1897, neo-Byzantine), Bardo Museum (4 km away, one of the world's most important Roman mosaic collections, 2015 terrorist attack heavily reduced tourist numbers). Excursions: Carthage 20 min by TGM train (UNESCO, ruins of the Phoenician and Roman city, baths of Antoninus, theatre, best with a guide to understand), Sidi Bou Saïd 25 min by train (signature white-and-blue village, Café des Nattes, panorama over the Gulf of Tunis), La Marsa (chic suburb), Dougga (UNESCO, 2h away, among the best-preserved Roman ruins in North Africa), Kairouan 2h away (UNESCO, Islam's 4th holy city, the Maghreb's largest mosque from the 8th century). Try brik (filo pastry with egg-tuna-capers), couscous, ojja, harissa (Tunisian signature), makroudh, mint tea.
djerba:en: Djerba, Tunisian island in the Gulf of Gabès (south-east): 165,000 inhabitants over 514 km², legendary island of the "land of the Lotus-eaters" mentioned in Homer's Odyssey (Odysseus reportedly stopped here, his sailors having tasted the lotus forgot their mission). Old Sephardic Jewish community (1st Ghriba synagogue mentioned 6th century BC, one of the world's oldest still-active synagogues). May is my favourite month (9.3/10): 18-26°C, 7% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From April to June and September-October, these are the best seasons: 13-28°C, 7-11% rain, ideal dry climate. From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (24-33°C sometimes 40°C+), and this is the tourist high season. From November to March, expect 10-22°C: mild, European snowbirds wintering (Djerba receives many Belgians, Germans, Italians in winter). Highlights: Houmt Souk ("neighbourhood market", capital and port of the island, picturesque medina, Bordj el-Kebir fort 16th century), Ghriba Synagogue (at Erriadh, legend has it that its founding stone comes from Solomon's destroyed temple in Jerusalem; pilgrimage of Lag Ba'omer 33 days after Pesach by the Sephardic Tunisian Jewish diaspora), Djerbahood (at Erriadh, 2014 street art project = 250 international murals on the white walls of the Berber village, become a major tourist attraction), Traditional Heritage Museum (Guellala), Lalla Hadria Museum (Islamic art collections). Beaches: Sidi Mahres Beach (northern tourist zone with hotels), Pink Flamingos Beach, Seguia Beach (wilder). Activities: camel ride on the beach at sunset, thalassotherapy (Djerba has a major balneo-thermal industry), pink flamingos island. Try Djerban fish couscous, brik, ojja, harissa, Djerban goat cheese.
essaouira:en: Essaouira, the opposite of Marrakech: Atlantic instead of arid, wind instead of heat, calmed medina instead of packed souks. July-August are my favourite months (9.4/10): 19-25°C, 0% rain, period when Marrakech 130 km inland is hitting 38°C. The Gnaoua festival mid-June transforms the city for 4 days (Sufi music, free jam sessions). The trade wind (taros) blows almost continuously from March to September: 25-40 km/h on average, which makes it a global kitesurf and windsurf spot. For classic beach use, that's a downside. From December to March, expect 11-21°C and 9-21% rain. Always wearable but changeable sky. The UNESCO medina can be seen on foot in 2h, the Mogador ramparts (where Welles shot Othello) and the fishing harbour remain the classics.
agadir:en: Agadir bets everything on its weather stability: 11 months out of 12 above 8/10, ocean regulated by the Canaries current. June-July are my favourite months (9.5/10): 17-26°C, 0% rain, 6 km well-oriented beach. September keeps the swimming warm (sea at 20°C) with sun less aggressive than in midsummer. From December to February, you drop back to 11-21°C with a few showers. Always wearable but the swimming season is over. This is the most economical angle: hotel rates halved or thirded vs July. 25 km to the north, Taghazout draws surfers all year thanks to the steady Atlantic swells. Don't look for a medina here: Agadir was rebuilt after the 1960 earthquake.
athenes:en: Athens has one of Europe's most extreme Mediterranean climates. September is my favourite month (9.2/10): 20-29°C, 10% rain, sea still at 24°C at the Apollo Coast beaches (45 min by tram), Acropolis without the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9-8.6/10 due to the heatwave (23-34°C, sometimes 42°C): Athenians fleeing to the islands, Acropolis scorching from 11h to 17h. Visit before 9h or after 18h. From December to February, expect 6-15°C and 25-29% rain: mild, ideal for visiting the museums (Acropolis Museum, National Archaeological Museum) without crowds. Plaka district stays touristy but charming, Psyrri for alternative nightlife, Anafiotika for Cycladic calm in the city centre. Try a souvlaki at Kostas (takeaway, 5€). May and October are the best crowd/weather compromises. The Piraeus port for ferries to the islands (1-12h depending on destination).
riga:en: Riga, Latvian capital: 615,000 inhabitants on the Baltic, the largest Baltic-state city. UNESCO medieval old town + 800 Art Nouveau buildings (jugendstil, the world's largest concentration, notably along Alberta Street). June is my favourite month (8.4/10): 12-22°C, 41% rain, 17h days in June (almost a white night at the solstice). From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 14-22°C. From November to March, expect -3/6°C and 35-48% precipitation often as snow: Christmas market on Doma laukums (the public Christmas tree was invented in Riga in 1510, Latvians defend the origin against claims from Tallinn), days down to 7h of light end of December. Highlights: Old town (UNESCO, Doma laukums cathedral square, Blackheads House medieval merchant guild rebuilt after WWII destruction), Riga Lutheran Cathedral (13th century, one of the largest in the Baltic states), Saint Peter's Church (123m, lift to the view), Swedish Bastion (signature yellow medieval gate), Three Brothers (3 adjacent medieval houses, the oldest in Riga), Art Nouveau quarter (Alberta Street = the most beautiful, buildings by Mikhail Eisenstein, father of the filmmaker), Riga Central Market (5 former WWI zeppelin hangars, one of Europe's largest markets), Freedom Monument (1935, guarded 24/7). Maskavas district (former Jewish/Russian quarter, more local). Excursions: Jūrmala 25 min by train (Baltic seaside resort, long beach), Sigulda 1h away ("Switzerland of Latvia", Gauja Valley, Turaida Castle). Try Riga sprats (smoked sardines in a tin), pīrāgi (savoury pastry), Black Balsam (45° bitter liqueur made from 24 plants, Latvian signature).
vilnius:en: Vilnius, Lithuanian capital: 590,000 inhabitants, UNESCO baroque old town (the largest in central and eastern Europe, 360 hectares). Fervently Catholic (95% Lithuanian Catholics, contrast with the Lutheran Estonian and Latvian neighbours). June is my favourite month (8.4/10): 13-23°C, 40% rain, long days, terraces along the Vilnia. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 14-23°C. From November to March, expect -4/6°C and 31-49% precipitation often as snow: Christmas market on Cathedral Square considered among Europe's most charming, more continental than Riga or Tallinn. Highlights: Old town (UNESCO, exceptional baroque maze): Cathedral and Cathedral Square (Katedros aikštė, white neoclassical, separate bell tower), Gediminas Tower (on the hill, foundation of Vilnius in 1323, panorama, 2-min funicular), Saint Anne's Church (flamboyant gothic in red brick, Napoleon wanted to take it back to Paris in the palm of his hand), Saints Peter and Paul Church (the most exuberant baroque interior in the Baltic states, 2000 stucco sculptures), Vilnius University (1579, one of Central Europe's oldest, complex of inner courtyards), Užupis quarter ("self-proclaimed republic" independent since 1 April 1997, constitution displayed in several languages, bohemian Montmartre-like atmosphere), Gates of Dawn (Aušros vartai, the only preserved gate of the medieval walls, miraculous icon of the Virgin Mary). Holocaust history: preserved Vilnius ghetto ("Jerusalem of the North" before 1939, 60,000 Jews murdered). Excursions: Trakai 30 min away (red-brick island castle on a lake), Kernavė 1h away (UNESCO medieval capital). Try cepelinai (stuffed potato dumplings), kibinai (savoury pastry), Lithuanian beer (Švyturys).
ljubljana:en: Ljubljana, Slovenian capital: 290,000 inhabitants, one of Europe's smallest and most charming capitals ("the green one" — European Green Capital 2016, car-free pedestrian centre since 2008). July is my favourite month (9.0/10): 16-28°C, 35% rain, terraces along the Ljubljanica, festivals (Ljubljana Summer Festival July-August). From June to September, it's the high season: 12-28°C, mild continental climate. From December to March, expect -4/12°C and 29-39% rain/snow: dry cold, skiing at Krvavec or Vogel 1h away, charming Christmas market (Festive December) by the river. Highlights: Old town (on foot, around Mestni trg main square), Triple Bridge (Tromostovje, signature by Plečnik 1932, 3 bridges fanning out at the heart of the city), Dragon Bridge (Zmajski most, 4 guardian dragons, signature of Ljubljana), Ljubljana Castle (on the hill, accessible by funicular or 15 min walk, panoramic view of the Alps), Central Market (Plečnik, neoclassical riverside), Metelkova (alternative artistic squat, former military barracks, underground nightlife). Excursions: Lake Bled 50 min away ("the Slovenian postcard image", island church, perched castle), Lake Bohinj 1h30 away (wilder, Triglav National Park), Postojna Caves 50 min away (24 km of galleries, underground train, the most visited in Europe), Predjama Castle (13th-century castle embedded in a cliff). Adriatic coast 1h30 away (Piran medieval Venetian town). Try potica (rolled cake), idrijski žlikrofi (ravioli), kranjska klobasa (sausage), Refošk wine.
belgrade:en: Belgrade, Serbian capital: 1.2 million inhabitants at the confluence of the Danube and Sava, a city rebuilt 44 times according to legend (strategic crossroads between East and West). World-class nightlife (splavovi = barge-clubs on the Sava), bohemian post-Yugoslav atmosphere. August is my favourite month (9.1/10): 19-30°C, 21% rain, summer festivals (Belgrade Beer Fest, EXIT in nearby Novi Sad). June-July stay excellent (8.9-9.0/10). From June to September, it's the high season: 15-30°C, dry continental climate. From November to March, expect -2/12°C and 31-35% rain: dry cold, but Belgrade is known for partying year-round (kafanas with gypsy music). Highlights: Kalemegdan Fortress (concrete-bound ruins, public park at the Danube-Sava confluence, panoramic view over New Belgrade and the sunset), Knez Mihailova (main pedestrian street), Bohemian Skadarlija quarter (kafanas and traditional restaurants with gypsy orchestra), Saint Sava Cathedral (one of the world's largest Orthodox churches, mosaics being completed since 1985), House of Flowers (mausoleum of Tito, leader of communist Yugoslavia 1953-1980), Beton Hala (Sava-side, trendy restaurants). Nightlife: splavovi (floating barge-clubs on the Sava and Danube, Freestyler/20-44/Lasta, atmosphere until 5am, affordable prices), bars on Cetinjska (former brewery converted into a party district). Excursions: Novi Sad 1h away (cultural city, Petrovaradin Fortress, EXIT Festival early July), Subotica 2h30 away (Hungarian Art Nouveau). Try ćevapi (sausages), pljeskavica (minced steak), karadjordjeva šnicla, rakija šljivovica (plum eau-de-vie).
sarajevo:en: Sarajevo, capital of Bosnia and Herzegovina: 275,000 inhabitants in a steep Dinaric valley. Unique cultural crossroads in Europe: 4 religions coexist within a 200m radius (Gazi Husrev-bey mosque, Catholic cathedral, Orthodox cathedral, Ashkenazi synagogue). Site of the Sarajevo assassination (1914, trigger of WWI) and of the terrible siege of 1992-1996 (1425 days, 11,541 deaths). July-August are my favourite months (8.8-8.9/10): 16-27°C, 32-37% rain, terraces in the Baščaršija. From June to September, it's the high season: 12-27°C, continental climate moderated by altitude (550m). From December to March, expect -4/10°C and 38-44% rain/snow: winter cold, skiing on the slopes of Mount Bjelašnica and Jahorina (1984 Olympics venues). Highlights: Baščaršija (old Ottoman bazaar, maze of alleys, Gazi Husrev-Bey mosque, Sebilj fountain), Latin Bridge and Sarajevo Museum 1878-1918 (exact site of the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand on 28 June 1914), Tunnel of Hope (800m tunnel under the airport, the only link with the outside during the siege, harrowing museum), Cemeteries on the hills (impressive number of graves 1992-1996), Mount Trebević (cable car rebuilt 2018, panoramic view, abandoned 1984 Olympic bobsleigh track turned urban graffiti spot), Sniper Alley (the main avenue Bulevar Meše Selimovića where Serbian snipers shot at civilians). Try ćevapi (at Ćevabdžinica Željo, the most famous), burek, baklava, Bosnian coffee (served Turkish-style). Sarajevo remains one of Europe's cheapest destinations.
tirana:en: Tirana, Albanian capital: 600,000 inhabitants, transformed since the end of Enver Hoxha's isolationist communism (1944-1985: Albania was Europe's most closed country, more than today's North Korea, 173,000 paranoid bunkers built). June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 18-28°C, 22% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (20-32°C), but the coast opens (Saranda 4h away, the little-known but beautiful Albanian Riviera). From April to June and September-October are the best compromises. From December to February, expect 3-13°C and 35-39% rain: mild for the Balkans. Highlights: Skanderbeg Square (Sheshi Skënderbej, main square renovated 2017 without cars, statue of the national hero), Et'hem Bey Mosque (18th century, frescoes rare for a mosque), Clock Tower (19th century), Pyramid of Tirana (former Enver Hoxha museum 1988, designed by the dictator's daughter, abandoned then rehabilitated 2023 as a youth/cultural centre), Bunk'Art 1 and Bunk'Art 2 (former Hoxha anti-atomic bunkers turned into museums on communism and persecution, very impressive), House of Leaves (former secret services HQ, surveillance museum), Mount Dajti (15-min cable car, panoramic view over the city, restaurants at altitude). Blloku district (formerly reserved for the communist nomenklatura, now a hipster zone, restaurants, nightlife). Excursions: Krujë 30 min away (Skanderbeg fortress, views), Berat 2h away (UNESCO, "city of a thousand windows"), Albanian Riviera coast 4-5h away (Saranda, Ksamil, Himara — cheap alternative to Croatia). Try tavë kosi, byrek, raki. Direct flight Paris-Tirana in 2h45, among Europe's cheapest capitals.
skopje:en: Skopje, capital of North Macedonia: 540,000 inhabitants, birthplace of Mother Teresa. Rebuilt after the devastating earthquake of 1963 (1100 deaths) then transformed by the megalomaniac "Skopje 2014" project of the Gruevski government (kitsch monumental statues, neoclassical façades made of polystyrene glued onto brutalist buildings). City fascinating for its urban chaos. June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 17-30°C, 21% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August stay good (8.9-9.0/10) despite the heat (19-32°C). From June to September, it's the high season: 16-32°C, dry continental climate. From December to March, expect -2/14°C: dry cold. Highlights: Macedonia Square (Plostad Makedonija, giant 22m statue of Alexander the Great "Warrior on a Horse", historical controversy with Greece), Stone Bridge (15th-century Ottoman, linking the Macedonian square to the old town), Old Bazaar (Stara Čaršija) (one of the largest Ottoman bazaars in the Balkans, maze of cobbled alleys, 15th-17th century mosques), Kale Fortress (Byzantine ruins on a hill, panoramic view), Mustafa Pasha Mosque, Mother Teresa Memorial House (on the exact site of her birth in 1910, baptised the next day, left Skopje aged 18), Mount Vodno (cable car to the Millennium Cross 66m erected in 2002, panoramic view). Excursions: Matka Canyon 30 min away (canyon, monastery, kayaking, caves), Lake Ohrid 3h away (UNESCO, one of the world's oldest lakes at 4 million years). Try tavče gravče (beans), ajvar (red pepper purée), kebapi, rakija (eau-de-vie). Among Europe's cheapest capitals.
zagreb:en: Zagreb, Croatian capital: 800,000 inhabitants, Austro-Hungarian city far from Dalmatian seaside tourism (often just a stopover before Plitvice or Dubrovnik). And yet one of Central Europe's most charming and cheapest capitals. July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 18-28°C, 29% rain, terraces, festivals (INmusic Festival late June, Zagreb Summer Evenings). August stays excellent (9.0/10). From June to September, it's the high season: 14-28°C, 29-49% rain. From December to March, expect -2/13°C and 28-32% rain: dry continental cold, Advent in Zagreb (Christmas market voted best in Europe 3 years in a row 2016-2018, November-January, ice rink on King Tomislav Square). Highlights: Old town Gornji Grad (Upper Town, accessible by the world's shortest funicular at 66m), Cathedral (neo-gothic, restoration ongoing after the 2020 earthquake), Saint Mark's Church (coloured-tile roof with the coats of arms of Zagreb-Croatia-Slavonia-Dalmatia), Ban Jelačić Square (main square), Dolac Market (colourful central market, signature red parasols), Museum of Broken Relationships (collection of objects linked to romantic break-ups, original and moving), Mirogoj (monumental cemetery, one of Europe's most beautiful), Grič Tunnel (WWII air-raid shelter turned into an underground passage). Excursions: Plitvice 2h away, Samobor 30 min away (kremšnita, traditional pastry). Try štrukli (cheese pastry), grah (beans), pálinka, continental wines (Graševina). Zagreb 2h from Vienna, 4h from Budapest and Munich.
bucarest:en: Bucharest, Romanian capital: 1.8 million inhabitants, nicknamed the "Little Paris of the Balkans" (19th-century French Belle Époque architecture). Marked by 50 years of communism (Ceaușescu) then a sometimes chaotic democratic transition. July-August are my favourite months (9.1/10): 19-31°C, 17-26% rain, terraces in the Lipscani old town. September stays excellent (9.1/10) with 26°C on average. From June to September, it's the high season: 15-31°C, 14-38% rain. From November to March, expect -3/11°C and 22-31% rain: dry continental cold, Christmas markets on Piața Constituției and Piața Universității. Highlights: Palatul Parlamentului (House of the People, the world's largest civilian building after the Pentagon, 365,000 m², built by Ceaușescu 1984-1989, 3500 people still work there, 50-min guided tour, 80 lei), Lipscani historic centre (pedestrian streets, restaurants, bars, nightlife), Romanian Athenaeum (1888 concert hall, neoclassical), Biserica Stavropoleos (small 18th-century church right in the centre), Village Museum (open-air, traditional Romanian houses from every region), Curtea Veche (Old Court, ruins of Vlad Țepeș aka Dracula), Cișmigiu Park (central park). Excursions: Sinaia and Peleș Castle 2h away (summer residence of the Romanian kings), Brașov and Bran Castle 3h away ("Dracula's castle", very touristy), Sibiu 4h away (UNESCO Saxon city). Try mititei (sausages), sarmale (stuffed cabbage), mămăligă (polenta), țuică (plum eau-de-vie), Romanian wines (Murfatlar, Cotnari).
hoi-an:en: Hoi An is the UNESCO former merchant trading post of central Vietnam: yellow Japanese and Chinese houses, colourful lanterns, beach 5 km away. February is my favourite month (7.9/10): 20-25°C, 49% rain, end of the dry season before the heat. From March to May, expect 22-32°C and 25-44% rain: mild, ideal for An Bang beach. From September to December, it's the rainy/typhoon season: 65-85% rainfall, flooding possible in the old town (the Thu Bon river overflows, the city becomes navigable by sampan, photogenic but disruptive). From April to August, often clear sky but heat (26-34°C). Highlights: Japanese Covered Bridge, Tan Ky House, Cantonese Assembly Hall, Central Market. Tailors everywhere (made-to-measure in 24-48h, suit 80-150$, variable quality). Cooking classes very popular (at Madame Vy, Red Bridge). My Son 1h away (Hindu Cham ruins). An Bang beach or Cua Dai (severe erosion since 2010). Try cao lau (specialty), white rose (dumplings), banh mi Phuong (the best in Vietnam according to Bourdain).
da-nang:en: Da Nang, Vietnam's 4th city: 1.1 million inhabitants on the central coast, tourist base between Hué (1h30 to the north) and Hoi An (45 min to the south). My Khe beach 30 km along the city (made famous by American soldiers during the war 1965-1973 = "China Beach"). February is my favourite month (7.9/10): 20-24°C, 49% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer heat. From January to March, it's the dry season: 20-27°C, 33-65% rain, the most pleasant period. From April to August, expect 25-34°C: rising heat, peak in July (27-33°C), this is the tourist high season for swimming (sea at 27-29°C). From September to November, it's typhoon season: 74-86% rainfall, real hurricane risk on Vietnam's central coast. Highlights: Dragon Bridge (Cầu Rồng, opened 2013, 666m long, golden dragon that breathes fire and water on Saturday/Sunday evenings at 21h), Marble Mountains (Ngũ Hành Sơn, 5 marble hills with cave-Buddhist temples, Da Nang was the capital of the Champa empire 2nd-15th century), Bà Nà Hills 30 min away (the world's longest cable car at 5.8 km to a reconstructed French village + the famous Golden Bridge held up by 2 giant hands opened 2018, viral Instagram photo), Da Nang Cathedral ("Pink Cock Cathedral"), Cham Museum (the world's largest collection of Cham art). Major excursions: Hoi An 45 min away (UNESCO, ancient port town with silk lanterns, made-to-measure tailors), Hué 1h30 away (UNESCO, former imperial Nguyen capital 1802-1945), My Son (UNESCO, Hindu Cham ruins 4th-13th century). Try mì quảng (regional noodles), bún chả cá (fish soup), bánh tráng cuốn thịt heo (rolls). Da Nang receives direct flights from several Asian cities.
penang:en: Penang (Pulau Pinang), island in north-western Malaysia in the Andaman Sea: 1.8 million inhabitants for the province (island + Seberang Perai mainland strip), Malaysia's culinary capital by many rankings ("food paradise of Asia" according to Lonely Planet and CNN Travel). Capital Georgetown = UNESCO 2008 for its preserved British colonial, straits-born Chinese and Indian mixed architecture. From January to February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 24-31°C, 55-62% rain, this is the relatively dry season. Penang never tops 6.9/10 (humid equatorial tropical climate year-round). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 24-31°C. From April to October, it's the wetter season: 66-97% rainfall. Georgetown highlights: Street Art (Georgetown is famous for its murals since 2012 when Lithuanian artist Ernest Zacharevic created several works that went viral "Children on Bicycle", "Boy on Motorbike", "Girl on Swing" — culture trail signposted on Armenian Street, Cannon Street), Cheong Fatt Tze Mansion ("Blue Mansion", 1880 mansion of Chinese magnate "the Rockefeller of the East", unique Sino-colonial architecture, restored 1990, hotel + guided tours), Pinang Peranakan Mansion (Peranakan mansion = straits-born community of mixed Chinese-Malay origin 19th-20th century), Khoo Kongsi (19th-century clan-temple, one of the most impressive outside China), Fort Cornwallis (18th-century British fortlet, founded by Francis Light who colonised Penang for the British Crown in 1786), Eastern & Oriental Hotel ("E&O Hotel", 1885 colonial hotel where Somerset Maugham and Rudyard Kipling stayed, view over the Andaman Sea), Kek Lok Si Temple (Malaysia's largest Buddhist temple, 36m Kuan Yin statue, lit up for Chinese New Year), Penang Hill (Bukit Bendera, accessible by Asia's steepest funicular at 27°, panorama Georgetown + strait + Sumatra on a clear day). Penang cuisine = the main reason to come to Penang: char kway teow (flat noodles wok-fried, signature dish, Sister Char Kway Teow institution since 1976), Penang laksa (assam laksa with tamarind, sardine-based, the spiciest of Malaysian laksas), Hokkien mee (prawn noodle soup), cendol (iced dessert), nasi kandar (Indo-Malay rice with curries), roti canai (Indian flatbread), Penang rojak (fruit salad). Hawker centres = the queen format: Gurney Drive, New Lane, Chulia Street, Lebuh Kimberley. Excursions: Langkawi 2h by ferry, Kuala Lumpur 4h by bus. Penang International airport (PEN).
langkawi:en: Langkawi, archipelago of 99 tropical islands in north-western Malaysia in the Andaman Sea: 95,000 inhabitants on the main island (Pulau Langkawi). Duty-free status since 1987 (alcohol, cigarettes, perfumes, electronics = very cheap compared to the rest of Malaysia). UNESCO Global Geopark since 2007. From January to March are my favourite months (6.9/10): 24-32°C, 31-49% rain, this is the ideal dry season. Langkawi never tops 6.9/10 (hot tropical climate, significant monsoon). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 24-32°C, sea at 28°C, hotel prices x2-x3 (Singaporeans on weekends, Middle Eastern tourists in winter). From April to October, it's the south-west monsoon: 77-91% rainfall, sometimes rough sea, many hotels stay open. Highlights: Langkawi Sky Bridge (Langkawi Sky Bridge, 125m suspension bridge curved in an arc, at 700m altitude above the jungle, accessible by Langkawi Cable Car = one of the steepest in the world at 42°, 2.1 km, 15 min, panoramic view over the 99 islands), Pantai Cenang (the main 2 km beach, white sand, turquoise water, nightlife, hotels in all categories, parasailing, jet ski), Pantai Tengah (south, more upmarket, Tanjung Rhu even more upmarket to the north), Telaga Tujuh ("7 Wells", 7-tier waterfall, swimming, natural slide down on the polished rocks), Eagle Square (Dataran Lang, 12m giant eagle statue, signature of the town, Kuah main port and capital), Underwater World (aquarium), Mahsuri's Mausoleum (mausoleum of the Mahsuri legend, princess of Langkawi unjustly executed in 1819, her curse is said to have caused 7 generations of misfortune on the island until 1980), Galeria Perdana (museum of gifts to Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamad, fascinating cabinet of diplomatic curiosities), Kilim Geoforest Park (mangroves, caves, kayak, Brahminy Kite eagle observation = symbol of Langkawi). Surrounding islands: Pulau Payar Marine Park (snorkelling, diving), Pulau Beras Basah, Pulau Singa Besar, classic island-hopping tour 4 islands in 1 day. Try nasi lemak, char kway teow, Penang laksa, satay, fresh seafood (the daily night market in rotating villages by day of the week is the institution). Langkawi airport (LGK) with direct flights from KL in 1h, Singapore, Bangkok.
borneo:en: Borneo, the world's 3rd largest island (after Greenland and New Guinea) in South-East Asia: 23 million inhabitants over 743,330 km² (larger than mainland France). Island shared between 3 countries: Indonesia in the south ("Kalimantan" 73% of the island), Malaysia in the north (Sabah + Sarawak 26%), Brunei 1% enclave. Renowned for its primary jungle = one of the world's oldest at 130 million years, sheltering 50% of Asia's humid tropical biodiversity + endangered endemic wild orangutans (deforestation for palm oil = a major ecological catastrophe of the 21st century, 50% of forests destroyed since 1973). February is my favourite month (6.5/10): 24-28°C, 56% rain, this is the relatively dry season. Borneo never tops 6.7/10 (humid hot equatorial climate year-round). From July to September, it's the relative "kemarau" dry season: 24-29°C, this is the tourist high season. From November to March, it's the monsoon: 79-92% rainfall.
