From April to May and October-December, these are the rainy seasons (43-86% rainfall, especially in April): national park tracks can become difficult. June to October remains the best period for safaris: Masai Mara with the Great Migration (July-September), Amboseli with Kilimanjaro in the background. Safety demands vigilance (Westlands, Karen, Lavington = secure neighbourhoods; avoid the city centre at night). The David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust (elephant orphanage) opens 1h/day at 11h. Nairobi National Park is unique: safari with the city skyline in the background. Karen district (after Karen Blixen) keeps its colonial charm with the Giraffe Centre nearby. Airport bus unreliable: Uber or taxi recommended.
kenya:en: Kenya, country in East Africa: 53 million inhabitants, capital Nairobi, crossed by the equator. One of the most popular safari destinations in the world thanks to the Masai Mara and the Great Migration (1.5 million wildebeest + 200,000 zebras crossing the Mara River from the Tanzanian Serengeti each year July-October). February is my favourite month (8.9/10): 15-28°C, 28% rain, this is the ideal dry season, wildlife visible. From June to October, it's the safari high season: 13-26°C, 15-47% rain, and it's the Great Migration in the Masai Mara (July-October, spectacular river crossings with crocodiles). From March to May, it's the long rainy season: 60-86% rainfall, prices halved but sometimes difficult access to the parks (muddy tracks), birth of baby animals. From November-December, it's the short rainy season: 47-76% rainfall, but brief showers. Regions to know: Masai Mara National Reserve (1500 km², the most famous, lions/leopards/cheetahs/elephants/giraffes/buffaloes = Big Five + zebras/wildebeest, hotels Mara Serena/Mara Plains/Sarova or mobile camps), Amboseli (park with spectacular view of Tanzanian Kilimanjaro, elephant herds), Tsavo East and West (Kenya's largest parks, reddish elephants), Lake Nakuru (millions of pink flamingos, rhinos), Lake Naivasha (hippos), Mount Kenya (5199m, Africa's 2nd highest peak after Kilimanjaro, 5-day climb), Samburu (in the north, more arid, unique species: reticulated giraffe, beisa oryx, Somali ostrich, gerenuk), Lamu (UNESCO coastal), Diani Beach (coastal), Nairobi (capital, see dedicated review). Try nyama choma (grilled meat), ugali (polenta), sukuma wiki (spicy collard greens), chapati (Indian heritage), Tusker beer (national pride).
tanzanie:en: Tanzania, country in East Africa: 60 million inhabitants, official capital Dodoma (but Dar es Salaam remains the economic capital). The most geographically diverse country in East Africa: Kilimanjaro (5895m, Africa's highest peak), Serengeti and Ngorongoro (UNESCO, the world's most famous safari parks), Indian coast with Zanzibar, Lake Tanganyika (Africa's deepest). August is my favourite month (9.6/10): 16-28°C, 0% rain, this is the ideal dry season, wildlife visible. From June to October, it's the safari high season: 15-31°C, 0-6% rain, blue sky, wildlife concentrated around water points (maximum visibility). From July-August, it's the Great Migration (1.5 million wildebeest cross from the Serengeti to the Masai Mara in Kenya, spectacular Mara River crossings with crocodiles). From March to May, it's the long rainy season: 14-47% rainfall, safari prices halved or thirded but difficult access (tracks), many lodges close. From November-December, it's the short rainy season: 17-57% rainfall. Regions to know: Serengeti National Park (UNESCO, 14,750 km², plains as far as the eye can see, Great Migration, abundant predators), Ngorongoro Crater (UNESCO, the world's largest intact caldera 18 km in diameter, complete closed-circuit ecosystem, record animal density), Tarangire (elephants, ancient baobabs), Lake Manyara (tree-climbing lions), Kilimanjaro National Park (UNESCO, 6-9 day ascent via 7 different routes, overall success rate 60%, up to 90% on the 8-day Lemosho route, altitude sickness the main challenge), Zanzibar (UNESCO Stone Town), Selous Game Reserve (UNESCO, Africa's largest hunting reserve but recently transformed into Nyerere National Park), Mafia Island (whale shark diving), Mwanza and Lake Victoria (Africa's largest lake). Try ugali, nyama choma, mishkaki, pilau, ndizi (banana), kahawa (Tanzanian coffee), Kilimanjaro Lager, Konyagi gin.
diani:en: Diani Beach, beach resort on the south Kenya coast 30 min from Mombasa: 17 km of fine white-sand beach on the Indian Ocean, ranked among Africa's best beaches by many rankings ("World Travel Awards"). September is my favourite month (7.8/10): 23-27°C, 73% rain, sea at 26°C, end of the cool season. Diani never tops 7.8/10 (humid equatorial tropical climate). From June to October, it's the tourist high season: 22-28°C, more bearable climate, calm sea on the lagoon side (coral reef 1 km from the beach). From March to May, it's the long rainy season: 49-84% rainfall, many hotels close or reduce activities. From November-December, it's the short rainy season: 64-72% rainfall. Activities: beach (turquoise water, fine talcum-style white sand, big tides ±3m exposing 500m of sand), snorkelling-diving at Kisite-Mpunguti Marine Park to the south (snorkelling with dolphins, manta rays), kitesurf at Galu Beach (steady wind June-September = kuzi season), butterfly farm (Bombo Forest), Colobus Conservation (centre for the protection of colobus monkeys, canopy bridges crossing the main Diani Beach Road), Shimba Hills National Park 30 min away (one of the only places in Kenya to see sable antelopes, elephants), Wasini Island by boat for a day (Swahili village, crab lunch at Charlie Claw's). Hotels and resorts: Diani Reef Beach Resort, Almanara Resort, Leopard Beach (formerly Sands at Nomad), 100-500€/night depending on category. Classic safari + beach Kenya combination: Masai Mara 3 nights + Diani 4 nights = very popular "Safari & Beach" circuit. Try mahamri (Swahili coconut-milk doughnut), fish curry, biriyani, dawa cocktail (vodka-lemon-honey-ginger, Kenyan signature). Logistics: Diani 30 min from Mombasa airport via Likoni Ferry (free for pedestrians, paid for vehicles), or Ukunda Airstrip (direct flights from Nairobi).
mombasa:en: Mombasa, Kenya's 2nd city and main port in East Africa: 1.2 million inhabitants on the Indian Ocean. Historic island linked to the coast by 4 bridges/causeways, unique Swahili-Arab-Portuguese-British heritage (successive occupations since the 8th century). From June to September are my favourite months (7.8/10): 22-27°C, 65-71% rain, this is the relative "cool season", sea at 26°C. Mombasa never tops 7.8/10 (humid equatorial climate). From July to October, it's the tourist high season: 23-28°C, more bearable climate. From March to May, it's the long rainy season: 52-90% rainfall, peak in April, many hotels close. From November-December, it's the short rainy season: 67-76% rainfall. Mombasa highlights: Fort Jesus (UNESCO 2011, Portuguese fortlet 1593-1596 built to protect Mombasa from the Ottomans, taken and retaken 9 times between Portuguese-Omani-British), Old Town (19th-century Swahili-Arab blend, alley maze, characteristic carved wooden doors), Mandhry Mosque (1570, one of East Africa's oldest mosques still in use), Mackinnon Market, Jain Temple (the most important outside India). South coast beaches (south of Likoni Ferry): Diani Beach 30 min away (the most famous, 17 km white sand, turquoise water, palm trees, ranked among Africa's best beaches — see dedicated review), Tiwi, Galu. North coast beaches: Nyali Beach, Bamburi, Shanzu (hotel zone). Excursions: Tsavo East National Park 3h away (safari, reddish elephants characteristic of the local red earth), Lamu 6h by bus + boat (UNESCO, see dedicated review), Wasini Island and Kisite Marine Park (snorkelling with dolphins). Try Swahili biriyani, Kenyan samosa, ugali, nyama choma (grilled meat), Tusker beer (the national pride).
arusha:en: Arusha is the capital of Tanzanian safari: Kilimanjaro airport 1h away, agencies for Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire, Manyara. At 1400m altitude, temperate African climate. September is my favourite month (8.8/10): 14-25°C, 19% rain, the right moment between the dry season and the first rains. From June to October, it's the great dry season: wildebeest migration in the Serengeti (Mara River crossing in July-August, peak of 1.5 million animals). From April to May are the long rains (56-90% rainfall): many lodges close or reduce rates (-50%), tracks sometimes impassable, but animals still present in green concentrations. From November to May, it's the green season that follows, with baby season (newborn wildebeest in February in the south of the Serengeti). Plan 200-1500$/night depending on lodge type. The Maasai Market in Arusha for souvenirs and shuka fabrics. Mount Meru (4566m) for acclimatisation before Kilimanjaro.
moshi:en: Moshi is the base camp for Kilimanjaro (5895m): the majority of treks start here or in nearby Marangu. At 900m altitude. September is my favourite month (9.3/10): 18-28°C, 11% rain, clear skies to see snow-capped Kibo from town. March-April are the long rains (42-87% rainfall): many tour operators advise against the climb, muddy trails, reduced visibility. October-November bring the short rains (25-44%), less of a handicap but to factor in. June to October remains the most prized period for the trek (full dry season). Plan 1500-3500€ for 6-7 days of climbing depending on the agency and route (Marangu, Machame, Lemosho). The region also produces excellent arabica coffee (plantation visits at Materuni). Arusha 1h30 away is the alternative base for Serengeti and Ngorongoro.
lamu:en: Lamu, island in the eponymous archipelago in northern Kenya: 25,000 inhabitants on the main island, UNESCO 1981 for its city centre Lamu Old Town (the oldest and best-preserved Swahili town in East Africa, founded in the 7th century, atmosphere frozen in the 18th-19th century). No cars on the island (apart from 2 administrative vehicles), only donkey and boats for transport. September is my favourite month (8.4/10): 24-28°C, 50% rain, end of the cool season. From June to October, it's the tourist high season: 24-29°C, sea at 27°C, SW monsoon, blue sky. From April to May, it's the long rainy season: 76-77% rainfall, many hotels close. From November-December, it's the short rainy season: 42-59% rainfall. Highlights: Lamu Old Town (UNESCO, maze of narrow alleys with characteristic carved wooden Swahili doors — 200 doors recorded, each with its own name, some 300 years old), Central Market (Donkey Market), Lamu Fort (19th century, built by the Sultan of Oman), Lamu Museum (siwa — crescent-shaped ceremonial ivory horns unique in the world), House of Swahili Culture (traditional house museum). Beaches: Shela Beach (12 km of white-sand beach 30 min by dhow from Lamu Town, smarter and quieter atmosphere, Peponi hotels), Manda Bay (on a neighbouring island). Activities: dhow ride (traditional sailing boat, half-day rental 25-50€/person with onboard fish barbecue lunch), sport fishing (marlin, sailfish), snorkelling at Manda Toto. Safety: Lamu is far from the Somali border (130 km to the north), an at-risk zone for Europeans since 2011 (tourist kidnappings), Kenyan military presence on the island, situation stabilised but travel advisories still demand vigilance for rural areas in northern Kenya. Try Lamu biryani (the most famous on the East African coast), samosa, kashata (coconut sweet), mishikaki meatballs. Lamu Cultural Festival in November (dhow racing, traditional dances).
dar-es-salaam:en: Dar es Salaam ("Abode of Peace" in Arabic), former capital and largest city of Tanzania: 7 million inhabitants in the urban area on the Indian Ocean (official capital moved to Dodoma in the centre of the country in 1996, but Dar es Salaam remains the economic capital and main hub). July is my favourite month (9.0/10): 22-28°C, 18% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From June to October, it's the tourist high season: 22-29°C, 18-51% rain, dry and (relatively) cool climate. From March to May, it's the long rainy season ("masika"): 52-89% rainfall, sometimes rough sea. From November-December, it's the short rainy season ("vuli"): 64-68% rainfall, but brief showers. Highlights: National Museum and House of Culture (the "Olduvai Gorge collection" with the human ancestor fossils discovered by Mary Leakey in 1959, including "Nutcracker Man" Australopithecus 1.8 million years old), Kariakoo Market (Tanzania's largest market, lively and chaotic), Mwenge Carvers Market (Makonde sculptures, ebony work), Coco Beach (Oyster Bay, urban beach), Kivukoni district (port and morning fish market). Excursions: Kigamboni Beach (south by ferry, deserted beaches), Bagamoyo 1h away (former capital of German East Africa then Tanganyika 1888-1916, 19th-century slave port), Mafia Island (30 min by plane, whale shark diving October-March). Dar es Salaam is above all the transit base for the safari parks in the north (Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire, Manyara 1h by plane to Arusha) and for Zanzibar (1h by ferry or 30 min by plane). Try ugali, nyama choma (grilled meat), Swahili pilau (spicy pilaf), mishkaki (skewers), biryani (Indian heritage), Kilimanjaro Lager.
stone-town:en: Stone Town, historic capital of the Zanzibar archipelago in Tanzania on Unguja island: UNESCO 2000 for its stone city centre, architectural blend unique in the world Swahili-Arab-Indian-European. Historic heart of the slave and spice trade between Africa, Arabia and India from the 10th to the 19th century. August is my favourite month (8.2/10): 23-28°C, 55% rain, this is the ideal dry season, sea at 26°C. From June to October, it's the tourist high season: 23-29°C, dry climate, kuzi wind (SE monsoon). From March to May, it's the long rainy season ("masika"): 64-93% rainfall, many hotels close, ferries to the mainland reduced. From November-December, it's the short rainy season ("vuli"): 67-77% rainfall, but brief showers. Highlights: Old Fort (17th-century Portuguese converted by the Omanis), House of Wonders (former 19th-century sultan's palace, the 1st electricity and 1st lift in East Africa, closed for renovation since 2015 partial collapse), Sultan's Palace (Beit el-Sahel), Aga Khan Mosque, Christ Church Cathedral (Anglican, built on the former slave market, altar on the very site of the whipping post, basement memorial of the underground rooms where slaves were crammed), Slave Market Memorial (poignant), Forodhani Gardens (night market every evening, skewers, pweza wa nazi octopus in coconut, mishkaki, Zanzibar sweet soup), Birthplace of Freddie Mercury (the Queen singer was born Farrokh Bulsara in Stone Town in 1946, plaque on the family building), Spice Tour on the plantations 30 min away (Zanzibar remains the world's largest exporter of cloves, plantations of cardamom, vanilla, cinnamon). East coast beaches 1h away: Paje, Jambiani (kitesurf at Paje, big tides). North coast beaches: Nungwi, Kendwa (no tide effect). Try Zanzibar pizza (unique street food), urojo soup ("Zanzibar mix"), Swahili biryani.
louxor:en: Luxor (former Thebes) is the "historic capital of Ancient Egypt": 500,000 inhabitants on both banks of the Nile, the world's most impressive pharaonic sites. March is my favourite month (8.8/10): 14-29°C, 1% rain, ideal temperature to visit the temples (heat still bearable). From November to March, it's the high season: 9-29°C, ideal dry climate. From May to September drop to 3.2-3.3/10 due to extreme heatwave (24-42°C, sometimes 50°C in July-August, desert nearby), ground temperature in the Valley of the Kings unbearable after 11h. East bank highlights: Karnak Temple (the largest religious complex ever built, 134 giant columns of the hypostyle hall, sound and light show in the evening), Luxor Temple (linked to Karnak by the Avenue of Sphinxes restored in 2021, magnificent when lit up in the evening). West bank: Valley of the Kings (62 royal tombs including Tutankhamun, ticket for 3 tombs + supplement for Tutankhamun/Seti I/Ramses VI), Valley of the Queens (Nefertari), Hatshepsut Temple (Deir el-Bahari, 3 terraces), Colossi of Memnon. Nile cruise Luxor-Aswan (3-4 nights, one of the classic Egypt experiences, dahabieh sailing boat = premium version). Hot air balloon at sunrise over the Valley of the Kings (100-150€). Safety: Luxor remains among Egypt's safest cities (tourist police everywhere). Direct flight Cairo-Luxor in 1h.
assouan:en: Aswan (Assouan), city in southern Egypt on the first cataract of the Nile: 290,000 inhabitants 900 km south of Cairo. Extreme desert climate of the eastern Sahara. November is my favourite month (8.7/10): 16-29°C, 0% rain, ideal temperatures after the summer heatwave. From November to February, it's the tourist high season: 9-29°C, ideal dry climate. From May to September drop to 3.1-3.3/10 due to extreme heat (24-42°C, sometimes 50°C in June-July, one of the hottest inhabited cities in the world), totally inadvisable for tourism. From March-April and October, these are the in-between seasons: 14-36°C. Highlights: Philae Temple (UNESCO, international rescue 1972-1980 = dismantled stone by stone then rebuilt identically on the neighbouring island of Agilkia to escape the flooding from the Aswan Dam, dedicated to the goddess Isis last ancient Egyptian temple closed in 537 AD), Aswan High Dam (1960-1970, financed by the Soviet USSR after American refusal in 1956 = Suez crisis, 3.6 km long, 111m high, created Lake Nasser 5250 km², the world's 3rd largest artificial lake), Unfinished Obelisk (ancient granite quarry with a 42m obelisk abandoned in the 4th century BC, cracked during cutting — would have been the tallest ever made if completed), Elephantine Island (Nile, archaeological excavations, Nubian village Siou and Koti), Aga Khan III Mausoleum (died 1957, his widow climbed there every day to lay a rose until her own death in 2000), Aswan Souk (one of Egypt's most picturesque, spices, cosmetics, hibiscus karkadé). Major excursions: Abu Simbel 280 km to the south (UNESCO, colossal temples of Ramses II 13th century BC, also rescued 1964-1968 = cut into 30-tonne blocks and reassembled 65m higher to escape Lake Nasser, 4 statues of Ramses II 20m high carved into the cliff, solar alignment 22 February and 22 October illuminating the inner sanctuary, 35 min flight or 3h30 by bus with military convoy), Nile cruise Aswan-Luxor (3-7 nights on a dahabieh or modern ship, descent of 200 km of the Nile, stops at the temples of Kom Ombo and Edfu). Try molokhia, ful medames, kebab and koshary, hibiscus karkadé tea.
marsa-alam:en: Marsa Alam is the opposite of Hurghada: a recent resort, calm, dedicated to serious diving rather than entertainment. Southern Red Sea, still preserved reefs. April is my favourite month (9.4/10): 22-29°C, 0% rain, sea at 24°C. The major sites: Elphinstone Reef (longimanus in autumn, for confirmed divers), Sataya Reef and Dolphin House (spinner dolphin pod, snorkelling from boat), Abu Dabbab (dugongs). From June to October, expect 25-36°C: dry heat remains bearable on the seafront but trying on the boat all day. From December to February, expect 14-23°C and a wind that can blow at 40 km/h, sharply cooling the feels-like. 5mm wetsuit for diving. Marsa Alam stays quieter than Sharm el-Sheikh, but restaurant and outing options outside the hotel are limited. Charter flights from Europe in high season.
jordanie:en: Jordan (Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan), Middle Eastern country between Israel, Syria, Iraq and Saudi Arabia: 11 million inhabitants. Constitutional kingdom led by King Abdullah II since 1999 (Hashemite dynasty descended from the Prophet Muhammad). One of the most stable and welcoming countries in the Middle East despite its explosive location. May is my favourite month (9.7/10): 13-29°C, 4% rain, ideal temperatures. April and October stay excellent (9.0-9.4/10). From March to May and September-November are the tourist high season: 8-30°C. From June to August, expect 18-36°C: intense heat (40°C+ Wadi Rum desert), but dry so bearable morning/evening. From December to February, expect 5-15°C and 14-19% rain: relative cold for the Middle East, snow possible in Amman and above 700m altitude. Highlights: Petra (UNESCO 1985, one of the 7 New Wonders of the World 2007, Nabataean city carved into the pink-red sandstone cliff 4th century BC, access via the Siq = narrow canyon 1.2 km snaking between 80m cliffs, opening onto the Khazneh = "Treasury" 40m high signature visual of Petra, recreated in Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade 1989, see at 6:30am to avoid heat and crowds), Petra by Night (the Khazneh lit by 1500 candles + Bedouin musical show, 3 evenings/week 20:30, paid extra, striking experience), Wadi Rum (red sandstone desert 720 km², T.E. Lawrence of Arabia fought here during the Arab Revolt 1916-1918, Lawrence of Arabia 1962 + The Martian 2015 + Dune 2021 were filmed here, signature experience: night in a Bedouin camp under the stars + 4×4 jeep tour + camel + hike), Dead Sea (border with Israel, 430m below sea level = lowest point on Earth's surface, miraculous floating swim thanks to 30% salinity = 10x the Mediterranean, therapeutic mud for the skin), Amman (capital, 4 million inhabitants, ancient Citadel with Roman Temple of Hercules and 8th-century Umayyad palace, Roman Theatre capacity 6000 seats 2nd century AD), Jerash (one of the best-preserved Greco-Roman cities in the Middle East, 2nd century AD, Oval Forum, Cardo Maximus columns 800m, theatres), Aqaba (see dedicated review), Mount Nebo (place from where Moses is said to have seen the Promised Land according to the Bible), Madaba ("city of mosaics", 6th-century Byzantine mosaic map of the Holy Land). Try mansaf (Jordanian national dish, lamb with fermented jameed yoghurt on pilaf rice), maqlooba, knafeh dessert (hot stringy cheese on syrup), Arabic coffee with cardamom, Bedouin tea with sage. Safety: country generally safe, caution at the Syrian and Iraqi northern and eastern borders. Logistics: Queen Alia airport Amman (AMM) with direct flights from Paris in 4h30, second airport Aqaba (AQJ).
aqaba:en: Aqaba, Jordan's only port on the Red Sea at the far south of the country: 150,000 inhabitants on the Israeli border (Eilat 8 km away), Saudi and Egyptian (Gulf of Aqaba shared by 4 countries). Special economic duty-free zone status since 2001 (tax-free shopping). Jordan's capital of seaside tourism and Red Sea diving. November is my favourite month (9.3/10): 13-26°C, 5% rain, sea temperature 25°C, ideal temperatures. December-March stay excellent (8.4-9.2/10). From November to April, it's the tourist high season: 13-30°C, sea at 22-26°C, hotel prices x2-x3. From June to August drop to 3.4-3.9/10 due to extreme heatwave (24-41°C, sometimes 47°C+). From April to May and September-October, expect 18-37°C: warm intermediate. From January to February, expect 9-22°C: mild for the Middle East, ideal for visiting without heat. Highlights: Aqaba beaches (free public beaches but limited, private beaches of the 4-5* hotels-resorts recommended: Movenpick, Tala Bay 17 km to the south), Aqaba Marine Park (protected marine park with snorkelling and diving accessible from the beach), Underwater site Cedar Pride (80m wreck of a Lebanese cargo ship deliberately scuttled 1985 for an artificial reef, unmissable dive accessible from the coast 7m deep), Tank Memorial (M42 Duster tank wreck scuttled 1999 at 6m depth, snorkelling-friendly, signature for underwater photographers), Red Sea diving = main Aqaba activity (warm waters 22-28°C, visibility 20-40m, 1200 fish species, multicoloured corals, PADI Open Water training 4-5 days 350-450€), Aqaba Mamluk Fortress (16th century, taken by T.E. Lawrence and the Bedouin army in July 1917 during the Arab Revolt, turning point of WWI in the Middle East), Aqaba Archaeological Museum, Aqaba Flag (gigantic mast 132m high flying the flag of the 1916 Arab Revolt, signature visual of the city visible from Israel and Egypt). Essential excursions: Wadi Rum 1h by car (the great Jordanian desert of Lawrence of Arabia, mentioned in Petra), Petra 2h by car (UNESCO, Jordan's major attraction, descend from Aqaba for a day if a short stay), Eilat Israel 8 km away (border crossable on foot at the Yitzhak Rabin Border Crossing 8h-20h, different culture-atmosphere, possibility of seeing Egypt Taba). Try sayadieh (typical Arab port fish-and-spiced-rice dish), fresh seafood, mansaf (Jordanian national dish), knafeh, Arabic coffee, fresh fruit juices. Logistics: King Hussein International Aqaba airport (AQJ) with direct flights Amman (45 min), Brussels, Berlin European charters.
wadi-rum:en: Wadi Rum ("Valley of the Moon") is Jordan's desert: 720 km² of Martian landscapes (used for Lawrence of Arabia 1962, The Martian 2015, Star Wars Rogue One and Rise of Skywalker, Dune 2021). October is my favourite month (9.5/10): 15-28°C, 2% rain, ideal desert temperatures. From September to November and March-May are the best periods: 8-31°C, ideal dry climate. From June to August, expect 20-34°C: intense daytime heat (45°C+ possible), but cool nights in the desert (15-20°C). From December to February, expect 4-16°C: pleasant days but freezing nights (sometimes 0°C, snow rare but possible), many camps still operate with heating. Activities: 4×4 tour half-day or full day (Lawrence Spring, Khazali Canyon, Burdah Bridge, red dunes, Nabataean petroglyphs, 30-60€), camel ride 1-3h, hiking on Jebel Khash or Jebel Burdah, climbing (Wadi Rum is a global climbing spot on sandstone). Night in a Bedouin camp = essential experience: classic Bedouin camp (40-80€/night, basic tents, zarb meals cooked in the earth), bubble tents ("Mars camps", transparent domes for stargazing, 100-300€/night). Logistics: Wadi Rum 4h by bus from Amman, 1h from Petra, 1h from Aqaba. Plan 2 days / 1 night minimum. Community tour with a Bedouin family rather than a classic tour operator. Try zarb, mansaf (Jordanian national dish), sage tea.
amman:en: Amman is the logistics base for Jordan: international airport, affordable taxis, English widely spoken. At 800m altitude, semi-arid continental climate. May is my favourite month (9.7/10): 15-28°C, 5% rain, sharp light on the Roman theatre and the citadel. From June to August, expect 19-33°C with 0% rain: hot and dry, but bearable as humidity is low. Evenings stay pleasant on Rainbow Street. From December to February, the nights drop to 4°C and snow can block traffic 1-2 days per winter (the city is not equipped for it). Petra 3h away by bus is a classic, Wadi Rum 4h away for 1-2 nights of bivouac. The café scene in the Jabal Lweibdeh district has grown around artists and the diaspora. The Jordanian mezze (mansaf, makluba) stays alive far from the tourist restaurants.
tel-aviv:en: Tel Aviv is one of the most secular and most 24/7 cities in the Middle East. UNESCO White City with 4000 1930s Bauhaus buildings, urban beaches, global tech scene. June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 21-29°C, 2% rain, sea at 23°C. From July to September, expect 23-31°C with heavy humidity (the hamsin/sirocco can push the feels-like to 40°C). Tel Avivians live in air conditioning, head out to the seafront in the evening. From December to March, expect 10-20°C and 25-39% rain: humid, sometimes storms, but mild winters. The Carmel and Levinsky markets for Israeli cuisine (sabich, hummus, sambousek). Yafo (Jaffa, old Arab city) to the south for the port and antique dealers. The security context evolves by period: to be checked (notably 2023-2025). Jerusalem 1h by train, Dead Sea 1h30 away, Negev desert 2h away. The nightlife scene is one of the most active in the Mediterranean basin.
abu-dhabi:en: Abu Dhabi, capital of the United Arab Emirates: 1.5 million inhabitants, calmer and culturally more developed than Dubai (90 min by road). December is my favourite month (9.2/10): 17-27°C, 4% rain, ideal temperatures, sea at 23°C. From June to September drop to 3.0-3.6/10 due to extreme heat (28-43°C, sometimes 50°C, maximum humidity). From November to March, it's the high season: 15-30°C, prices x2-x3, cultural events (Abu Dhabi Festival, Formula 1 late November/early December). Highlights: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque (one of the world's largest mosques, 40,000 worshippers, 96 columns, the world's largest carpet and Swarovski chandelier, free, abaya lent for women), Louvre Abu Dhabi (Jean Nouvel architecture, signature metallic dome, partnership with Louvre Paris), Qasr Al Watan (presidential palace open to the public since 2019), Yas Island (Ferrari World, Yas Marina F1 Circuit, Warner Bros World), Saadiyat Island (Louvre + future Frank Gehry Guggenheim), Corniche (8 km of seaside promenade). Empty Quarter (Liwa) 2h away for the purest desert. Try luqaimat, harees, machbous. Direct flight from Paris in 7h.
doha:en: Doha, capital of Qatar on the Persian Gulf: 2.4 million inhabitants (90% expatriates mostly Asian for construction and services, only 10% Qataris). Ultra-rich emirate thanks to oil and especially natural gas (3rd world reserve after Russia and Iran). Host of the 2022 Football World Cup (1st Arab country to organise the event, controversy over working conditions of Asian workers and LGBTQ+ rights). March is my favourite month (8.9/10): 16-26°C, 7% rain, this is the ideal dry season. November-December stay excellent (8.6/10). From November to April, it's the tourist high season: 13-31°C, pleasant dry and warm climate. From May to October drop to 2.2-6.4/10 due to extreme heatwave (25-42°C, sometimes 50°C+, oppressive coastal humidity). Highlights: Souq Waqif (traditional souk rebuilt in 2006 in its 19th-century style, traditional oriental atmosphere with merchants of spices/perfumes/fabrics/falcons — falconry = traditional Qatari sport, Arab restaurants, terraces, Doha's #1 attraction for tourists), Museum of Islamic Art ("MIA", I.M. Pei architecture 2008, one of the architectural masterpieces of the 21st century, exceptional global Islamic art collection, free), National Museum of Qatar (spectacular architecture by Jean Nouvel 2019 inspired by desert rose crystals, panorama of Qatari history), The Pearl-Qatar (4 km² artificial island European Riviera-style with marinas, smart restaurants, luxury boutiques), Katara Cultural Village (cultural centre opened 2010, amphitheatre, mosques, exhibitions), Doha Corniche (7 km seaside promenade with panoramic view over the West Bay skyscraper skyline, best photos at sunset), Doha Skyline (West Bay, signature skyscrapers by international architects Norman Foster + Jean Nouvel + I.M. Pei + Zaha Hadid), Lusail (futuristic new city created 30 km north of Doha for the 2022 World Cup, Lusail Stadium 80,000 seats site of the Argentina-France final 18 December 2022), Aspire Park and Khalifa International Stadium (World Cup legacy), Inland Sea Khor Al Adaid 1h away (UNESCO under listing, inland sea unique in the world where the sea meets the desert dunes), Qatari desert ("dune bashing" = 4×4 expedition on the dunes signature of Gulf stays), Camel Race Track (camel races on Friday mornings November-March, camels ridden by robot-jockeys since 2005 after scandal of underpaid Asian child jockeys). Excursions: Bahrain 30 min by plane (other neighbouring island emirate), Dubai 1h by plane. Try machboos (spiced rice Qatari national dish), majboos kebab, harees (spiced porridge for ramadan), shawarma, kunafa, Arabic coffee with mabsali dates, fresh fruit juices (alcohol is strictly controlled in Qatar, sold only in certain 5*+ licensed hotels at prohibitive prices). Safety: Qatar is one of the safest countries in the world, zero tolerance for drugs/alcohol/indecent dress. Hamad International airport (DOH) Qatar Airways hub, one of the world's best airports.
reunion:en: La Réunion, French overseas department in the Indian Ocean: 2500 km², 870,000 inhabitants, volcanic island with 3 UNESCO cirques (Mafate, Salazie, Cilaos), Piton de la Fournaise (volcano in permanent eruption). September is my favourite month (7.3/10): 9-16°C in the mountains / 23-26°C on the coast, 42% rain. La Réunion has 2 radically different climates: East (Au Vent) very wet year-round, West (Sous le Vent) drier and sunnier. From May to November, it's the austral winter: 17-23°C in the mountains / 22-25°C on the coast, the driest period, humpback whales mid-July to mid-October. From December to April, it's the austral summer: 14-19°C in the mountains / 24-30°C on the coast, cyclone season (3-5 cyclones/season), very pleasant swimming but also sharks (swimming banned outside protected lagoons since the 2011 shark crisis, 11 deaths since). Highlights: Piton de la Fournaise (2632m, accessible by 4×4 + 6h return hike, one of the world's most active, last eruption in the enclosure), Cirque de Mafate (accessible only on foot, by helicopter or porter, GR R2 to cross, refuges in isolated îlets), Cirque de Salazie (Hell-Bourg listed), Cirque de Cilaos (road of 400 bends), Piton des Neiges (3070m, the highest point in the Indian Ocean, 8h return ascent, very famous sunrise at the summit), Saint-Denis (capital, Creole architecture), Saint-Gilles (lagoon, seaside resort). Try rougail saucisse, cari poulet, samosas, rhum arrangé.
mayotte:en: Mayotte (Maore in Mahorais), French overseas department in the Mozambique Channel north-west of Madagascar: 320,000 inhabitants over 374 km² (huge density 855 hab/km²), 101st French department since 2011 (the youngest department). Made up of Grande-Terre and Petite-Terre linked by a ferry. Administrative status unique in France: 95% Muslim population, polygamy tolerated until 2010, mother tongue Shimaoré for the majority. Chronic migration and security crisis for the past 20 years (50% of the population are undocumented Comorians, high crime rate). Cyclone Chido in December 2024 devastated the island. July is my favourite month (9.0/10): 22-27°C, 21% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From June to September are the tourist high season: 22-28°C, lagoon water at 25°C, also humpback whale season (June-October, observation of the Madagascar-Antarctica crossing). From November to April, it's the rainy + cyclonic season: 70-89% rainfall, real cyclone risk but usually low (Chido 2024 = major exception since 1934). Highlights: Lagoon of Mayotte (1500 km², one of the world's largest closed lagoons, turquoise waters over white-sand bottom, rare double coral barrier = the outer barrier + an inner barrier, exceptional diving and snorkelling), Mayotte islands: Petite-Terre (atolls Dzaoudzi, Pamandzi with airport, no direct link with Mayotte), Mayotte/Grande-Terre (main island, capital Mamoudzou), Ilot M'Bouzi (reserve, lemurs), Mtsamboro Island, Turtle Island, Bandrélé and Saziley (green turtle laying Nov-March), Mount Choungui (594m, the highest point accessible by 1h walk, 360° panorama), Lake Karihani (sacred lake in a volcanic crater), Saziley (extreme southern peninsula, difficult access, preserved beaches), Soulou Waterfall. Activities: diving in the lagoon (Manta Point, S Pass, Wreck of Sébastien), snorkelling, humpback whale watching (June-October, more accessible and cheaper than in neighbouring Réunion or Tahiti), kayaking in the mangrove at Bouéni, excursion to Moutsoumboutsou ("rooster crests" = sandy limestone islets, signature tropical landscapes). Safety: Mayotte has significant security problems (burglaries, social tensions), solo travel inadvisable, frequent thefts even in broad daylight, official France Diplomatie advisory of heightened vigilance. Try ailes (rice with coconut accompanied by fish), grilled lagoon fish, brèdes mafane (a particular leaf vegetable), fruit pickles, forest honey. Logistics: Dzaoudzi-Pamandzi airport (DZA) on Petite-Terre, flights from Paris-CDG in 11h via Réunion or Nairobi, high prices (1500-2500€ return).
moroni:en: Moroni, capital of the Union of the Comoros in the Indian Ocean north-west of Madagascar: 65,000 inhabitants on the island of Grande-Comore (Ngazidja). Archipelagic country of 4 islands of which 3 are independent (Grande-Comore, Anjouan, Mohéli) and 1 is French (Mayotte) which refused independence by referendum in 1974, a permanently tense diplomatic issue with France. Africa's poorest island country, chronic political instability (20+ coup attempts since 1975), little tourism. Population a cultural blend Arab-Bantu-Malagasy, Sunni Muslim religion 99%. October is my favourite month (8.2/10): 23-29°C, 47% rain, this is the end of the dry season. May-September stay excellent (7.8-8.1/10). From May to October, it's the dry season "Kashkazi": 21-29°C, sea at 25°C, this is the tourist high season. From November to April, it's the cyclonic season "Kusi": 50-84% rainfall, real cyclone risk but the Comoros are less affected than neighbouring Madagascar. Highlights: Medina of Moroni (traditional old town with narrow paved alleys, preserved Arab-Swahili atmosphere, mosques, traditional cloths), Friday Mosque (Badjanani, 17th century, one of the oldest mosques in the Comoros, signature white façade), Volga (main square by the port), Central Market (spices, tropical fruits, ylang-ylang = the Comoros' signature flower, producing 80% of the world's ylang-ylang essential oil used in French perfumes Chanel N°5/Guerlain), Presidential Palace Beit-Salam (exterior visit), Iconi 10 km away (former pre-colonial historic capital, sultanic ruins, spectacular cliff), Itsandra Beach and Galawa Beach in the north (white-sand beaches with bougainvilleas, little frequented by international tourism). Karthala volcano (2361m, one of the world's largest active volcanoes, last eruption 2007, demanding 7-9h ascent from Boboni village = signature Comoros experience reserved for hikers in good shape, 360° panorama over the archipelago + Madagascar visible on clear days, summit caldera 3 km in diameter), Trou du Prophète ("Bangwa Kuni", natural pool in the volcanic lava, swimming), Salt Lake (volcanic crater turned into a salt-water lake). Inter-island excursions: Anjouan 1h by plane or ferry (greener island, ylang-ylang plantations, round island "perfume island"), Mohéli (the smallest and most preserved, marine park with green turtle laying + dolphins + humpback whales July-October), Mayotte 30 min by plane (French territory, already covered by BDW, sensitive political subject on the Comorian side). Try vanilla lobster (Comorian signature blending Arab spices + Malagasy vanilla), coconut chicken, Comorian samosa (Indian heritage), ladu (coconut-almond cake), local coffee with cardamom (Arab heritage), Indian chai tea. Logistics: Moroni Prince Said Ibrahim airport (HAH), flights from Paris via Nairobi/Addis Ababa 12h, from Mayotte 30 min, very basic tourist infrastructure.
cap-vert:en: Cape Verde ("Cabo Verde"), Atlantic archipelago off West Africa: 560,000 inhabitants over 4033 km², 10 inhabited islands in 2 groups (Barlavento in the north and Sotavento in the south), 570 km west of Senegal. Former Portuguese colony (1462-1975), Portuguese Creole. Extremely arid dry tropical climate (the islands sit in an intermediate rainfall situation between the Sahara and the humid tropical zone, with cyclical historic droughts). From May-June and November are my favourite months (9.4-9.5/10): 21-28°C, 0-2% rain, sea at 23°C, refreshing trade winds, ideal temperatures. From November to June, it's the tourist high season: 19-27°C, 0-3% rain, steady trade winds, ideal dry climate. From August-October drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (24-30°C) and the relative rainfall increase (8-36% but stays dry in absolute value). All year round, stable temperatures (19-30°C) thanks to the maritime effect, sea always navigable, near-constant wind. Islands to know: Sal ("salt", the most touristy thanks to the international airport, long Santa Maria beach 8 km of white sand, kitesurf at Punta Preta ranked top globally, salt pans at Pedra de Lume salt crater), Boa Vista ("beautiful view", 2nd most touristy, desert dunes at Viana, deserted Santa Monica beach 18 km, observation of laying loggerhead turtles August-October), São Vicente (Mindelo cultural capital, music festival in August, Cesária Évora the "barefoot diva" world morna legend born here), Santo Antão (the greenest and most mountainous island, Paul Valley hiking — surprising tropical landscapes for Cape Verde), Fogo ("fire", Pico do Fogo volcano 2829m the country's highest point, last eruption 2014-2015 partially destroyed Chã das Caldeiras village of 1000 inhabitants), Santiago (the largest, capital Praia, Cidade Velha UNESCO 1st European tropical city 1462). Activities: kitesurf (one of the global spots), diving, hiking in Santo Antão and Fogo, humpback whale watching March-May, turtles at Boa Vista. Try cachupa (national dish, corn/bean/cassava/vegetable/meat stew), pastel de massa, lagosta grelhada (grilled lobster), grog (local eau-de-vie), morna música (signature slow nostalgic musical style).
sal:en: Sal, gateway to Cape Verde: a flat desert island whose appeal lies entirely in the sea. A curious data point: September and October drop to 6.9/10 due to late rains brought by the Intertropical Convergence Zone (rare but striking). May-June are my favourite months (9.4/10): 21-27°C, 0-1% rain, steady trade winds. Santa Maria in the south concentrates 90% of the hotels and the kitesurf schools: the sheltered bay and the thermal winds make it a world-class spot. From August to October, stormy showers are possible (4-23% rain depending on the month). Always warm but less reliable sky. From December to April remain the European high season: escape from winter, prices climb, beaches fill up. Pedra de Lume (salt mines in a crater) and the shark watching near Essinger Beach are worth the detour.
oman:en: Oman (Sultanate of Oman), country in the Arabian peninsula at the far south-east: 5 million inhabitants over 309,500 km². One of the most stable and preserved Arab countries = sultanic monarchy under Sultan Qaboos bin Said 1970-2020 (peaceful modernisation of the country without revolution), now his cousin Haitham bin Tariq since 2020. Oman keeps a preserved cultural identity vs Dubai-Doha modernised to excess, traditional Arab atmosphere with banco-stone architecture + women in black abaya + men in signature white dishdasha. March is my favourite month (9.2/10): 22-32°C, 4% rain, this is the ideal dry season. February-November stay excellent (8.6-9.0/10). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 16-32°C, sea at 23-27°C, ideal dry and temperate climate. From May to September drop to 3.7-6.0/10 due to extreme heatwave (28-42°C, sometimes 50°C+ with oppressive coastal humidity on the Muscat side). The southern Dhofar region (Salalah) paradoxically benefits from the Khareef monsoon June-September = humidity from the Indian Ocean transforms the Dhofari desert into a green tropical landscape = unique on the Arabian peninsula, signature visual of Oman, attracts millions of Gulf tourists in summer to escape the heatwave. Highlights: Muscat capital (already covered by BDW = preserved Arab atmosphere + Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque signature), Nizwa 1h30 away (former capital of the sultanate 17th century, Nizwa fort 17th century with huge signature copper cannon, traditional date market + goats Friday morning), Bahla (UNESCO 1987, preserved oasis with banco fort), Jabrin Castle 17th century (one of Oman's most beautiful palaces), Wadi Shab 2h away (spectacular wadi with signature turquoise natural pools visual of Oman, hiking + swimming), Wadi Bani Khalid (another major wadi), Wahiba Sands Desert 3h away ("Sharqiya Sands", desert with spectacular red sand dunes, night in a Bedouin camp with dinner-stargazing + 4×4 dune-bashing signature), Sur (east coast fishing port, traditional wooden "dhow" boat building, Omani maritime heritage), Ras Al Jinz Beach (protected reserve for green turtle laying May-October, one of the world's sites for observation, signature night experience), Salalah in the south Dhofar (already covered), Musandam Peninsula at the extreme north ("Norway of Arabia", spectacular Arab fjords, dolphins, accessible via Khasab from Emirates Fujairah 1h by car), Jebel Akhdar ("Green Mountain", massif at 2000m, spectacular panoramas, signature cultivated terraces of Damascena roses producing Omani rose water, exclusive Anantara + Alila 5*+ hotels), Jebel Shams (3009m Oman's highest point, "Grand Canyon of Oman" Wadi Ghul = spectacular canyon 1000m deep). Activities: Sea of Oman diving top globally with Daymaniyat Islands in Muscat (protected marine reserve, seasonal whale sharks), desert safari Wahiba Sands, Musandam dhow cruise 1-2 days signature, Jebel Shams hiking. Try shuwa (lamb cooked 24h in a pit oven, Omani culinary signature for Eid Al Adha), majboos (spiced rice), Omani halwa (signature traditional confectionery), Omani Khalas dates, Omani coffee with cardamom + saffron + rose water, halwa with pomegranate. Logistics: Muscat airport (MCT), accessible directly from Europe via Oman Air or Emirates Doha-Dubai. Audience: Europeans in the majority (Germans + British + French), more traditional and preserved atmosphere than Dubai-Abu Dhabi, nicknamed "Norway of the Gulf" for its grandiose natural landscapes and respectful, sustainable tourism.
muscat:en: Muscat, capital of the Sultanate of Oman on the Gulf of Oman: 1.4 million inhabitants in the urban area. Reputed to be one of the most discreet and preserved Arab capitals (strict architecture respecting traditional heritage: no skyscrapers, mandatory white-beige paint on all buildings, no gaudy advertising panels). Absolutist sultanate but peaceful and welcoming (no religious conflicts, openness to non-Muslims, crime among the lowest in the world). March is my favourite month (9.2/10): 22-29°C, 6% rain, this is the ideal dry season. November stays excellent (9.1/10). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 17-29°C, ideal dry and temperate climate. From May to September, it's the extreme heatwave: 25-39°C (sometimes 47°C+), impossible to visit in the middle of the day, oppressive coastal humidity. From April to October drop to 3.4-6.9/10 due to the heat. Highlights: Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque (Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque, 2001, one of the world's largest mosques, capacity 20,000 worshippers, houses the world's 2nd largest hand-woven carpet 70m × 60m + 14m-tall Swarovski chandelier, open to non-Muslims Saturday-Thursday 8h-11h, strict dress code for women: abaya + headscarf provided at the entrance), Royal Opera House Muscat (opera house inaugurated 2011, traditional Omani architecture in white and marble, capacity 1100 spectators, classical and contemporary programming, one of the smartest opera houses in the Arab world), Mutrah Souk (former traditional Omani souk, one of the oldest in the Gulf, frankincense (Oman = world capital of frankincense since Antiquity, the incense route ran from Salalah to the Mediterranean), spices, traditional silver jewellery khanjar = signature Omani daggers, fabrics, perfumes), Mutrah Corniche (3 km seaside promenade, one of Oman's most beautiful with traditional atmosphere), Portuguese forts Al-Jalali and Al-Mirani (16th century, defended the royal port, accessible only from outside), Al Alam Palace (ceremonial palace of the Sultan, modern typically Omani architecture, no visit access but exterior photos), Bait Al Zubair Museum (museum of traditional Omani art: khanjar collection, costumes, jewellery), Old Muscat district (historic old town, guarded by fortified walls, almost deserted in the evening but traditional), National Museum (Oman's largest museum opened 2016). Essential Oman excursions: Wadi Shab 2h away ("turf valley", turquoise canyon with natural pools + signature hidden cave, 1h hike to get there, swimming required to reach the cave through a tunnel, one of the natural wonders of Oman), Wadi Bani Khalid 3h away (another canyon with turquoise pools, more accessible), Wahiba Sands Desert 3h away (huge orange dunes 100m, night in a Bedouin desert camp under the stars, dune-bashing 4×4), Nizwa 1h30 away (former Omani capital 17th century, signature circular fort, traditional Friday souk with goat-camel auctions), Jebel Akhdar 2h away ("Green Mountain" 2000m, rose plantations for AOC rose water, cool temperatures), Jebel Shams 3h away ("Mountain of the Sun" 3009m, Oman's highest point, the "Grand Canyon of Arabia" with panoramic Balcony Walk hike). Try shuwa (lamb cooked for 24h in an underground oven in banana leaves, signature Omani festive dish), majboos (spiced biryani-like Arab rice), Omani halwa (signature spiced sweet), Arabic coffee with dates (Omani welcome ritual), kahwa (cardamom coffee).
agra:en: Agra is home to the Taj Mahal (UNESCO), India's most visited monument: 7 million visitors/year. Mausoleum built 1632-1653 by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in tribute to his wife Mumtaz Mahal who died in childbirth. February is my favourite month (8.5/10): 12-25°C, 6% rain, ideal temperatures, morning mist that sublimates the Taj. From November to March, it's the high season: 8-31°C. From April to June, it's the extreme heatwave: 23-39°C (sometimes 47°C), impossible to visit in the middle of the day. From July to September, it's the monsoon: 46-73% rainfall, often grey sky (Taj less photogenic). Highlights: Taj Mahal (entry 1300 INR for foreigners, open sunrise-sunset except Friday, sunrise access MANDATORY for the golden light and to avoid crowds, 4 days/month open at night around the full moon), Agra Fort (UNESCO, Mughal fort 16th-17th century, view of the Taj from Musamman Burj where Shah Jahan ended his days imprisoned by his son Aurangzeb), Itimad-ud-Daulah ("baby Taj", more delicate forerunner of the Taj), Mehtab Bagh (garden on the other side of the Yamuna for Taj view at sunset), Fatehpur Sikri 40 km away (UNESCO, abandoned former Mughal capital). Logistics: Agra 3h from Delhi by Gatimaan Express train, mandatory stopover on the Golden Triangle. One day is enough. Try petha (local sweet).
rajasthan:en: Rajasthan, the largest Indian state (342,000 km²): 80 million inhabitants, land of Maharajas, Thar desert, palaces, fortresses, colourful traditions. February-March are my favourite months (8.9/10): 13-31°C, 3-9% rain, ideal temperatures. From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 9-27°C, ideal dry climate. From April to June, it's the extreme heatwave: 23-40°C (sometimes 49°C in the desert), impossible to visit. From July to September, it's the light monsoon: 41-69% rainfall, green landscapes unusual for the region. Classic itineraries: Golden Triangle Delhi-Agra-Jaipur (5-7 days), Royal Loop Delhi-Jaipur-Pushkar-Udaipur-Jodhpur-Jaisalmer-Bikaner-Delhi (15-21 days). Main cities: Jaipur ("pink"), Udaipur ("white"), Jodhpur ("blue"), Jaisalmer ("golden"), Pushkar (Hindu holy city, camel festival in November), Bikaner (Junagarh Fort, Karni Mata rat temple). National parks: Ranthambore (Bengal tigers, October-June, jeeps booked 90 days ahead), Sariska (less frequented alternative). Festivals: Holi (March, festival of colours), Diwali (October-November), Pushkar Camel Fair (November, 50,000 camels). Try dal baati churma, gatte ki sabzi, ker sangri, Jodhpur lassi. October-November and March-April are the best tourism/weather compromises.
katmandou:en: Kathmandu, capital of Nepal: 1 million inhabitants in the city, 2.5 in the urban area. At 1400m altitude in a mountainous valley, 7 UNESCO sites. Rebuilt after the devastating earthquake of April 2015 (9000 deaths, 600,000 houses destroyed). April is my favourite month (8.2/10): 14-25°C, 42% rain, ideal temperatures, rhododendrons in bloom. From March to May, it's the high trekking season (8.0-8.2/10): 10-26°C, clear morning skies on the summits (Everest, Annapurnas). From June to September, it's the monsoon: 90-100% rainfall, leeches on the trails, summits hidden, but the greenest landscapes (rare period to visit Mustang and Dolpo, valleys in the rain shadow). From October to November, it's the 2nd high trekking season (8.0-8.1/10): 10-24°C, the clearest skies of the year after the monsoon, but crowds on the Annapurna and Everest BC. From December to February, expect 5-19°C: cold at altitude, treks difficult above 4000m. Highlights: Durbar Square (historic centre, partially destroyed in 2015), Pashupatinath (Hindu temple, cremations on the ghats), Boudhanath Stupa (one of the world's largest Buddhist stupas, Tibetan quarter), Swayambhunath ("Monkey Temple", stupa on a hill), Patan and Bhaktapur (medieval city-states). Classic treks from here: Annapurna Circuit (15-21 days), Annapurna Base Camp (10-14 days), Everest Base Camp (12-16 days, Lukla flight). Try momos, dal bhat, sel roti.
salamanque:en: Salamanca, in north-west Spain: 145,000 inhabitants, university city (the oldest in Europe, 1218, Europe's 3rd oldest university after Bologna and Oxford), UNESCO historic centre in golden sandstone ("piedra de Villamayor" that takes a coppery colour at sunset). July is my favourite month (9.5/10): 16-30°C, 6% rain, ideal temperatures thanks to the altitude (800m). August stays excellent (9.3/10). From June to September, it's the high season: 12-30°C, dry and hot continental climate. From October to May, expect 0-20°C: long cool spring (university term lively September-June), snow possible December-February. From December to February, expect 0-13°C and 23-26% rain: dry cold, fireplace atmosphere in the bars, terraces on Plaza Mayor with blankets and heaters. Highlights: Plaza Mayor (18th century, Churrigueresque baroque, one of Spain's most beautiful squares, the social epicentre of Salamantines in the evening), University of Salamanca (Plateresque façade 1529, game: find the "frog on the skull" = lucky charm of the students), Old and New Cathedrals (adjacent, 12th-century Romanesque + 16th-century Gothic, spot the astronaut and the faun with ice cream sculpted in the 20th century during a restoration), Casa de las Conchas (façade decorated with 300 carved 15th-century Saint Jacques shells), Ieronimites, Ponte Romano (1st-century Roman bridge, 26 arches), Convent of San Esteban, Convento de las Dueñas (Plateresque cloister). Try jamón ibérico de bellota (neighbouring Guijuelo region = the most prestigious AOC in Spain), hornazo (savoury ham-chorizo cake), farinato (bread sausage), neighbouring Rueda and Toro wines. 2h30 from Madrid by AVE train.
ronda:en: Ronda is one of the most spectacular pueblos blancos of Andalusia: town perched at 750m, crossed by the Tajo canyon (120m deep), Puente Nuevo built in 1793. June is my favourite month (9.8/10): 17-28°C, 7% rain, ideal temperatures before the heat, golden light on the Arab walls. From July to August stay excellent (8.7-8.8/10) with 21-33°C: dry heat bearable thanks to the altitude (10°C cooler than in Seville). From December to February, expect 6-15°C and 22-28% rain: mild during the day, cool in the evening, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Puente Nuevo (plunging view over the Tajo, signature photo), Plaza de Toros (1785, one of Spain's oldest, ronda bullfighting school), Casa del Rey Moro and its hanging gardens, Baños Árabes (13th-century Arab baths), Mirador de Aldehuela. Hemingway and Orson Welles stayed here regularly (the Plaza de Toros is mentioned in For Whom the Bell Tolls, the author buried in Sun Valley). Camino de los Molinos trail to descend into the Tajo. Setenil de las Bodegas (troglodyte village) 30 min away, Grazalema and the sierra 1h away for hiking. Try rabo de toro (oxtail), Ronda wine. May and September-October are the real sweet spots tourism/weather.
sierra-nevada:en: Sierra Nevada is Europe's southernmost ski resort and Spain's highest: Veleta summit 3398m, 30 km from Granada. July is my favourite month (9.3/10): 13-25°C, 13% rain, ideal sky for hiking to Mulhacén (3479m, the highest peak in mainland Spain). From December to March, expect -2/6°C and 26-45% precipitation often as snow: this is ski season, 100 km of pistes, modern resort (Pradollano at 2100m), reliable snow thanks to the altitude (north-facing). Particularity: you can ski in the morning and swim on the Costa del Sol in the afternoon (Granada-Malaga 1h30 by road). April to June and October-November are the off-seasons: skiing winding down (sometimes until late April), hiking not yet in shape. The Alpine Skiing World Cup returns regularly. Outside skiing: 3-5 day trek through the Alpujarras (south slope, Moorish villages of Pampaneira, Bubión, Capileira), Mulhacén ascent (15 km return from Hoya del Portillo, June-October). Try jamón de Trevélez (cured at altitude). Granada and Malaga are the logistics bases. Direct flight Granada or Malaga from Paris.
baqueira-beret:en: Baqueira Beret, the largest ski resort in Spain and the Spanish Pyrenees in the Aran Valley in Catalonia: 30 permanent inhabitants (10,000 in ski season). Favourite resort of the Spanish royal family = King Juan Carlos I then Felipe VI come to ski regularly at Baqueira since the 1980s = nicknamed "the royal resort" even if less exclusive than Swiss St Moritz. Aran Valley = Catalan enclave of Lerida province beyond the Bonaigua pass, the Aranese people ("aranés") speaking Occitan = unique Occitan-speaking enclave of Spain (co-official language with Catalan + Spanish since 2006). July is my favourite month (7.5/10): 9-22°C, 47% rain, ideal temperatures for Pyrenean hiking. Baqueira never tops 7.5/10 (Pyrenean alpine climate with a narrow season). From June to September, it's the summer season: 4-22°C, Pyrenees hiking, mountain bike, paragliding, wildlife observation (chamois, marmots, bearded vultures). From December to April, expect -7/3°C and 22-49% precipitation often as snow: this is ski high season (the resort benefits from abundant snow cover thanks to north exposure and high altitude). Pistes: 168 km Baqueira Beret-Bonaigua (one of the largest Pyrenean ski areas, in competition with Andorra's Grandvalira), 3 linked sectors: Baqueira, Beret (panoramic high plateau 1850m), Bonaigua (south slope 2200m), altitude 1500-2510m, vertical drop 1010m, 36 lifts. Signature pistes: Cap de Baqueira 2510m (panoramic summit), Stadium (main descent), Mirador (familiar red), Davall (panorama over Baqueira village). Off-piste: Baqueira has 80 km of off-piste accessible with short skin hikes, several couloirs respected by Iberian freeriders. Snowpark Beret = modern freestyle. Audience: Spaniards in the majority (Madrilenians-Barcelonans on weekends), south-western French (Toulouse 3h by car), many Britons thanks to charter flights Toulouse-Lourdes, few other Europeans. Strengths: prices much cheaper than Swiss-French Alps (passes 60-65€/day vs 70-90€ at Chamonix-Verbier), renowned Spanish catering ("après-ski tapas" institution), more festive Catalan atmosphere warmer than French alpine resorts. Aran Valley: preserved and traditional, preserved Romanesque villages Vielha Aranese capital, Salardú, Tredòs, traditional Pyrenean atmosphere with UNESCO Catalonia Romanesque churches. Excursions: Vielha 30 min away (Aranese capital, traditional atmosphere), Andorra Grandvalira 2h away (other large Pyrenean area), Toulouse 3h away (already covered by BDW), Lourdes 2h away, Barcelona 4h away. Try olla aranesa (Aranese signature pot stew), venison stew, Pyrenees trout, Catalan wines Priorat + Spanish cava, ratafia Catalan liqueur. Logistics: Baqueira accessible by car from Toulouse airport (TLS) in 3h via the Bielsa tunnel (the tunnel sometimes closes in winter, alternative Bonaigua pass), from Barcelona airport (BCN) in 5h, from Lourdes airport (LDE) in 2h.
valence:en: Valencia, Spain's 3rd city: 800,000 inhabitants on the Mediterranean coast. Birthplace of paella (the real one, with chicken/rabbit/green beans, never with seafood for orthodox Valencians), modern architectural capital (City of Arts and Sciences by Santiago Calatrava). Climate among Europe's best. June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 19-28°C, 11% rain, sea at 22°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (22-30°C, often 35°C+ with coastal humidity). September stays excellent (8.9/10). From November to March, expect 7-17°C and 12-19% rain: one of the mildest winter climates in continental Europe, plenty of sunshine, sea temperature 14-16°C, many European snowbirds. Highlights: City of Arts and Sciences (Ciudad de las Artes y las Ciencias, futuristic complex by Santiago Calatrava, a Valencian architect born here in 1951: L'Hemisfèric eye-shaped planetarium, Museu de les Ciències Príncipe Felipe science museum, L'Oceanogràfic = Europe's largest aquarium with 45,000 marine animals, Palau de les Arts Reina Sofía opera house, L'Umbracle covered garden), Old town: Cathedral (13th-15th century Gothic, houses the chalice considered the Holy Grail by the Vatican since 1437, one of the most contested artefacts of Christianity), Lonja de la Seda (UNESCO, Silk Exchange 15th-century flamboyant Gothic), Mercado Central (one of the largest fresh markets in Europe, modernist architecture 1928), Towers of Serrano and Quart (former medieval gates), Plaza del Ayuntamiento, El Carmen district (bohemian), Turia Gardens (riverbed diverted after the great flood of 1957, transformed into a 9 km park crossing the city). Malvarrosa Beach 4 km from the centre (urban), Albufera (saline lagoon 15 km south, place of origin of paella, boat trips). Try paella valenciana (the real one: chicken, rabbit, ferraure and garrofó beans, no seafood or chorizo), arroz a banda, fideuà (paella with pasta), horchata de chufa (local tiger nut orgeat), agua de Valencia (cocktail orange juice + cava + gin + vodka). Las Fallas in March (UNESCO fire festival, huge papier-mâché ninot statues burned on the night of 19 March). Valencia airport (VLC).
grenade:en: Granada is dominated by the Alhambra, Nasrid palace UNESCO-listed and one of the most visited monuments in the world. May is my favourite month (9.0/10): 12-25°C, 20% rain, Generalife gardens in full bloom. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to crushing heat (21-35°C) and the crowds: Alhambra ticket mandatory to book 3 months ahead (5000 visitors/day limit). From December to February, expect 3-16°C and 24% rain: mild during the day, cool in the evening, Sierra Nevada just 30 min by car for skiing (season November to April). Albaicín quarter (UNESCO, former Moorish quarter) for the view of the Alhambra from Mirador de San Nicolás at sunset, Sacromonte for the gypsy flamenco caves, Realejo for local life. Tradition of free tapas with each drink (rare in Spain). March-April and October are the best tourism/weather compromises. Cordoba 2h away, Seville 3h by train.
toscane:en: Tuscany is the image of classic Italy: hills with cypresses, vineyards, perched villages, Renaissance in Florence. June is my favourite month (9.2/10): 16-29°C, 25% rain, landscapes before the summer drought, crowds still reasonable. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to stifling heat (19-32°C, feels like 38°C+): Tuscans fleeing to the sea, suffocating interior. From December to February, expect 2-13°C and 36-42% rain: mild for visiting Florence, Siena, Pisa without crowds. September-October is my real sweet spot: 12-26°C, harvests (Chianti, Brunello di Montalcino, Vino Nobile di Montepulciano), white truffle in November. Highlights: Florence (UNESCO, already covered), Siena (Piazza del Campo, Palio on 2 July and 16 August), Pisa (Leaning Tower, Piazza dei Miracoli), San Gimignano (UNESCO, 14 medieval towers), Lucca (intact ramparts), Volterra (Etruscans), Val d'Orcia (UNESCO, Pienza, Montalcino, Montepulciano). Etruscan Coast (Castiglioncello, Bolgheri for Sassicaia). Try bistecca alla fiorentina, pici, ribollita, pappardelle al cinghiale, gelato. May and September-October are the real sweet spots.
lac-come:en: Lake Como, in an inverted-Y shape, 50 km long, 410m deep (one of the deepest in Europe). George Clooney and Hollywood villas (Bellagio especially). July-August are my favourite months (8.4/10): 21-28°C, 43-50% rain in afternoon storms, swimming in the lake (water at 24°C). From December to February, expect 4-11°C and 24-30% rain: often grey sky (mist over the lake), many villas and restaurants close. Highlights: Como (Duomo, funicular to Brunate for the view), Bellagio (pearl of the lake, gardens of Villa Melzi and Villa Serbelloni), Varenna (charming village, Villa Monastero), Tremezzo (Villa Carlotta, Italian gardens), Villa del Balbianello at Lenno (used in Star Wars Episode II and James Bond Casino Royale). Navigazione Lago di Como boats serving all the villages. Try risotto al pesce persico, missoltini (dried fish), polenta uncia, local cheeses (Bitto). Milan 1h by train, Switzerland (Lugano) 30 min by car. May-June and September-October are the best crowd/weather compromises.
pouilles:en: Puglia, the heel of the Italian boot: 800 km of coastline, ancient olive trees, trulli, baroque salentino. June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 19-28°C, 26% rain, sea at 23°C, crowds still reasonable. July-August stay excellent (8.9/10) but massive crowds, prices x3, Italians on holiday. From December to February, expect 4-14°C and 29-34% rain: mild for Italy, many hotels close, but this is the season for new olive oils. Highlights: Alberobello (UNESCO, 1500 trulli, dry-stone houses with conical roofs), Lecce ("Florence of the South", baroque salentino), Polignano a Mare (perched on a limestone cliff), Ostuni (the "white town"), Matera (just next door in Basilicata, see dedicated review), Bari (old town, San Nicola basilica, famous for Father Christmas), Otranto and Gallipoli (south Adriatic coast). Salento and its beaches (Pescoluse "Maldives of Salento", Punta Prosciutto). Try orecchiette with cime di rapa, focaccia barese, burrata (origin here in Andria), pasticciotto, Primitivo and Negroamaro wine. Airports: Bari and Brindisi, low-cost flights from Paris. May and September-October are the real sweet spots.
taormina:en: Taormina, smart resort in north-east Sicily, perched at 200m on the Mediterranean with Etna in the background. June is my favourite month (9.5/10): 20-27°C, 15% rain, sea at 23°C, ancient theatre still open for Taormina Arte (music-theatre-cinema festival June-August). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-31°C) and especially the extreme crowds: Corso Umberto I packed, hotel prices x4 (the White Lotus series Season 2 filmed at San Domenico Palace in 2022 caused prices to explode), 4-6 month bookings in high season. From December to February, expect 8-15°C and 27-32% rain: mild, many hotels open, more peaceful atmosphere. Highlights: Ancient theatre (3rd century BC, one of the most beautiful in the Greek world, view Etna+sea), Corso Umberto I (main pedestrian street), Piazza IX Aprile (panoramic terrace), Castelmola (perched village 30 min walk away), Isola Bella (beach with peninsula). Etna excursion 1h30 away. Mazzaro/Spisone beach by cable car from the centre. Try almond granita, Sicilian cannoli. Catania 1h away. May and September-October are the best crowd/weather compromises.
ubud:en: Ubud is the cultural heart of Bali (Indonesia): 80,000 inhabitants, 35 km north of Denpasar, in the terraced rice paddies, capital of yoga, wellness and Balinese Hindu spirituality. Popularised by Eat Pray Love with Julia Roberts in 2010. August is my favourite month (7.6/10): 21-27°C, 73% rain. Ubud stays wetter than the Balinese coasts (relative altitude 1500m, mountain). From May to October, it's the relative dry season: 49-81% rainfall. From November to March, it's the rainy season: 71-93% rainfall in violent afternoon showers (mornings often sunny). From April to June and September to October are the real sweet spots crowd/weather. Highlights: Tegalalang Rice Terraces (photogenic but touristy terraced paddies, Jatiluwih 1h away more local and UNESCO), Sacred Monkey Forest (700 macaques, watch your belongings), Saraswati Temple (lotus pond), Goa Gajah ("elephant cave"), Tirta Empul (sacred-springs temple, purification ritual), Mt. Batur (1717m volcano, night ascent for sunrise at the summit, 4h). Activities: Balinese cooking classes, yoga retreats (Yoga Barn, Radiantly Alive), traditional massages, rafting on Ayung River. Central market Ubud Art Market. Try nasi campur, babi guling (suckling pig), sate lilit, jamu (traditional herbal drink).
canggu:en: Canggu, trendy beach resort on the south-west coast of Bali in Indonesia: 20 km north-west of Kuta. Former fishing village amid the rice paddies, transformed into the "Brooklyn of Bali" since 2015 (rapid gentrification for digital nomads, surfers, French-American-Russian expats). Brunch cafés, beach clubs, yoga studios, surf beaches. July-August are my favourite months (8.0/10): 23-28°C, 60-61% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From May to October, it's the dry season: 23-30°C, sea at 27°C, this is the tourist high season, hotel prices x2-x3. From November to April, it's the rainy season: 75-94% rainfall, but stable temperatures (24-30°C), beach clubs open, surfers in "weekend warrior" mode. Highlights / Beaches: Echo Beach (Pantai Batu Mejan, point break ideal for intermediate-advanced surfers, 2 left and right reef breaks), Berawa Beach (volcanic black sand, beach clubs: Atlas, Finns, La Brisa), Batu Bolong Beach (black sand, beach clubs: La Brisa, The Lawn, Sand Bar, beginner surfers), Pererenan Beach (north, quieter, bohemian alternative Pererenan vs gentrified Canggu), Mengening Beach, Tanah Lot 15 min away by scooter (Hindu temple on a rocky islet in the sea, one of Bali's most famous temples, view at sunset, beware 17h extreme crowds). Activities: surf (Canggu's #1 attraction, schools everywhere, board rental 50,000 IDR/day ≈ 3€), yoga (Samadi Bali, The Practice, FlowState renowned studios), Balinese cooking classes, scooter (Canggu without a scooter = impossible, mediocre road condition, chaotic driving, helmet mandatory). Renowned beach clubs: Finns Beach Club (the largest, infinity pool facing the ocean, high prices but an experience), La Brisa (smart bohemian, driftwood structure), Atlas Beach Fest (the most recent, huge), Sand Bar, The Lawn. Trendy restaurants: Crate Café (brunch institution), Milk & Madu, Old Man's, La Cabina, Mason. Nightlife: Old Man's, Pretty Poison (skate ramp + bar), Vault Cinema (open air), Black Diamond. Audience: digital nomads in the majority (Canggu is one of the world capitals of digital nomadism with Bali Coworking Spaces: Tropical Nomad, Outpost, Dojo), surfers, long-term expats. Try nasi goreng, mie goreng, satay, gado-gado, babi guling (Balinese suckling pig), kopi luwak (civet coffee — ethically controversial). Logistics: Canggu 1h30 from Bali Ngurah Rai airport (DPS) by taxi (300,000 IDR ≈ 18€) or Grab (150,000 IDR ≈ 10€).
nusa-penida:en: Nusa Penida, volcanic island south-east of Bali in Indonesia: 200 km², 50,000 inhabitants. The largest of the 3 Nusa islands (with Lembongan and Ceningan). Recent tourist discovery (2017-2018 thanks to the explosion of Instagram = viral photos of Kelingking Beach in the shape of a Tyrannosaurus-Rex head). Spectacular cliffs, turquoise waters, preserved beaches. From July to August are my favourite months (8.4/10): 21-26°C, 42-44% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From May to October, it's the dry season: 22-27°C, sea at 26-27°C, this is the tourist high season, hotel prices x2-x3. From November to April, it's the rainy season: 72-94% rainfall, sometimes rough sea for the crossing from Bali, possible mists on the cliffs. Highlights: Kelingking Beach ("T-Rex Beach", Nusa Penida's signature visual, dinosaur-head-shaped cliff plunging into a white-sand beach 200m below, the most-visited photo-stop panorama, descent possible but steep 30 min via ropes and improvised ladders, dangerous swimming with currents), Angel's Billabong (natural pool in the rock, swimming at low tide only), Broken Beach (Pasih Uug, geological natural arch with turquoise sea in a round cavity), Crystal Bay (crescent-shaped bay, one of the most beautiful beaches, snorkelling with mola mola = seasonal sunfish July-October), Atuh Beach (east side, limestone cliffs, preserved beaches with "Diamond Beach" rocks), Diamond Beach (next to Atuh, recent access via a staircase built in 2018), Peguyangan Waterfall ("Blue Waterfall", 700 descending steps along a cliff to reach a sacred Hindu temple + seaside pools, with turquoise waters). Diving: Nusa Penida is one of Asia's major diving sites: manta rays at Manta Point year-round (gentle, 3-5m wingspan, visibility 10-25m), mola mola (giant sunfish 1-3m, seasonal July-October, Nusa Penida's signature for divers), Crystal Bay and Toyapakeh main sites. Typical visit: 1-day west tour (Kelingking + Angel's Billabong + Broken Beach + Crystal Bay) or east tour (Atuh + Diamond + Peguyangan), no time to do everything in 1 day so an overnight stay is recommended. Activities: snorkelling, diving, scooter hire (mediocre road condition, rough terrain, difficult driving), cliff swing (panoramic swings above the void). Logistics: fast ferry from Sanur Bali in 30 min (200,000-300,000 IDR ≈ 12-18€), often rough return in the afternoon.
gili:en: Gili Islands, archipelago of 3 islands off the coast of Lombok island in Indonesia (east of Bali): Gili Trawangan (the largest, festive), Gili Air (intermediate, bohemian atmosphere), Gili Meno (the smallest and quietest). 3000 permanent inhabitants in total, explosive footfall since 2010 (Gili Trawangan = former backpacker spot, now much smarter). No cars or scooters on any of the 3 islands (transport: on foot, by bicycle, by cidomo = horse-drawn cart). May is my favourite month (6.9/10): 25-30°C, 55% rain. The Gilis never top 6.9/10 (tropical climate with marked rainy season). From May to October, it's the dry season: 23-31°C, sea at 28°C, this is the tourist high season, hotel prices x2-x3. From November to April, it's the rainy season: 78-96% rainfall, sometimes rough sea for the crossing from Bali, but stable temperatures. Highlights / Activities: Gili Trawangan = the festive one ("Gili T" to regulars), 16 km tour on foot 2h, east-coast beaches more beautiful, nightlife (Sama-Sama Reggae Bar, Tirnanog Irish bar, fire shows every evening), beach clubs (Pearl Beach Lounge, La Moomba Beach Club), many diving centres. Gili Meno = the calm and romantic one ("Honeymoon Island", 5 km tour, 1h on foot), white-sand beaches, central salt lake, ideal for couples, little nightlife, underwater statue Nest (circle of 48 metal statues underwater, work by Jason deCaires Taylor 2016, accessible by snorkelling). Gili Air = the bohemian intermediate (7 km on foot 2h), between Gili T's energy and Meno's calm, smart-backpacker atmosphere. Diving and snorkelling: excellent around the 3 islands (visibility 20-30m, green turtles guaranteed, blacktip reef sharks, rays, clownfish, seasonal manta rays), renowned PADI centres (Manta Dive, Big Bubble), Open Water training 4-5 million rupiahs (250-300€). Snorkelling tour 3 islands by boat (meeting point Turtle Point Gili Meno = swimming with turtles guaranteed). Sunset on Gili Trawangan west side = institution with view of Mount Agung Bali on a clear day. Try nasi goreng, mie goreng, gado-gado, satay, fresh seafood BBQ, Bintang beer, jaffles (toasted filled sandwich). Logistics: fast ferry from Bali (Padang Bai or Serangan ports) in 1h30-2h30 (300,000-500,000 IDR ≈ 18-30€), from Lombok airport (LOP) in 1h taxi + 30-min ferry from Bangsal port.
komodo:en: Komodo, island in the Lesser Sunda Islands archipelago in Indonesia: 390 km², 2000 inhabitants, famous for its Komodo dragons (Varanus komodoensis = the largest lizard in the world, up to 3m long and 90 kg, endemic to the islands of Komodo, Rinca, Padar, west Flores). Komodo National Park = UNESCO 1991, one of the 7 New Natural Wonders of the World 2012. July is my favourite month (9.0/10): 25-29°C, 15% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From June to September, it's the dry season: 24-31°C, this is the tourist high season, the most golden landscapes (burnt savannah), the calmest sea. From December to March, it's the rainy season: 75-83% rainfall, sometimes rough sea, greener landscapes. Highlights / Main park islands: Komodo (the main island), Rinca (the other dragon island, smaller, more accessible from Labuan Bajo), Padar Island (island without dragons but with the most famous panorama: 3 differently coloured beaches — one pink, one white, one black — seen from the summit at 30 min walk, one of the most viral photos in Indonesia), Pink Beach (one of the only pink beaches in the world thanks to red coral fragments mixed with the white sand, snorkelling), Manta Point (snorkelling with manta rays, almost guaranteed), Kanawa, Kelor, Sebayur = snorkelling islands around Labuan Bajo. Visiting the dragons: guided tours mandatory on Komodo and Rinca (with rangers armed with forked sticks), dragons in near-total freedom, attacks on humans rare but real (1 death per year on average among the local villagers who live near the dragons), to observe but NEVER to approach within 10m. Diving: Komodo National Park is one of the world-renowned diving sites (top 5 globally according to several rankings), 1000 fish species, manta rays, white and black tip reef sharks, dolphins, whales, strong currents (reserved for advanced divers), cult sites: Batu Bolong, Crystal Rock, Castle Rock, Manta Point, Cauldron. Liveaboard 3-7 days = the ideal way to visit the park (30-300€/day depending on boat category, from Labuan Bajo). Try nasi goreng, ikan bakar (grilled fish), sambal terasi. Logistics: Labuan Bajo airport (LBJ) on Flores with direct flights from Bali Denpasar 1h, then private boat or 1-3 day tour for the park (150-300€/day for a private boat for 4-6 people).
ho-chi-minh:en: Ho Chi Minh City (former Saigon) is Vietnam's economic capital: 9 million inhabitants, tropical dynamism, French heritage. January-February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 23-34°C, 13-14% rain, this is the dry season, but HCMC never tops 7/10 (constant hot tropical climate). From May to November, it's the monsoon: 64-98% rainfall in afternoon tropical showers (often 30 min then clear sky). March-April = peak heat (25-35°C). Highlights: Cu Chi Tunnels 2h away (Viet Cong underground network, very narrow), War Remnants Museum (Museum of War Crimes), Notre-Dame Cathedral (bricks from Toulouse), Central Post Office (Eiffel), Reunification Palace. District 1 for the business centre, District 4 for the food streets, Cholon for Chinatown and the Binh Tay market. Try pho (different from Hanoi), banh xeo (crispy pancakes), com tam (broken rice), coffee with condensed milk. Mekong delta 2h away for the floating markets (Cai Be more local than Cai Rang).
baie-halong:en: The Halong Bay (UNESCO 1994) is the postcard image of Vietnam: 1969 emerging karst limestone islands over 1500 km² in the Gulf of Tonkin. November is my favourite month (8.6/10): 20-26°C, 25% rain, clear skies after the monsoon, mild temperatures. From October to March, it's the high season: 16-28°C, 15-38% rain, some days can be misty (reduced visibility, especially January-February, but also atmospheric for photography). From May to August, it's typhoon and monsoon season: 63-87% rainfall, hot temperatures (25-31°C), real risk of storms (cruises cancelled for several days). From April to May, expect 22-29°C: humid transition. Cruises: 1 night (2 days) = standard "classic" circuit, you see the essentials but it's short (50-150€/person), 2 nights (3 days) = circuit recommended to truly enjoy it (less-frequented Bai Tu Long Bay, kayak, caves, night fishing, 200-500€), 3 nights = for enthusiasts. Cat Ba Island to explore for an extra 2-3 days (national park, hikes). Highlights: Sung Sot Cave ("cave of surprises"), Ti Top Island (panorama), Lan Ha Bay (less-frequented alternative to the east, near Cat Ba), Bai Tu Long Bay (north-east, even quieter, premium cruises).
