Highlights: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding (15 km north of Chengdu, the largest in the world, 80 adult pandas + babies, visit early morning 7:30-9am when pandas eat and are active, 55 yuan), Old Jinli Street (reconstituted traditional commercial street, street food, crafts), Wenshu Monastery (the largest Buddhist monastery in Chengdu, famous tearoom), Du Fu Thatched Cottage (memorial to the great Tang poet Du Fu who lived here for 4 years in exile 759-763), Wuhou Shrine (memorial to Zhuge Liang strategist of the Three Kingdoms), Kuanzhai Quarter (Wide and Narrow Alleys, Qing-era traditional alleys converted into restaurants-bars). Excursions: Mount Emei (UNESCO) 2h30 away (3099m, one of the 4 sacred Chinese Buddhist mountains, 2-day hike), Giant Buddha of Leshan (UNESCO, 71m, the largest stone Buddha in the world, sculpted in 713 in the cliff at the confluence of 3 rivers, view by boat for the perspective), Jiuzhaigou (UNESCO) 1h by plane (valley of the 9 Tibetan villages, multi-coloured turquoise lakes). Try Sichuanese hot pot (huo guo, the spiciest in China), mapo tofu, kung pao chicken (all originating from Sichuan), dan dan noodles.
guilin:en: Guilin, city in Guangxi province in China: 760,000 inhabitants, unique karst landscapes (limestone peaks rising from the Li river), considered among the most beautiful regions of China by the ancients ("Guilin shanshui jia tianxia" = "Guilin landscapes are the most beautiful in the world"). October is my favourite month (8.6/10): 18-26°C, 25% rain, ideal temperatures before winter. From September to November, it's the tourist high season: 13-31°C, ideal dry climate, orange grove harvests. From June to August, expect 24-32°C with 62-84% rain: Chinese summer high season (maximum crowds, prices x2-x3, mountain trails stormed), but also the greenest and mistiest landscapes. From December to February, expect 6-16°C and 28-55% rain: humid cold, few tourists but mystical landscapes with low mists. Highlights: Li River cruise (Lijiang, from Guilin to Yangshuo 1h by bus then 4-5h cruise, 80 km of signature karst landscapes of traditional Chinese landscape — these are the landscapes that feature on the 20-yuan banknotes), Yangshuo (tourist village to the south, cycling in the rice paddies, climbing on the karst peaks, light show on the Li river with 600 figurants choreographed by Zhang Yimou), Longji Rice Terraces 2h away ("Dragon's Backbone", 5-level terraced rice paddies in 1000m mountains, spectacular landscapes in May-June green growth or October golden harvest, Yao/Zhuang minority villages), Reed Flute Cave (limestone cave neon-coloured in Guilin), Elephant Trunk Hill (signature collised hill on the riverbank in Guilin). Try Guilin rice noodles (mi fen), beer fish (river fish in beer, Yangshuo specialty), osmanthus tea/cake (osmanthus flowers = symbol of Guilin).
zhangjiajie:en: Zhangjiajie, national park in China in Hunan province: city of 1.5 million inhabitants at the foot of the park, famous for its 3000 quartz-sandstone karst peaks from 200 to 800m high, landscapes that inspired James Cameron for the floating mountains of the Avatar film 2009 (the Hallelujah Mountain peak was officially renamed after the film's success). September is my favourite month (8.2/10): 21-29°C, 35% rain, ideal temperatures and characteristic mystical mists. From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 14-33°C, but also extreme crowds (one of the most touristy sites in China, 30 million visitors/year mainly Chinese, Bailong elevator and cable car often congested with 2-3h waits). From June to August, expect 22-33°C with 48-58% rain: stifling heat at the bottom, cooler at altitude. From December to February, expect 3-12°C: winter cold, trails sometimes closed for snow, but spectacular landscapes under mist. Highlights: Wulingyuan Scenic Area (UNESCO 1992, Zhangjiajie national park + Suoxi Valley + Tianzi Mountain), Bailong Elevator ("Hundred Dragons Elevator", the tallest outdoor elevator in the world 326m, 3 parallel cabins, 2 min ascent, vertiginous experience), Zhangjiajie Glass Bridge (Grand Canyon Bridge, 430m long, 300m above the ground, the longest glass bridge in the world 2016), Tianmen Mountain (Heaven's Gate, cable car 7.5 km the longest in the world, staircase 999 steps to the natural cavity Tianmen Cave 131m high), Yellow Stone Village (Huangshi Village), Golden Whip Stream. Logistics: park entry 230 yuan/4 days (several entrances Wulingyuan/Forest Park/Yangjiajie), plan 3-4 days minimum to fully enjoy. Direct flight Zhangjiajie from Shanghai/Beijing/Chengdu in 2-3h.
lijiang:en: Lijiang, UNESCO town in Yunnan province in south-western China: 1.3 million inhabitants at 2400m altitude at the foot of the Jade Dragon Snow Mountain (5596m), cradle of the Naxi culture (ethnic minority with Dongba pictographic writing = the only living pictographic writing in the world today, still used by Dongba shamans). April is my favourite month (8.1/10): 7-21°C, 33% rain, ideal temperatures at altitude, clear sky to see the Jade Dragon. From March to May and October-November are the best seasons: 3-21°C, dry and sunny climate. From June to September, it's the monsoon: 75-84% rainfall, green landscapes but frequent mists hiding the mountain. From December to February, expect -1/15°C: dry cold, sunny, snow possible on the surrounding summits, far fewer tourists. Highlights: Old Town of Lijiang (UNESCO 1997, maze of paved alleys with canals, traditional Naxi houses in carved wood, unique preserved atmosphere despite mass tourism — since 2000 Lijiang has become one of the most popular Chinese tourist destinations, sometimes congested), Black Dragon Pool (Heilongtan, public park to the north with pond offering the signature view of the Jade Dragon Snow Mountain reflected in the water and Wufeng pavilion), Mu Family Mansion (residence of the hereditary Naxi chiefs from the 14th to the 18th century), Jade Dragon Snow Mountain (cable car to 4506m glacier, oxygen at 4000m+, to buy in a bottle for those sensitive to altitude). Excursions: Tiger Leaping Gorge 2h away (one of the deepest gorges in the world, 3790m difference between the Yangtze river and the Haba summit 5396m, 2-day hike), Shangri-La 4h away (formerly Zhongdian, renamed in 2001 for tourism, Songzanlin Tibetan monastery, altitude 3200m), First Bend of the Yangtze at Shigu, Lake Lugu 6h away (matriarchal Mosuo people). Try Naxi cuisine (yak butter tea, baba grilled, hot pot mushroom).
hangzhou:en: Hangzhou, capital of Zhejiang province in China: 12 million inhabitants 200 km south-west of Shanghai, former capital of the Southern Song dynasty (1127-1279), nicknamed "paradise on earth" by Marco Polo who visited it in the 13th century ("the largest and noblest city in the world"). World headquarters of Alibaba (Jack Ma founded the company here in 1999). September is my favourite month (7.9/10): 22-29°C, 47% rain, ideal temperatures after the heatwave. From September to November, it's the tourist high season: 10-29°C, fragrant osmanthus flowers in September, red maples in November. From April to May, expect 13-26°C: spring with new Longjing tea leaves (the spring harvest before the "qing ming" festival 5 April gives the most prestigious and expensive tea). From June to August, expect 22-34°C with 49-62% rain: stifling heat typical of the delta. From December to February, expect 2-12°C and 23-39% rain: humid cold. Highlights: West Lake (Xi Hu, UNESCO, one of the most famous natural landscapes in China, model for traditional Chinese landscape paintings, walk around 15 km or by dragon boat 2h, the most beautiful views: Leifeng Pagoda to the south, Su Causeway, Bai Causeway, 3 Pools Mirroring the Moon), Lingyin Temple ("Temple of the Soul's Retreat", one of the largest Chan/Zen monasteries in southern China, founded in 326), Six Harmonies Pagoda (Liuhe Ta, on the banks of the Qiantang river, view of the Qiantang Bore famous tidal wave phenomenon mid-September), Hefang Street (restored traditional pedestrian street, shops and street food), Longjing tea plantations ("Dragon Well", the most prestigious Chinese green tea, to visit in the Meijiawu or Longjing villages 30 min away). National Tea Museum. Try dongpo pork (sweet-savoury braised pork, Hangzhou dish invented by the poet Su Dongpo 11th century), beggar's chicken (chicken wrapped in lotus leaves and clay), Longjing tea.
suzhou:en: Suzhou, 100 km west of Shanghai in Jiangsu province: 6 million inhabitants, nicknamed the "Venice of the East" for its canals dating from the Tang-Song dynasties (6th-10th centuries). UNESCO 1997 for its classical Chinese gardens (Suzhou is the cradle of the art of Chinese gardens). September is my favourite month (8.0/10): 22-28°C, 44% rain, ideal temperatures after the summer heatwave. From September to November, it's the tourist high season: 10-28°C, garden maple foliage in November. From April to May, expect 12-26°C: spring with cherry blossoms. From June to August, expect 21-34°C with 46-54% rain: stifling heat typical of the Yangtze delta, oppressive humidity. From December to February, expect 1-11°C and 20-30% rain: humid cold, few tourists. Highlights: Classical Gardens of Suzhou (UNESCO, 9 listed gardens, to see in priority: Humble Administrator's Garden Zhuozheng Yuan the largest 5 hectares 16th century, Lingering Garden Liu Yuan calligraphies, Master of the Nets Garden Wangshi Yuan the most intimate to see illuminated in the evening, Lion Grove Garden Shizilin with its limestone rocks in lion shapes), Pingjiang Road (historic pedestrian street along a canal, ancient atmosphere, tearooms, traditional shops), Tiger Hill (Huqiu, hill with 11th-century leaning pagoda that leans even more than the Tower of Pisa), Hanshan Temple (made famous by the Tang poem "Mooring at night at Maple Bridge" by Zhang Ji, ancestral bell struck 108 times for the New Year), Suzhou Museum (architecture I.M. Pei born in China 2006), Suzhou Silk Museum (Suzhou is the historic capital of Chinese silk for 4000 years). Excursions: Tongli, Zhouzhuang, Wuzhen (water towns 1-2h away, ancestral water villages). Try Suzhou-style noodles, songshu yu (squirrel fish sweet-sour), Jiangsu cuisine. 30 min by fast train from Shanghai, ideal day trip.
grindelwald:en: Grindelwald, Swiss village resort at the foot of the Eiger (3970m, famous 1800m-high north face, one of the great alpine walls with the Matterhorn and Grandes Jorasses): 4000 inhabitants at 1000m in the canton of Bern (Bernese Oberland). August is my favourite month (7.8/10): 13-23°C, 59% rain in storms, alpine hiking, unique panorama of Eiger-Mönch-Jungfrau (Big Three of the Bernese Alps). From June to September, expect 6-23°C: very active summer season (climbing, hiking, paragliding, photogenic Bachalpsee altitude lake). From December to March, expect -1/8°C and 47-55% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 100 km Jungfrau Ski Region (Grindelwald + Wengen + Mürren, 1 single ski pass), accessible by train (Wengernalpbahn, one of the rare car-free resorts for Wengen). Signature pistes: Lauberhorn at neighbouring Wengen (mid-January World Cup descent, one of the longest on the circuit at 4.5 km). Eiger Express (cable car inaugurated 2020, 15 min to Eigergletscher 2300m, access to Jungfraujoch 3454m "Top of Europe" via railway tunnel dating from 1912). First (cable car, Bachalpsee hike, First Cliff Walk, zipline). Audience: very many British historically (Grindelwald was "discovered" by the British in the 19th century), Asians on day visits to Jungfraujoch. Hotels: Belvedere Swiss Quality, Sunstar Alpine, Hotel Spinne, 150-800€/night. Try rösti, raclette, fondue, Älplermagronen. Logistics: Grindelwald 1h30 from Bern by train via Interlaken, 3h from Zurich.
saas-fee:en: Saas-Fee is the Swiss "village of glaciers" in the Valais: 1800m altitude, surrounded by 13 summits over 4000m (Dom, Allalinhorn, Täschhorn). Car-free village (mandatory parking at the entrance). July is my favourite month (7.6/10): 9-19°C, 54% rain, good for hiking towards the Mittelallalin (3500m, accessible by funicular) and the rotating Allalin restaurant. From December to April, expect -9/2°C: ski season. Permanent summer skiing on the Fee glacier (one of the rare in the world). From November and May, it's the shoulder season. Traditional chalet architecture, unlike the neighbouring concrete French resorts. Population: 1700 permanent residents. Access by train from Brig (1h by bus) or car via the St Bernard tunnel from Italy. High Swiss prices (250-500 CHF/night for a 4-star, day pass 80-90 CHF).
laax:en: Laax, ski resort in the Grisons Alps in the Vorderrhein valley in Switzerland: 1900 inhabitants at 1100m altitude. Capital of snowboard and freestyle in Switzerland, one of the most avant-garde European resorts for snow culture (the Crap Sogn Gion park has the largest half-pipe in the world 200m long 22m wide). Linked area Flims-Laax-Falera = 224 km of pistes 28 lifts, one of the largest in Romansh-Grisons Switzerland. August is my favourite month (7.7/10): 9-21°C, 50% rain, ideal temperatures for alpine hiking. Laax never tops 7.7/10 (alpine climate with narrow season). From June to September, it's the summer high season: 3-21°C, Grisons hiking, mountain bike (Laax = renowned summer bike park), kayaking, paragliding, Lake Cauma alpine turquoise Caumasee accessible by funicular = signature summer visual, swimming in emerald water 18-22°C in midsummer. From December to March, expect -8/0°C and 37-65% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 224 km Flims-Laax-Falera, altitude 1100-3018m (Vorab Glacier), vertical drop 1918m, 28 lifts including modern 3S Vorab gondolas. Signature pistes: FIS Pista da Vauz (occasional World Cup descent), Vorab Glacier (3018m, summer skiing possible some years). Snowpark Laax = considered one of the top 5 in the world with 90 obstacles + the largest natural half-pipe in the world, host of the Burton European Open = signature snowboard competition, freestyle world cup stage, attracts international pros. Off-piste: 3 legally groomed off-piste routes ("freeride routes" marked secured) = unique practice in Switzerland with Verbier, accessible to intermediate-advanced skiers. The Roundshot = panoramic 360° rotating restaurant at 2228m, signature visual of Laax. Strengths: Riders Hotel (design hotel conceived by snowboarders, hostel-chic atmosphere, 50€/night dorms accessible to young-budget clientele = unique in expensive Swiss resort), Galaaxy apple-educative-cultural. Audience: Swiss Germans in the majority (Zurich 1h30 by car), Germans, many British, international snowboarders, younger and cooler atmosphere than Verbier or Saint Moritz. Hotels: Rocksresort (5* modern Domenig design 2009), Riders Hotel, prices 150-700€/night. Excursions: Saint Moritz 2h away (already covered by BDW), Davos 1h30 away (annual economic forum + skiing), Chur 30 min away (the oldest town in Switzerland 5000 years), Glacier Express Saint Moritz-Zermatt crosses neighbouring Laax. Try capuns (signature Grisons ravioli), pizokels (Grisons gnocchi), Bündner fleisch (IGP dried meat), nusstorte walnut cake, Veltliner red wine. Logistics: Laax 1h30 from Zurich airport (ZRH) by car/train, direct access without painful transfers.
engelberg:en: Engelberg, Swiss ski resort in the canton of Obwald in central Switzerland: 4400 inhabitants at 1000m altitude at the foot of Mount Titlis (3239m). 1h from Lucerne and 1h30 from Zurich = the most accessible ski resort from the Swiss capitals for weekends. July-August are my favourite months (7.3-7.4/10): 13-21°C, 70-78% rain, ideal temperatures for alpine hiking. From June to September, it's the summer high season: 6-21°C, hiking, mountain bike, paragliding. From December to March, expect -4/6°C and 47-78% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 82 km Engelberg-Titlis (modest vs 600 km Portes du Soleil or 3 Valleys, but excellent snow quality thanks to altitude and the Titlis glacier which guarantees snow year-round), 2 sides: Brunni north side (family, sunny in the afternoon) and Titlis-Jochpass south side (more demanding, access to the 3000m glacier). Legendary pistes: Laub (legendary off-piste descent 1300m vertical between Stand 2400m and Engelberg 1050m, one of the longest groomed off-pistes in Europe, attracts world freeriders), Steinberg (glacial descent), Jochpass-Engstlenalp (long 7 km family piste). Off-piste: Galtiberg, Sulz, Steinberg back, accessible by cable car from Stand 2400m, freeriders from around the world come to Engelberg for the famous "Engelberg Powder", one of the deepest powders in Europe in good season (1m+ after snowfalls). Mount Titlis (cable car "Titlis Rotair" from Engelberg-Trubsee-Stand-Titlis 3020m summit, 30 min journey, the cable car of the last section Stand-Titlis rotates 360° in 5 min = unique in the world, panorama over 100 alpine summits, panoramic restaurant at the summit, Cliff Walk = suspension bridge 100m above the void, Glacier Cave = cave sculpted in the glacier, Ice Flyer = chairlift over the glacier). Excursions: Lucerne 1h by train (already covered by BDW), Mount Pilatus (other panoramic summit near Lucerne with the steepest cogwheel train in the world 48%), Zurich 1h30 away, Mount Rigi. Audience: Swiss Germans in the majority, many British (Engelberg = one of the British favourite resorts historically), Asians in groups via Titlis cable car (Engelberg is one of the alpine resorts most visited by Asian Chinese tourists, especially in summer for the Cliff Walk photo), few French. Hotels: Kempinski Palace Engelberg (5*+ luxury opened 2021), Hotel Bellevue Terminus (4*), Hotel Schweizerhof, 200-1500€/night. Try rösti, fondue moitié-moitié, raclette, Älplermagronen (potato pasta gratin), bündner fleisch (dried meat). Logistics: Engelberg accessible by direct train from Lucerne 1h (Zentralbahn picturesque line), 1h30 from Zurich airport.
wengen:en: Wengen, Swiss village ski resort without cars (accessible only by Wengernalpbahn 1893 cogwheel train from Lauterbrunnen): 1300 inhabitants at 1274m, spectacular view of the Jungfrau (4158m). August is my favourite month (7.6/10): 12-20°C, 51% rain in altitude storms, alpine hiking, views from the Mannlichen platform 2230m. From June to September, expect 5-20°C: active summer season. From December to March, expect -4/6°C and 47-53% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 100 km Jungfrau Ski Region (Wengen + Grindelwald + Mürren, 1 single ski pass), altitude 944-2486m, vertical drop 1500m. Signature pistes: Lauberhorn = the longest World Cup descent piste in the world (4.5 km, vertical drop 1028m, max speed 160 km/h on the Hundschopf jump, race mid-January weekend since 1930), Männlichen-Kleine Scheidegg (easy and photogenic descent with Eiger in the background). Off-piste Tschuggen, Innerwengen. Audience: British traditionally very present (Downhill Only Club founded in 1925 by 12 British), Swiss Germans, family and quiet atmosphere. Hotels: Beausite Park (4*), Hotel Regina (4*), Hotel Belvedere, Eiger Selfness, 150-700€/night. No motor traffic in the village = unique atmosphere, luggage transport by sledge. Try rösti, fondue, Älplermagronen, Engadiner Nusstorte (walnut cake). Logistics: Wengen 30 min from Interlaken by train (Lauterbrunnen + Wengernalpbahn), no road possible by car to the village.
lac-bled:en: Lake Bled, alpine glacial lake in Slovenia in the Julian Alps: 2.1 km², at 500m altitude at the foot of the Triglav (2864m, highest point of Slovenia). The signature postcard image of Slovenia: white baroque church on rocky islet in the middle of the lake with karst cliffs in the background and medieval Bled Castle perched at 130m on a dominant cliff. July-August are my favourite months (8.3-8.4/10): 14-25°C, 40-49% rain, ideal temperatures for swimming in the lake (water temperature 22-24°C in summer). June stays excellent (8.3/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 8-25°C, large crowds (Italians in the majority thanks to proximity, Germans, British, Asians, hotel prices x2-x3). From November to March, expect -2/9°C and 27-41% rain: alpine winter cold, lake partially frozen December-February some years, unique atmosphere under snow. Highlights: Bled Island (Blejski Otok, the only natural Slovenian islet, 145m × 90m, accessible only by pletna = traditional flat-bottomed boat manoeuvred by oar standing by a single oarsman since the Middle Ages, family monopoly of 23 families since 1740 = one of the oldest usage rights in the world, 25-min crossing one way, 18€ per person round trip with 30 min on site), Church of the Assumption on the island (17th-century baroque, 99 stone steps from the dock, Slovenian wedding traditions: the groom must carry his wife on the 99 steps without stopping for good luck, magnificent baroque interior, wishing bell = ring 3 times grants a wish according to legend), Bled Castle (Blejski Grad, the oldest castle in Slovenia, first mentions 1011, perched at 130m on rocky cliff above the lake, signature panorama over the lake and the island, historical museum, gastronomic restaurant with panoramic terrace), Path around the lake (Bled Promenade, 6 km on foot or rented bike 5€/day, one of the most picturesque walks in Central Europe, possibility of swimming at the various public beaches), Mala Osojnica (observation point 100m above the lake, 30-min steep uphill hike, signature panoramic photo over the lake with island in the centre = the most viral image of Instagram-worthy Europe, ideal sunrise 5am), Velika Osojnica (higher variant), Vintgar Gorge 4 km away (spectacular gorges of the Radovna river, path on wooden walkways 1.6 km above the turquoise water, open April-November, one of the natural wonders of Slovenia). Essential excursions: Lake Bohinj 30 min away (less touristy cousin of Lake Bled, larger 4.2 km², wilder, free swimming, hike Mt Vogel cable car 1922m), Triglav National Park (the only Slovenian national park, 838 km², demanding 2-3 day hike Mt Triglav 2864m in refuge), Ljubljana 50 min away (charming Slovenian capital), Postojna Cave 1h away (one of the largest tourist caves in Europe, underground train since 1872), Skocjan Caves 1h30 away (UNESCO, more impressive than Postojna), Piran 1h30 away (Adriatic Venetian port). Try Bled cream cake (Kremšnita, signature Bled vanilla cream puff cake since 1953, created by Park Hotel chef), grilled lake trout, jota (Slovenian sauerkraut bean soup), local Stelvio cheese. Logistics: Lake Bled 50 min from Ljubljana by car/bus, 1h from Ljubljana airport (LJU).
piran:en: Piran, port town of Slovenia on the Adriatic coast: 4000 inhabitants at the extreme south-west of the country (Slovenia has only 47 km of coast). Former Venetian possession 1283-1797, preserved Venetian atmosphere with near-intact 13th-18th century architecture ("Slovenian pearl of the Adriatic"), nicknamed "little Venice" despite its modest size. Birthplace of the baroque composer Giuseppe Tartini (1692-1770, virtuoso Devil's Trill Sonata). July-August are my favourite months (9.1-9.2/10): 19-28°C, 22-27% rain, sea at 24°C, ideal temperatures. June stays excellent (9.1/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 14-28°C, large crowds (Slovenes and Croats on weekend, Italians thanks to Trieste proximity 30 min, Germans, Austrians, hotel prices x2-x3). From November to March, expect 3-15°C and 30-44% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many establishments close, unique off-season atmosphere. Highlights: Tartini Square (Tartinijev Trg, marble main square dedicated to Giuseppe Tartini with his statue 1896, Venetian atmosphere with 19th-century town hall and 18th-century Tartini palace), Saint George's Cathedral (Sv Jurij, 14th-17th century Venetian baroque perched on 70m hill, signature panorama over Piran and the Adriatic at sunset), Punta of Piran (extreme tip of the peninsula where the sea meets the sea = signature visual of the town, lighthouse and church Saint Mary of Salute 1637), 1st of May Square (Trg 1 Maja, former main medieval square before Tartini replaced it, 18th-century cistern in the centre), Venetian House (Casa Veneziana, 14th-century medieval house with inscription "LASA PUR DIR" = "let them speak" engraved by a Venetian to his mistress to silence the rumours), Marine Museum (Sergej Mašera Pomorski Muzej, 17th-century palace, maritime history of Piran), Medieval ramparts (30 min walk from the centre, 360° panorama over Piran and the bay), Piran beaches (essentially rocky with concrete ramps, no sand as in most of the northern Adriatic, but crystalline water). Excursions: Sečovlje Salt Pans 8 km away (nature park traditional salt pans still in operation since the 13th-century Middle Ages, "Piran salt" harvest technique preserved by craftsmen, therapeutic spa with local brines Lepa Vida), Portoroz 4 km away ("Port of Roses", more lively modern beach resort, casinos, equipped beaches), Strunjan Nature Reserve (the highest flysch cliffs of the Adriatic 80m), Trieste Italy 30 min away (already covered by BDW), Croatian Istrian coast Pula/Rovinj 1-2h away, Postojna Cave 1h away, Lipica 30 min away (Lipizzan white horse stud farm since 1580), Ljubljana 1h30 away. Try Adriatic seafood (Piran is one of the rare Slovenian towns for traditional seafood), brodet (Croatian-Slovenian fish stew), Piran olive oil (the best in Slovenia), Piran salt (gourmet gifts), Refošk (local Istrian red wine), Malvazija (white wine). Logistics: Piran 1h30 from Ljubljana by car, 30 min from Trieste, 2h30 from Venice.
kotor:en: Kotor, fortified town of Montenegro: 14,000 inhabitants at the bottom of the Bay of Kotor (UNESCO 1979), the southernmost Mediterranean fjord in Europe. Comparable to Dubrovnik but less frequented and cheaper. June is my favourite month (9.1/10): 20-27°C, 27% rain, sea at 23°C, golden light on the ramparts. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-30°C) and the cruise crowds (the bay receives 4-6 liners per day, town congested 10am-5pm). From November to March, expect 6-15°C and 34-48% rain: mild but humid, many hotels close. From April-May and September-October are the best compromises. Highlights: Fortified old town (UNESCO, maze of medieval alleys, Saint Tryphon Cathedral 12th century), Saint John's Fortress (1350 steps on the cliff for 280m vertical drop, exceptional panoramic view over the bay, climb early morning before the heat, 8€), Arms Square, Clock Tower. Bay of Kotor excursions: Perast (Venetian baroque town, 12 17th-century palaces), Our Lady of the Rocks (artificial island created by fishermen since 1452, accessible by boat), Risan (Roman mosaics), Boka Bay tour by boat (3-4h, swimming in the blue cave). Montenegro excursions: Lovćen National Park (mausoleum of Petar II Petrović-Njegoš at the summit, 461 steps), Budva 30 min away (beach resort), Sveti Stefan (5* island-hotel, signature photo). Try njeguški pršut (smoked ham), kačamak, Montenegrin raki. Nearest airport: Tivat (10 min) or Podgorica (1h30).
dubrovnik:en: Dubrovnik, the "Pearl of the Adriatic": 40,000 inhabitants, UNESCO fortified town, 1900 km of preserved ramparts. Popularised by Game of Thrones (King's Landing filmed here since 2011, tourist multiplication by 5). June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 19-28°C, 24% rain, sea at 23°C, ideal temperatures before the stifling heat. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (21-30°C) and especially the extreme crowds: 1.4 million tourists/year, 4-6 cruise liners per day disembarking 10,000 visitors simultaneously, congested fortified town, prices x4-x5. The town hall now limits to 4000 simultaneous visitors in the old town (counters at the gates). From November to March, expect 3-15°C and 31-51% rain: mild but many hotels and restaurants close. Highlights: Stradun (main artery in marble polished by footsteps), Ramparts (2 km tour on foot, 35€ entry, take early 8am or late 5pm to avoid crowds and heat), Rector's Palace, Cathedral, Mount Srđ (cable car for panoramic view at sunset), Lokrum Island by ferry 15 min (nature park, Iron Throne for GoT fans). Excursions: Kotor (Montenegro) 2h away (UNESCO, spectacular bay), Elaphite Islands (Lopud, Šipan), Mostar (Bosnia, 3h away, Stari Most), Korčula and Hvar by ferry. Try peka (meat/octopus cooked under bell), pasticada (beef in sauce), rožata (local flan), Dingač wine (local red).
zadar:en: Zadar, town in Croatia in northern Dalmatia: 75,000 inhabitants on the Adriatic coast. Often overlooked in favour of Split or Dubrovnik, but both more traditional and strategically placed to visit Plitvice National Park (1h30) and the Kornati Islands (140-island rocky archipelago). June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 19-27°C, 18% rain, sea at 22°C, golden light on the Riva. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (21-30°C) and the summer crowds (Italians and Germans in the majority, hotel prices x3). From November to March, expect 3-13°C and 30-45% rain: mild for Croatia, many establishments close off-season. Highlights: Old town on peninsula, rich Roman heritage (1st-century Roman Forum, the largest in the Balkans) and Venetian (Clock Tower, Land Gate), Saint Anastasia Cathedral (13th century), Saint Donatus Church (9th-century pre-Romanesque, one of the oldest in Croatia), Sea Organ ("Sea Organ", 2005 acoustic installation by architect Nikola Bašić: 35 tubes under the promenade emit sounds under the effect of the waves, to listen to at sunset), Greeting to the Sun (next to the Sea Organ, 22m disc of solar panels that lights up at night), Riva (seaside promenade, one of the most beautiful sunsets in the world according to Alfred Hitchcock who stayed here in the 60s). Excursions: Plitvice National Park 1h30 away, Krka National Park 1h away (waterfalls, swimming permitted), Kornati Islands by boat (140 islands in a marine national park). Try pasticada (Croatian-style beef), peka, Skradin wines. Direct airport from Paris (Ryanair).
hvar:en: Hvar, smart and festive Croatian island: 70 km long, 11,000 inhabitants, lavender and rosemary, the most stable sun of the Adriatic (2724h/year, record). June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 21-28°C, 15% rain, sea at 23°C, lavender in full bloom (June-July). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-31°C) and the extreme crowds: Hvar Town has become since 2010 a party destination (Carpe Diem, Hula Hula, Falko), hotel prices x4-x5, harbour congested with yachts (among the smartest in Europe with Saint-Tropez and Mykonos). From November to March, expect 7-15°C and 34-43% rain: 80% of hotels and restaurants close, reduced ferries. Highlights: Hvar Town (Fortica citadel at the summit, Saint Stephen Cathedral, main square Pjaca — the largest in Dalmatia), Stari Grad (UNESCO, Stari Grad Plain, ancient agricultural landscapes preserved since the Greeks), Jelsa and Vrboska ("Little Venice", canals), lavender fields around Velo Grablje (June-July). Pakleni Islands by water-taxi 15 min (Sveti Klement, Marinkovac, Jerolim with Stipanska beach: trendy Carpe Diem Beach restaurant). Beaches: Dubovica (south, splendid little cove), Pokonji Dol, Mlini. Brač by neighbouring ferry (Zlatni Rat beach, famous horseshoe that changes shape with the currents). Try peka (under bell), gregada (fish with potatoes), pršut (Dalmatian ham), Plavac Mali wine. Nearest airport: Split (1h by ferry) or Brač (summer). May and September-October = best compromises.
plitvice:en: Plitvice National Park, in Croatia: 295 km², UNESCO listed since 1979, one of the most beautiful natural parks in Europe. 16 cascading lakes connected by waterfalls (90 falls total), extraordinary turquoise colour due to limestone and mineral deposits. July-August are my favourite months (8.7-8.8/10): 16-26°C, 30-34% rain, ideal temperatures for the wooden paths (15 km total, several A to K circuits from 2h to 8h). From June to September, it's the high season: 12-26°C, but also extreme crowds: Plitvice receives 1.8 million visitors/year, full car park by mid-morning, congested paths (particularly around Veliki Slap = the great waterfall 78m). Online ticket booking mandatory since 2020 with timed slots. From November to March, expect 0-10°C and 33-48% rain or snow: Plitvice under snow is spectacular (waterfalls partially frozen, deserted paths), prices divided by 2, some paths closed for safety. Highlights: Veliki Slap (78m, highest waterfall in Croatia), Lower Lakes (Donji Jezera, spectacular limestone deposits, access by the most photogenic path), Upper Lakes (Gornji Jezera, the largest), Electric boats on Lake Kozjak (the only authorised boats, free with entry ticket). Wooden paths entirely on stilts above the water (sensation of walking on water). Logistics: entry 35€ (high season) or 10€ (low season). 2-3h minimum for the short circuit, 6-8h for the full circuit (A,B,C,E,F,K). Hotels Jezero/Plitvice/Bellevue in the park (booking 6 months ahead). 2h by car from Zagreb, 2h30 from Zadar, 3h30 from Dubrovnik. Krka National Park (alternative or complement 2h away, swimming permitted at Skradinski Buk).
budva:en: Budva, Montenegrin beach resort: 14,000 inhabitants on the Adriatic coast, nicknamed the "Montenegrin Saint-Tropez" for its party-smart atmosphere, 30 min from Kotor. June is my favourite month (9.1/10): 21-27°C, 29% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-30°C) and the extreme crowds (Russians and Ukrainians in the majority historically, more Serbs and Germans today, hotel prices x4-x5, packed beaches, intense nightlife type Bay 42 and Top Hill). From November to March, expect 5-13°C and 41-49% rain: mild for the Balkans, many hotels close off-season. Highlights: Stari Grad (15th-century Venetian fortified old town, maze of paved alleys, citadel, Saint John Cathedral), Mogren I and II Beach (under the ramparts, one of the most beautiful beaches on the Montenegrin coast), Slovenska plaža (the largest beach of Budva, 1.6 km, lively), Ballerina Statue (Mogren). Sveti Stefan 5 km away (15th-century fortified island-village transformed into 5*+ hotel by Aman Resorts since 2009, signature photo of Montenegro but hotel closed since 2021 for disputes, paid access to ramparts only, public Sveti Stefan beach magnificent). Excursions: Kotor 30 min away (UNESCO, spectacular bay), Lovćen National Park 1h away (mausoleum Petar II Petrović-Njegoš at the summit 1660m, 461 steps), Cetinje 45 min away (former royal capital of Montenegro). Try pršut (smoked ham), kačamak, Montenegrin raki. Tivat airport (15 min).
lofoten:en: The Lofoten Islands, Norwegian archipelago beyond the Arctic Circle: 7 main islands connected by bridges, 24,000 inhabitants, the most spectacular landscapes of Norway (1000m mountains plunging into the sea, colourful fishing villages). July is my favourite month (7.5/10): 11-16°C, 44% rain, this is the "midnight sun" (from 28 May to 14 July, no sunset for 7 weeks), hikes, kayaking, fishing. From June to August, expect 8-16°C: summer high season (few tourists outside cruises), Reine and Hamnøy are the most photographed villages (packed in July). From September to March, expect -5/7°C and 47-61% precipitation often as snow: this is the Northern Lights season (Lofoten = one of the best spots in the world, visible as soon as it's dark AND sky clear, which is rare in winter Lofoten), cod fishing (skrei) in March (the gold of the Norwegian sea, traditional fishing continued for 1000 years). From December to January, it's the polar night (no sun above the horizon for 4 weeks, but magnificent blue light). Highlights: Reine (the most famous village, signature photo from Reinebringen 448m, 1500 wooden steps renovated 2018), Hamnøy and its red rorbuer (fishermen's cabins on stilts), Henningsvær ("Venice of the Lofoten", bridge since 1981), Kvalvika Beach (1h hike, white sand), Ramberg (the most accessible beach). Activities: kayaking between the islands, climbing, fishing, Munkebu/Reinebringen hike. Try bacalao, fish soup, brunost (brown goat cheese). Logistics: Oslo-Bodø flight + Bodø-Moskenes ferry 4h or direct Oslo-Leknes/Svolvær flight.
tromso:en: Tromsø, Norwegian town beyond the Arctic Circle: 80,000 inhabitants 350 km north of the Arctic Circle, the largest town at this latitude in the world. Nicknamed the "Paris of the North" in the 19th century for its refinement compared to the polar explorers (Amundsen, Nansen were based here). July is my favourite month (7.1/10): 10-17°C, 47% rain, this is the midnight sun (from 18 May to 27 July, no sunset for 2 months), hiking, fishing, whale watching (orcas in November-January in the neighbouring fjords). From June to August, expect 7-17°C: summer high season. From November to March, expect -8/-1°C and 50-55% precipitation often as snow: this is the tourist high season for the Northern Lights (Tromsø is on the "aurora belt", visible 200 nights/year when sky clear), polar night from 27 November to 15 January (no sun above the horizon, but magnificent blue light 4-5h per day). Hotel prices x3 in winter high season. Highlights: Arctic Cathedral (Ishavskatedralen, 1965, signature modern architecture visible from the town), Fjellheisen Cable Car (Storsteinen 421m, panorama over the town and the islands, 4-min ascent), Polaria (polar museum, seals), Polar Museum (history of Amundsen explorations, expeditions to the pole), Tromsø Bibliothek (modern architecture). Activities: Northern Lights chase by bus or minivan (3-5h, 100-200€), husky dog sledding (2h initiation or 1-7 day expedition), whale safari (from Skjervøy in November-January, orcas and humpback whales), night in a Sami cabin, snowshoeing, skiing at Tromsø Alpinpark. Try bidos (reindeer stew, Sami dish), salmon, whale (controversial). Direct flight Oslo in 2h.
bergen:en: Bergen, second city of Norway: 290,000 inhabitants on the west coast, nicknamed the "Gateway to the Fjords" (starting point for Geirangerfjord, Sognefjord, Hardangerfjord). Also called the "rainiest city in Europe" (200-300 days of rain/year, 2300 mm/year). June is my favourite month (7.1/10): 9-16°C, 53% rain, long days. From June to August, it's the high season: 9-17°C, 53-66% rain (the chance of having a day without rain is low all year). Bergen never tops 7.1/10 (very humid oceanic climate). From September to March, expect -1/10°C and 56-68% precipitation (often snow in winter): Bergen under the rain or snow has its particular charm. From November to March, days at 6-7h of light, but Northern Lights rare at this latitude (60°N, further south than Tromsø). Highlights: Bryggen (UNESCO, 14th-century Hanseatic quarter, colourful wooden houses on the harbour, signature of Bergen, several fires over the centuries, rebuilt identically), Fish Market (Fisketorget) (on the harbour, since 1276, fresh seafood), Fløibanen Funicular (8 min to Mount Fløyen 320m, panoramic view of the fjords and the town), Ulriksbanen Cable Car (Mount Ulriken 643m, 360° view), Saint Olaf Cathedral (12th century), Hanseatic Museum (life of the Hanseatic merchants). Fjord excursions: Norway in a Nutshell (train + boat + bus combo from Bergen to Flåm/Sognefjord, full day 1500-2000 NOK), Geirangerfjord cruise (UNESCO, 5h by ferry), Hardangerfjord (3rd longest fjord in the world). Try fiskesuppe (fish soup), bacalao, brunost (brown cheese characteristic of Norway). Very expensive (Norway = one of the most expensive countries in the world, meal 30-60€, beer 8-12€).
svalbard:en: Svalbard (Spitsbergen), Norwegian Arctic archipelago halfway between continental Norway and the North Pole: 2900 inhabitants over 61,022 km². The northernmost inhabited Norwegian territory in the world = capital Longyearbyen at 78°N, 1300 km from the North Pole, closer to the North Pole than continental Norway. Unique administrative status: Svalbard Treaty 1920 = Norwegian territory but open without visa to all signatories (44 countries including France-Russia-USA-China). No agriculture, no inter-village roads, transport by snowmobile or boat exclusively. May is my favourite month (5.3/10): -3-0°C, 31% rain, relatively mild Arctic temperatures. Svalbard never tops 5.3/10 (extreme polar climate). From April to August, it's the tourist high season: -12 to 10°C, total midnight sun 20 April-22 August (124 days without sunset), ideal Arctic hiking-fauna observation conditions. From September to March, it's the polar night: -17 to -1°C, sun never rises 26 October-15 February (112 days without sunrise), optimal conditions for Northern Lights but extreme temperatures -30°C night possible. Highlights: Longyearbyen (capital 2400 inhabitants, former coal-mining town 1906, unique Arctic atmosphere with abandoned former coal mine + colourful houses on stilts in the permafrost = obligation to preserve permafrost), Svalbard Global Seed Vault ("World Seed Vault", 2008 biological safe buried in the Platåberget mountain 130m under the rock, protecting 1 million seed samples of all the world's edible crops to counter global agricultural extinction catastrophe, public access forbidden but spectacular exterior visit), Svalbard Museum (Arctic museum with preserved fauna + mining history), North Pole Expedition Museum (museum of the polar expeditions Roald Amundsen-Salomon Andree-Umberto Nobile), Svalbard Cathedral (the northernmost in the world), Pyramiden (former Soviet mining town abandoned 1998, preserved post-apocalyptic atmosphere, accessible by boat in summer or snowmobile in winter), Barentsburg (other Russian town still active, 470 Russian-Ukrainian inhabitants working in the Russian coal mine). Activities: polar bear safari = unique signature experience in the world but firearm mandatory to leave Longyearbyen (3500 polar bears on the territory, more than human inhabitants, several fatal attacks including tourists 2011 + 2020), guided expeditions with armed guide exclusively, observation from afar recommended, never on foot alone; snowmobile safari March-May on the pack ice (one of the most impressive Arctic experiences in the world), dog sledding, fjord cruise June-August (Magdalenefjord-Liefdefjord with calving glaciers + walruses + seals), Arctic diving (reserved for expert dry suit divers, water 2-4°C). Safety: strict regulation firearm mandatory to leave the urban zone, no birth or death authorised in Longyearbyen (bodies must be repatriated to the Norwegian continent because the permafrost prevents proper decomposition), no cats authorised in Svalbard (to protect nesting migratory birds). Try reindeer, Norwegian whale (controversial), dried Arctic cod, klippfisk (salted cod), Norwegian Aquavit Lyngens. Logistics: Longyearbyen airport (LYR), unique direct flight from Oslo Norway 3h via SAS or Norwegian, from Tromsø 1h30, also access by boat via cruise lines from Tromsø in summer. Cost: extremely expensive (300-800€/day minimum), Antarctic equivalent, few off-season summer options.
goteborg:en: Gothenburg, 2nd city of Sweden: 600,000 inhabitants on the west coast, port city with 21 km of canals (planned by the Dutch in the 17th century). More accessible and cheaper than Stockholm, more relaxed and creative atmosphere (Volvo has its headquarters here). June is my favourite month (7.9/10): 12-19°C, 43% rain, 18h days, terraces on the canals. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 12-20°C, 43-55% rain. November to March, expect -1/7°C and 46-57% precipitation often as snow: Christmas market at Liseberg mid-November to late December (one of the largest in Scandinavia, amusement park transformed into enchanting market with 5 million lights). Highlights: Liseberg (the largest amusement park in Scandinavia, founded 1923, Helix roller coaster, wooden Balder leading the world rankings), Haga (old pedestrian working-class quarter, wooden houses, kanelbullar = giant cinnamon buns at Café Husaren), Skansen Kronan (17th-century fortress on hill, panoramic view), Feskekôrka Fish Market ("Fish Church", astonishing 1874 neo-Gothic architecture), Götaplatsen (square with statue of Poseidon by Carl Milles), Universeum (science centre with animals, popular with families), Avenyn (main avenue Kungsportsavenyn). Excursions: Gothenburg Archipelago (20 islands accessible by ferry, car-free for the most numerous, ideal in summer), Marstrand 1h away (fortress island, sailing), Smögen (colourful fishing village). Try västkustsallad (seafood salad), kanelbullar, gravlax, surströmming (fermented herring = shock olfactory experience), Swedish craft beer. More accessible than Stockholm in price terms (-15-20%).
laponie:en: Lapland is the circumpolar region covering the north of Finland, Sweden, Norway and Russia. The most accessible touristically: Finnish Lapland around Rovaniemi (capital). July is my favourite month (7.6/10): 11-18°C, 46% rain, this is the "endless day" (midnight sun, no sunset for 73 days at Rovaniemi), hikes, lakes and taiga. From June to August, expect 6-18°C: summer high season (few mosquitoes in June, many in July-August). From November to March, expect -12/-2°C and 32-41% precipitation often as snow: this is the tourist high season for the Northern Lights (visible 200 nights/year when the sky is clear, best probabilities September-March), prices x3-x5 in December (Father Christmas), nights lasting 24h in midwinter. Winter activities: Northern Lights (chase by snowmobile or lavvu tent, 9pm-2am window), husky dog sledding (1h initiation, mushing 2-7 days), Sami reindeer sledding (indigenous people, more peaceful), snowmobile safari, cross-country skiing, ice fishing, night in a glass igloo (Kakslauttanen, Wilderness Hotel at Inari, 400-1000€/night). Highlights: Rovaniemi ("Official Capital of Father Christmas", Santa Claus Village at the Arctic Circle, Father Christmas letter sent from the official office), Levi (ski resort with life), Saariselka and Inari (Lake Inari, Sami cultural capital). Try reindeer (in Poronkäristys pâté), salmon smoked over the fire (loimulohi), berries (cloudberry).
levi:en: Levi, the main ski resort of Finnish Lapland 250 km north of the Arctic Circle: 1100 permanent inhabitants (10,000 in tourist season). Largest ski resort in Finland with 43 pistes and 27 lifts, but above all signature destination for Northern Lights observation (Aurora Borealis), Father Christmas at neighbouring Rovaniemi + Nordic winter activities (dog sledding, snowmobile, cross-country skiing, Finnish sauna, etc.). July is my favourite month (7.8/10): 11-19°C, 42% rain, ideal temperatures in Nordic summer with midnight sun (June-July the sun never sets under the Arctic Circle 24h/24 of day). June stays excellent (7.5/10). From June to August, it's the paradoxical summer high season: 8-19°C, Lapland hiking, mountain bike, fishing (Levi has a 9-hole golf course above the Arctic Circle open even at midnight thanks to the midnight sun). From December to March, expect -14/-2°C and 30-40% precipitation often as snow: this is the ski + Northern Lights observation high season, glacial temperatures (sometimes -40°C peak January-February, very cold clothing mandatory). From November to January: polar night ("Kaamos" = sun never rises 1-2h max twilight, optimal conditions for the auroras). Pistes: 43 km Levi Resort (modest vs Alps: 600 km 3 Valleys), altitude 200-531m (very low compared to Alps 1000-3500m, but high latitude compensates for the snow), long ski season November-May, snow guaranteed thanks to permanent cold climate. Signature pistes: Levi Slope (Alpine Ski World Cup giant slalom descent every November, traditionally the first stage of the FIS circuit), Front Slope (main side). Signature Nordic winter activities: dog sledding ("husky safari", signature Lapland experience, ride or full day, 100-300€/person, drive 6-dog huskies yourself), snowmobile ("snowmobile safari"), cross-country skiing (Levi = paradise of langlauf 230 km of trails), snowshoeing, fat bike (wide-tyre bike on snow), reindeer safari in the company of indigenous Sami breeders (traditional Scandinavian Lapp people), Northern Lights safari (night outing 9pm-1am by snowshoe or snowmobile in search of the Northern Lights far from urban lights, optimal conditions required: clear sky + solar activity = Kp +3 minimum level, no guarantee but 70-80% chance per stay of 5+ days in season December-March). Finnish sauna = unique Finnish cultural institution in the world (UNESCO inscription 2020), to practise authentically with ritual: sauna 90°C → ice/pure snow bath → sauna → ice bath → relaxation, mandatory signature experience in Finland. Hotels: Hotel Levi Panorama (4*+ panoramic), Hotel K5 Levi, Glass Igloos Levin Iglut (signatures of Levi = transparent glass igloo rooms to observe the auroras from your bed, 400-1000€/night in high season), Aurora Cabins. Excursions: Rovaniemi 2h by bus ("Santa Claus Village" = official Father Christmas village where all children's letters from the world addressed to Father Christmas are received, biggest tourist attraction in Lapland, already covered by BDW), Saariselkä 2h away (other ski resort + auroras further north). Try reindeer (signature Lapland reindeer pan with berry sauce), salmon soup, korvapuusti (Finnish cinnamon buns), cloudberry (Arctic blackberry local berry), lakka liqueur, glögi (spiced Finnish mulled wine), salmon brine. Kittilä airport (KTT) 15 min away, direct flights Helsinki + European charters in season.
aarhus:en: Aarhus, second city of Denmark: 290,000 inhabitants on the east coast of Jutland, vibrant student city (40,000 students, 30% of the population), European Capital of Culture 2017. Younger and more accessible than Copenhagen for discovering modern Denmark. June is my favourite month (7.9/10): 12-19°C, 40% rain, 18h days in June. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 12-20°C, 40-46% rain, beaches to the north (Bellevue) and south (Marselisborg Forest). From November to March, expect 1-8°C and 37-46% precipitation often as snow: Danish hygge atmosphere (cocooning concept), Christmas markets in the town hall square. Highlights: ARoS Aarhus Kunstmuseum (modern art museum, signature of the rainbow "Your Rainbow Panorama" by Olafur Eliasson on the roof, 360° panorama through coloured glass), Den Gamle By ("Old Town", open-air museum with 75 historic Danish 16th-20th century houses transported and reconstituted), Aarhus Cathedral (the longest in Northern Europe 93m, 13th century), Moesgaard Museum (museum of prehistory and the Viking age, impressive architecture with grass roof), Latin Quarter (medieval old town, charming alleys, restaurants), Aarhus Street Food (popular food court), Marselisborg Slot (royal family's summer residence, public park). Samsø Island 1h30 by ferry (100% renewable energy island since 2007). Skagen 2h30 away (extreme north of Denmark, meeting point of 2 seas Skagerrak and Kattegat). Try smørrebrød (Danish open sandwiches), wienerbrød (pastries), Danish craft beers (Aarhus has 30+ craft breweries).
moscou:en: Moscow, Russian capital: 12 million inhabitants, Eastern European megacity, extreme continental climate. August is my favourite month (8.3/10): 14-23°C, 25% rain, parks in full life (Gorky Park, VDNKh), terraces, 16h days. From June to August, it's the summer high season: 12-24°C, ideal temperatures. From December to February, expect -9/-2°C with 30-43% precipitation often as snow: real Russian winter, days at 7h of light, but Moscow under snow is magnificent (colourful cathedrals, Red Square ice rinks, Christmas markets), heated metro, hotels at half-price. From March-April and October-November are difficult transitions (mud, grey sky, variable temperatures). Highlights: Red Square (UNESCO, Saint Basil's Cathedral with colourful domes, Lenin Mausoleum still visitable, GUM historic department store), Kremlin (UNESCO, presidential palace, Orthodox cathedrals, armoury with Fabergé egg collection), Moscow Metro (one of the most beautiful in the world, underground palaces marble/mosaics/chandeliers, stations Komsomolskaya, Mayakovskaya, Novoslobodskaya). Bolshoi (opera and ballet, booking 3 months ahead, tickets 50-300€). Tretyakov Gallery (Russian art), Pushkin Museum (Western art). Quarters: Arbat (touristy pedestrian street), Patriarshie Prudy (smart, trendy), Tverskaya (luxury). Try borscht, pelmeni, blini with caviar, vodka. Important: since the invasion of Ukraine (February 2022), Russian visa difficult to obtain for Europeans, direct Paris-Moscow flights suspended (mandatory stopover via Istanbul or Dubai), financial restrictions (Visa/Mastercard not accepted in Russia).
saint-petersbourg:en: Saint Petersburg, former imperial Russian capital (1712-1918): 5 million inhabitants, founded by Peter the Great in 1703 on the marshes of the Neva, nicknamed the "Venice of the North" for its 800 bridges and 42 islands. UNESCO 1990. June is my favourite month (8.0/10): 12-20°C, 36% rain, this is the season of the White Nights (mid-June to mid-July, sun almost never set, 19h of day, night festivals, drawbridges open 1am-5am to let boats pass), tourist peak. From July to August, expect 14-22°C: high season, days still very long. From December to March, expect -7/2°C with 31-46% precipitation often as snow: Saint Petersburg under snow is magnificent (imperial palaces, frozen Neva, but days at 5h of light end of December, seasonal depression common among the inhabitants). From October-November and April, expect -4/9°C: humid and grey transitions. Highlights: Hermitage Museum (one of the largest museums in the world, 3 million objects, Winter Palace of the tsars itself a masterpiece), Cathedral of the Saviour on the Spilled Blood (traditional Russian style, mosaics), Saint Isaac's Cathedral (4th largest cathedral in the world, observatory at the top), Palace Square, Nevsky Prospect (4.5 km of main avenue), Peter and Paul Fortress (where the Romanovs are buried), Mariinsky (opera and ballet, world prestige). Excursions: Peterhof ("Russian Versailles", spectacular fountains), Tsarskoye Selo / Pushkin (Catherine Palace with its reconstituted Amber Room). Important: Russian visa difficult for Europeans since 2022, mandatory stopovers.
sotchi:en: Sochi, Russian seaside resort on the Black Sea coast in Krasnodar Krai: 410,000 inhabitants. Unique humid subtropical climate in Russia (the only one in the country), nicknamed the "Russian Riviera". Hosted the 2014 Winter Olympics (the most expensive in history estimated at 50 billion $, controversy over massive corruption and working conditions), 2018 Football World Cup, F1 until 2021 (cancelled following the invasion of Ukraine). August is my favourite month (9.0/10): 22-28°C, 26% rain, sea at 25°C, ideal temperatures. From June to September, it's the seaside tourist high season: 18-28°C, warm sea, beaches packed with Russians (Sochi has been the main Russian summer resort since the Soviet era). From December to February, expect 3-12°C and 43-49% rain: mild winter for Russia (sometimes 15°C in January), but skiing on the neighbouring heights at Krasnaya Polyana (35 km, 1500-2300m, Rosa Khutor complex of the 2014 Olympics, one of the most modern Russian ski resorts). Sochi-city highlights: Seaside promenade, Riviera Park (since 1898, botanical garden, magnolia avenue), Cathedral of Saint Michael the Archangel (1874-1891, neo-Byzantine), Mount Akhun (663m, 1936 panoramic tower), Dendrarium (alpine botanical garden), Fisht Olympic Stadium (2018 World Cup), Sochi Park (Russian theme park). Excursions: Krasnaya Polyana 35 min away (skiing, summer hiking), Lago-Naki Plateau, Verticulina Falls, Caucasus hiking. Audience: Sochi remains an essentially Russian destination (Europeans become rare since 2022 sanctions, EU-Sochi direct flights almost non-existent), nostalgic post-Soviet atmosphere still present. Try Georgian-Russian shashlik (close cultural heritage), khachapuri (Georgian cheese bread popular in Sochi), pelmeni, blini, vodka.
la-havane:en: Havana, Cuban capital: 2 million inhabitants, museum-city stuck in the 1950s (American embargo since 1962, vintage American cars, dilapidated but charming colonial buildings). February is my favourite month (8.8/10): 21-29°C, 23% rain, end of the dry season, ideal temperatures. From December to April, it's the high season: 20-31°C, relative dry climate, hotel prices x2-x3. From June to November, it's the cyclone season: 51-73% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Irma 2017 category 5 hit Cuba), heavy heat (24-33°C). From May and October-November are the shoulder seasons. Highlights: Habana Vieja (UNESCO, 4 founding squares: Plaza de la Catedral, Plaza Vieja, Plaza San Francisco, Plaza de Armas), Malecón (8 km of seaside promenade, meeting point of Cubans at sunset), Capitol (replica of the Washington Capitol), Vedado (modern 50s quarter, Hotel Nacional, Coppelia ice cream), Miramar (embassies, Florida ferry dock in 1958, no longer since), Fortresses Castillo de la Real Fuerza, Morro, Cabaña (ritual cannonade 9pm every evening since 1762). Hemingway bars: La Bodeguita del Medio (mojito), El Floridita (daiquiri), Hemingway lived in the finca Vigía at San Francisco de Paula (museum). Try ropa vieja (shredded beef), moros y cristianos, congrí, tostones, daiquiri, mojito, Havana Club rum. Safety: Habana Vieja safe by day, vigilance at night. Cuban economy in serious crisis (chronic shortages, dual currency removed 2021, inflation 200%+).
trinidad-cuba:en: Trinidad, colonial town in central Cuba in Sancti Spíritus province: 75,000 inhabitants 6 km from the Caribbean coast. Founded in 1514 by Diego Velázquez, it's one of the oldest towns in the Americas. UNESCO 1988 with the neighbouring Valle de los Ingenios for the best-preserved colonial ensemble in Cuba (frozen in time after the decline of sugar at the end of the 19th century). January-February are my favourite months (8.9/10): 20-29°C, 14-16% rain, this is the ideal Caribbean dry season. From November to April, it's the tourist high season: 20-31°C, 9-30% rain. From March-April drop to 6.9/10 due to increasing heat. From May to October, it's the rain and cyclone season: 47-62% rainfall. Highlights: Plaza Mayor (main square, intact 17th-19th century colonial ensemble), Iglesia Parroquial de la Santísima Trinidad (19th-century neoclassical), Museo Romántico (Casa Brunet, 19th-century palace furnished from origin), Museo Municipal (Palacio Cantero, 360° panorama from the bell tower), Casa de la Música (staircase in front of the church, salsa/son live music every evening in the open air, the Cuban soul), Casa de la Trova (traditional music), Plaza Carrillo, Casitas Quarter (colourful colonial houses). Excursions: Valle de los Ingenios (UNESCO, valley of the former sugar plantations with 75 ruined 18th-19th century refineries, Manaca-Iznaga Tower 45m built by a planter to watch his slaves), Playa Ancón 12 km away (signature beach of Trinidad, turquoise water), Topes de Collantes 30 min away (mountainous national park 800m altitude, hike Salto del Caburní waterfall), Cienfuegos 80 km away (UNESCO, French town founded 1819, baroque). Logistics: Trinidad 4h by car from Havana (Carretera Central road), casa particular (with locals) recommended over hotels (15-30€/night, more local and traditional experience). Try ropa vieja (shredded meat), congrí (black rice with beans), canchánchara (signature Trinidad honey-lemon-rum cocktail), Cubita coffee.
lviv:en: Lviv (Lvov in Russian, Lwów in Polish), large cultural city in north-western Ukraine: 720,000 inhabitants. Cultural capital of Ukraine, former Polish town (1349-1772 Polish, 1772-1918 Austro-Hungarian under the name Lemberg, 1918-1939 Polish, 1939-1991 Soviet, Ukrainian since 1991). UNESCO 1998 for its historic centre. Unique Central European-Mitteleuropean atmosphere preserved. August is my favourite month (8.6/10): 16-25°C, 34% rain, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (8.3/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 12-25°C. From November to March, expect -3/8°C and 33-44% rain: continental cold, snow possible, unique Mitteleuropean atmosphere under winter mists and Ukrainian Christmas markets. Highlights: Market Square (Rynok Square, medieval main square with 19th-century Renaissance town hall + 44 preserved colourful 16th-18th century burgher houses, one of the most beautiful squares in Central Europe), Armenian Cathedral of the Assumption (14th century, one of the oldest Armenian cathedrals in Europe, witness to the historic Armenian community of Lviv), Latin Cathedral (14th-18th century Polish Gothic-baroque, formerly the main Roman Catholic cathedral), Greek-Catholic Saint George's Cathedral (18th-century baroque, metropolitan seat of the Ukrainian Greek-Catholic Church of Ukraine), Golden Space Synagogue (Beis Aharon V'Yisroel, memorial to the synagogues destroyed during the Holocaust in Lviv), Lviv Opera (Solomiya Krushelnytska Theatre, one of the most beautiful operas in Central Europe 1900 neo-renaissance), Pharmacy-Museum (the oldest pharmacy in Ukraine still in operation 1735), House Museum (Architecture Skansen, open-air museum of traditional Ukrainian houses), Lytchakiv Cemetery (one of the most beautiful cemeteries in Central Europe with monumental tombs of Polish-Ukrainian writers and artists), Lviv Castle ("Vysokyi Zamok", hill panorama 413m). Excursions: Ukrainian Carpathians 3h away, Olesko and Pidhirtsi castles, Chernivtsi 4h away (UNESCO 2011, spectacular neo-Byzantine residential University), Kyiv 6h by train (capital, Russian border, war situation since 2022). Safety: Ukraine at war since the Russian invasion of 24 February 2022. Lviv has been relatively spared from the fighting (furthest from the eastern front) but still suffers periodic Russian missile strikes. Travel inadvisable to civilians by France Diplomacy. If travel maintained for solidarity or humanitarian aid: trip possible from Poland by direct Krakow-Lviv train 8h. Try borsch (signature Ukrainian red soup), varenyky (dumplings), salo (fermented smoked lard), pampouchki (raised doughnuts), Ukrainian goulash, Ukrainian Trans-Carpathian wines, local Lvivske beer. Lviv airport (LWO) currently closed for international civil flights for war reasons.
jodhpur:en: Jodhpur, the "Blue City": 1.1 million inhabitants, capital of the Thar desert, dominated by the Mehrangarh fortress perched on a 122m rocky outcrop. February is my favourite month (9.5/10): 14-28°C, 1% rain, ideal temperatures, golden light on the blue Brahmin walls. From November to March, it's the high season: 10-30°C, ideal dry climate. From April to June, it's extreme heatwave: 25-41°C (sometimes 49°C, one of the hottest cities in India), impossible. From July to September, it's the monsoon: 21-48% rainfall (low for India), more bearable temperatures (25-35°C). Highlights: Mehrangarh Fort (UNESCO World Monuments Watch, one of the largest forts in India, 121m on the cliff, 7 gates, extraordinary interior palace with Phool Mahal and Sheesh Mahal, museum), Jaswant Thada (19th-century white marble mausoleum), Umaid Bhawan Palace (Art Deco palace 1929-1943, the last Indian palace built, partially converted into Taj 5*+ hotel and current Maharaja's residence), Clock Tower / Sardar Market (old bazaar, spices and omelettes at Omelette Shop), Brahmpuri (blue old town, labyrinthine alleys). Bishnoi Villages 30 km away (excursion to ecologist rural communities). Try mawa kachori, mirchi vada, makhaniya lassi. October-November and March are the best tourism/weather compromises.
jaisalmer:en: Jaisalmer, the "Golden City": 78,000 inhabitants, yellow sandstone citadel in the heart of the Thar desert near the Pakistani border, one of the rare still-inhabited fortresses in the world. November is my favourite month (9.2/10): 17-29°C, 1% rain, ideal temperatures for the desert. From November to February, it's the high season: 9-27°C, ideal climate. From March to June, it's the extreme heatwave: 19-41°C (sometimes 49°C), desert at 50°C, impossible. July is the worst month (2.1/10): 28-38°C with 24% light monsoon, monstrous humidity for the desert. Highlights: Sonar Quila/Golden Fort (one of the only "living forts" in the world, 5000 people still live here, but overcrowding threatens its foundations), Patwon Ki Haveli (5 sumptuously sculpted havelis), Salim Singh Ki Haveli, Nathmal Ki Haveli, Jain Temples (12th-17th centuries in the fort), Gadi Sagar Lake (walking distance from the fort, atmospheric at sunset). Desert excursions: Sam Sand Dunes 42 km away (the most famous but touristy, camel ride, night in luxury "glamping" tent), Khuri Sand Dunes 50 km away (quieter, less frequented). Try ker sangri (desert vegetables), bajre ki roti, ghotua ladoo. Logistics: night train from Jodhpur (5h) or Delhi (15h), no direct commercial airport. October and March are the best compromises.
amritsar:en: Amritsar, holy city of Sikhism in north-western India in the state of Punjab on the Pakistani border: 1.1 million inhabitants. Spiritual centre of Sikhism with its Golden Temple, one of the most visited religious sites in the world (100,000 pilgrims per day, 30 million per year). Memory of the Jallianwala Bagh massacre 1919 (379 dead by British troops, trigger of the Indian independence movement) and of the Partition 1947 (creation of Pakistan, border 30 km from the city, the most violent partition in history). March is my favourite month (8.8/10): 14-26°C, 18% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 6-26°C, dry and cool climate. From June to September, it's the monsoon: 24-58% rainfall, temperatures remaining high (24-39°C) with oppressive humidity. From April to June, expect 19-39°C: extreme northern Indian heatwave, sometimes 45°C, impossible to visit in midday. Highlights: Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib, the "Sanctuary of God", central Sikh temple, founded 1577 by Guru Ram Das 4th Sikh guru, covered with 750 kg of gold by Maharaja Ranjit Singh 1830, located in the middle of a sacred pool "Amrit Sarovar" = "the pool of nectar" which gave its name to the city Amritsar, accessible 24h/24 to all religions = Sikh principle of universal welcome, exceptional spiritual atmosphere at sunrise 4am with continuous recitation of the Guru Granth Sahib Sikh holy book, crowd of pilgrims immersed in the sacred pool), Langar (free communal kitchen inside the Golden Temple, 100,000 meals served daily to all religions = Sikh principle of equality, overwhelming human experience, possibility of helping in the kitchen to serve), Jallianwala Bagh (memorial of the 1919 massacre where Brigadier Reginald Dyer ordered firing without warning on 5000 unarmed people gathered in an enclosed garden, 10 minutes of fire, well where some jumped to escape the bullets still visible, memorial), Partition Museum (Town Hall, 2017, first world museum dedicated to the partition of India 1947, survivor testimonies), Akal Takht (Sikh temple seat of religious authority), Wagah Border Ceremony 30 km away at the Pakistani border (daily flag-lowering ceremony at 5pm, Indian and Pakistani military rivalling in exaggerated choreographed steps, crowd of 25,000 people on the Indian side and 5000 on the Pakistani side, unique atmosphere between military circus and nationalist expression, free, arrive 1h before). Try amritsari kulcha (Punjabi flatbread stuffed with potato), chole bhature, butter chicken (signature of Punjab), Punjabi lassi, parantha, sweet prasad of the Golden Temple. Logistics: Amritsar airport (ATQ) with direct flights from New Delhi 1h, direct Delhi-Amritsar train 6h.
mysore:en: Mysore (Mysuru), 2nd city of Karnataka in southern India: 1 million inhabitants at 770m altitude at the foot of the Western Ghats. Former capital of the kingdom of Mysore under the Wodeyar dynasty (1399-1947, one of the longest uninterrupted reigns in history), second tourist destination of Karnataka after Bangalore. World capital of Ashtanga Vinyasa yoga (Pattabhi Jois school founded 1948, one of the most influential yoga schools in the world, students: Madonna, Sting, Gwyneth Paltrow). January is my favourite month (9.3/10): 17-29°C, 6% rain, ideal temperatures. December stays excellent (9.0/10). From November to February, it's the tourist high season: 17-32°C, dry and cool climate thanks to altitude. From March to May, expect 20-35°C: hot but bearable thanks to altitude (Mysore is cooler than the plain cities). From June to October, it's the south-west monsoon: 61-79% rainfall, but milder temperatures (20-29°C). Highlights: Mysore Palace ("Amba Vilas Palace", one of the largest palaces in India, neo-Saracenic 1912 rebuilt after the wood palace fire of 1897, mix of Hindu Rajput Mughal Gothic architectures, 7 royal gates, 12 temples in the enclosure, richly decorated interior with glass mosaics and painted ceilings, illuminated every Sunday evening and every evening during the Dussehra festival with 100,000 golden bulbs = signature visual of Mysore, the postcard image of South India), Chamundi Hills (1062m 13 km away, 1000 steps on foot or car road, Nandi the bull of Shiva 5m statue carved in a single block, Chamundeshwari temple dedicated to the patron goddess of Mysore), Devaraja Market (colourful spicy market of flowers and fruits since 1880, one of the most photogenic in South India), Brindavan Gardens 25 km away (Mughal-style gardens on the Krishnaraja Sagar dam 1932, illuminated musical fountains in the evening), Maharaja's Palace Jaganmohan Palace (Sri Jayachamarajendra art gallery), Railway Museum (one of the only railway museums in India), Mysore Zoo (one of the oldest in India 1892, captive elephant breeding programme). Major festival: Dussehra (10 days September-October according to the Hindu calendar, one of the largest religious festivals in South India, monumental royal processions of caparisoned elephants gold and illuminated palace), Yoga World in June (International Yoga Day). Excursions: Bandipur and Nagarhole National Parks 1-2h away (tigers, elephants), Hassan and Halebidu/Belur 3h away (UNESCO 12th-century Hoysala temples), Sravanabelagola (Bahubali statue 17m monolithic 9th century), Coorg/Kodagu 4h away ("Scotland of India", coffee plantations). Try Mysore masala dosa (the crispiest and spiciest version), Mysore pak (chickpea flour cake with ghee), bisi bele bath, Coorg coffee, Mysore silk sari (renowned). Logistics: Mysore airport (MYQ) with flights to Bangalore, direct Bangalore-Mysore train 3h.
darjeeling:en: Darjeeling, mountain station of West Bengal in north-eastern India in the foothills of the Himalayas: 120,000 inhabitants at 2050m altitude. World capital of Darjeeling tea ("champagne of teas", IGP 2004 limited to 87 plantations on 17,800 hectares, one of the most protected appellations in the world). Spectacular view of Kangchenjunga (8586m, 3rd highest summit in the world after Everest and K2). November is my favourite month (7.4/10): 8-16°C, 27% rain, clear sky, ideal temperatures. Darjeeling never tops 7.4/10 (Himalayan climate with torrential monsoon). From October to December, it's the tourist high season: 4-19°C, the clearest sky to see the Kangchenjunga. From March to May, it's the 2nd best season: 6-19°C, rhododendron blooming, first "first flush" tea harvest the most prestigious. From June to September, it's the monsoon: 87-97% rainfall, constant mists (Kangchenjunga view often hidden), frequent landslides closing roads. From January to February, expect 2-12°C: winter cold, snow possible at higher altitudes. Highlights: Tiger Hill (2585m 13 km away, sunrise observation point on the Kangchenjunga — the summit lights up gold-pink before the rest of the landscape, unique experience 4:30am by jeep, 5000-8000 people per morning in high season, also Mt Everest visible 200 km to the west on exceptionally clear days), Darjeeling Himalayan Railway ("Toy Train", UNESCO 1999, miniature steam train 60 cm gauge since 1881, 88 km between New Jalpaiguri and Darjeeling, 7-8h to cross the hills with 360° bends, one of the most picturesque trains in the world, still partly operating on steam), Tea plantations (free visit at most: Happy Valley Tea Estate 1854 = the most famous, tasting possible, direct purchases), Himalayan Mountaineering Institute (HMI, founded 1954 by Tenzing Norgay the sherpa who climbed Everest with Hillary 1953, fascinating museum), Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park (one of the only zoos in the world to breed in captivity the red panda, snow leopard, Tibetan wolf), Mall Road (British pedestrian street with Chowrasta central square), Observatory Hill, Japanese Peace Pagoda, Bhutia Busty Monastery (Buddhist monastery with original Tibetan Book of the Dead). Excursions: Kalimpong 2h away (other less touristy mountain station), Sandakphu 5-day trek (3636m, 4 summits over 8000m visible including Everest), Sikkim 3h away (former Buddhist kingdom, Rumtek monasteries). Try momos (Tibetan dumplings), thukpa (Tibetan soup), aloo dum (spicy potato), Darjeeling first flush tea (March-April) or autumnal flush (October-November), local yak cheese. Logistics: Bagdogra airport (IXB) 3h by jeep or Toy Train, New Jalpaiguri (NJP) train station 3h.
siargao:en: Siargao, island in the Mindanao region in the south-east of the Philippines: 100,000 inhabitants on 437 km². Nicknamed the "surf capital of the Philippines", paradise of surfers since the discovery of the cult spot Cloud 9 in 1992 (one of the best waves in Asia according to WSL rankings). Bohemian surfer atmosphere, much less developed than Boracay or Bali. February is my favourite month (7.4/10): 24-27°C, 84% rain. From September to November, it's the surf high season (the most powerful waves, north Pacific swell), peak in September-October. From January to May, it's the relatively drier season (in reality Siargao receives a lot of rain year-round, it's one of the wettest islands in the Philippines with 3500 mm/year), 24-29°C. From June to October, it's the cyclone season (one of the most exposed in the Philippines, typhoon Odette/Rai in December 2021 devastated 90% of the island, reconstruction still in progress). Highlights: Cloud 9 (the signature wave, extremely powerful right-hander reef break, wave for experts only, international WSL Siargao Cloud 9 contests every year September, characteristic wooden observation jetty of the surf magazine photos), General Luna ("GL" = main tourist village, restaurants-bars-hotels, white sand beach), Magpupungko Rock Pools (natural pools in the limestone rock, accessible at low tide 2-3h, crystalline water), Sugba Lagoon (turquoise lagoon between limestone cliffs, paddle or kayak, 20m natural diving board for cliff jumping), 3 Islands Tour (Naked Island without vegetation sandbank, Daku Island larger BBQ lunch, Guyam Island), Maasin River (river in the mangrove, cult Instagram liana-swing for jumping-into-water photos), Coconut Road (road bordered by coconut trees, palm grove of 3 million palm trees, to see at sunset), Pacifico Beach to the north (wild, intermediate surf). Activities: surf (Cloud 9 for experts, more accessible alternatives: Quicksilver, Stimpys, Tuason Point, Jacking Horse for intermediates, schools at GL), diving (modest but possible), island hopping, motorbike tour of the island. Audience: international surfers in the majority, bohemian backpackers, Filipino celebrities (the island has become very fashionable post-2018). Try kinilaw (Filipino raw fish ceviche with coconut milk), fresh seafood, coconut everything (water, milk, meat), San Miguel beer. Logistics: Siargao airport (IAO) with direct flights Manila (2h15) and Cebu (1h).
coron:en: Coron, town and island in the north of the Palawan archipelago in the Philippines: 65,000 inhabitants, capital of the northern Palawan district. Coron town is on Busuanga Island, but the touristy "Coron" = Coron Island 30 min by bangka, geological paradise with black limestone cliffs, hidden lagoons and sacred lakes of the Tagbanwa people (one of the oldest peoples of the Philippines, descendants of the first Austronesians). From January to March are my favourite months (6.9/10): 25-32°C, 20-24% rain, this is the ideal dry season. Coron never tops 6.9/10 (hot tropical climate). From November to May, it's the tourist high season: 25-33°C, sea at 28°C, hotel prices x2-x3, more sheltered from the south-west monsoon than El Nido thanks to the north-east position. From June to October, it's the wetter season: 67-93% rainfall. Highlights: Twin Lagoon (2 lagoons connected by a passage to swim under a rock fault, saline water + freshwater mixed creating thermoclinal mirage effect, signature Coron), Kayangan Lake ("purest lake in Asia" according to UNESCO, crystalline water among limestone cliffs, access by 300 steep steps from the dock 15 min, the panorama of the path towards the lake is one of the most famous images of the Philippines), Barracuda Lake (marine lake with halocline = brutal thermocline between cold salt water bottom and warm fresh water top, unique diving in the world for total visibility), Siete Pecados ("7 Sins", 7 islets with snorkelling around corals), Bulog Dos (thin sandbank between 2 seas, postcard image), Beach 91, Banol Beach, Malcapuya Island (preserved white sand). WWII wreck diving: Coron is one of the most famous wreck diving sites in the world. 12 Japanese Second World War wrecks sunk by US aviation on 24 September 1944 during a surprise attack on the Japanese fleet sheltering in the bay of Coron: Akitsushima (light cruiser 113m), Irako (refrigerator-cargo 147m), Olympia Maru, Okikawa Maru (190m, the largest), depths 12-43m, advanced technical diving. Maquinit Hot Springs (seaside hot springs, 38-40°C). Mount Tapyas (700 steps, sunset panorama over Coron bay). Excursions: El Nido 8h by ferry (the other Palawan paradise). Try kinilaw, pancit, lechon, balut, sinigang, Filipino mangoes. Logistics: Busuanga/Francisco B. Reyes airport (USU) 30 min by van from Coron Town, direct flights from Manila (1h15).
vientiane:en: Vientiane, capital of Laos: 950,000 inhabitants on the Mekong on the border of Thailand (the Friendship Bridge connects Vientiane to Nong Khai on the Thai side in 20 min). One of the calmest and most bohemian capitals in South-East Asia, very present French colonial heritage (architecture, daily baguette, café au lait). January is my favourite month (9.3/10): 18-28°C, 7% rain, end of the dry season, ideal temperatures. December stays excellent (9.3/10). From November to February, it's the dry season: 18-32°C, dry and cool climate in the morning (sometimes 12°C in December, surprisingly cool for tropical Asia). From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 25-34°C (sometimes 40°C), mists from agricultural slash-and-burn pollute the atmosphere of northern Laos. From June to October, it's the monsoon: 47-89% rainfall, but more bearable temperatures (23-31°C). Highlights: Pha That Luang (national signature, golden 16th-century stupa, the most sacred monument in Laos), Patuxai ("Laotian Arc de Triomphe", resembles the one in Paris built with American cement intended for an airport in 1968), Wat Si Saket (19th century, the oldest temple remaining after the Siamese sacking of 1828, 6800 Buddha statues), Wat Sisaket and Wat Ho Phra Kaew (historic places), COPE Visitor Center (poignant memorial on the UXO cluster bombs still present 50 years after the war, Laos received more US bombs than the entire Second World War combined). Vientiane Night Market (on the banks of the Mekong, sunset, street food, crafts). Excursions: Buddha Park (Xieng Khuan) 25 km away (200 eclectic Buddhist and Hindu statues 1958), Vang Vieng 4h by bus (karst landscapes, tubing). Try laap (chopped meat salad), sticky rice, papaya salad (tam mak hoong), Beer Lao (the national pride, 90% of the beer market in Laos).
park-city:en: Park City, Utah, is the former mining town reconverted into a premium ski resort, 35 minutes from Salt Lake City.
