Highlights / Beaches: West Bay (the signature beach of Roatán, powdery white sand 2 km, unique turquoise water, snorkelling from the beach with coral reef at 50m, one of the most beautiful beaches in the Caribbean according to many rankings), West End (seaside tourist village, restaurants-bars, smart backpacker atmosphere), Half Moon Bay (between West End and West Bay, sandy half-moon bay), Coxen Hole (capital of Roatán, ferries and cruisers), French Harbour (fishing port), Sandy Bay (peaceful residential area with diving institutes), Camp Bay Beach (east side, wild less touristy). Diving and snorkelling: Roatán is one of the world-renowned diving sites (top 10 worldwide according to many international diver rankings), 175 diving sites recorded around the island, PADI Open Water training 250-350 USD among the cheapest in the world, Mary's Place = signature site with limestone arch above the reef, Hole in the Wall, Spooky Channel, diving with reef sharks, green turtles, eagle rays, sometimes whale sharks March-May. Activities: Roatán Institute for Marine Sciences (dolphin research centre with framed ethical interactions, contested by some NGOs), Gumbalimba Park (exotic animals + zipline), Mahogany Bay (Carnival cruise port, day access only). Audience: Americans in the majority (60% of tourists via direct flights from Miami, Atlanta, Houston), Canadian snowbirds, international divers, Carnival/Royal Caribbean cruisers. Safety: Roatán is one of the safest destinations in Honduras, island sheltered from the crime problems of the continent (gangs MS-13 and Mara Salvatrucha which makes Honduras one of the countries with the highest homicide rates in the world, but the Bay Islands are protected by their insularity). Try baleadas (tortilla stuffed with beans-cheese, Honduran national dish), conch ceviche (lambi), grilled barracuda and grouper, fried plantain, sopa de caracol (lambi-coconut soup). Juan Manuel Galvez Roatán airport (RTB).
uruguay:en: Uruguay, the smallest Spanish-speaking South American country: 3.5 million inhabitants on 176,000 km², squeezed between Argentina and Brazil. Considered one of the most stable, democratic and progressive countries in Latin America (first country to legalise gay marriage and cannabis). Temperate climate without extremes (reversed seasons of the southern hemisphere). January is my favourite month (9.0/10): 20-26°C, 27% rain, this is the austral summer, beaches of Punta del Este and José Ignacio in full effervescence with Argentinians and Brazilians on holiday (prices x3-x5 on the coast in January-February). From December to March, it's the tourist high season: 18-26°C, 26-36% rain. From April to May and September-November are the best shoulder seasons: 11-22°C, mild climate. From June to August, it's the austral winter: 9-15°C, 25-29% rain, few tourists, but Montevideo stays pleasant. Regions to know: Montevideo (capital, see dedicated review), Punta del Este ("South American Saint-Tropez", smart beach resort since the 60s, casinos, peninsula between Atlantic and Rio de la Plata, Brava and Mansa beaches, Casapueblo by Carlos Paez Vilaró Uruguayan architect, prices x3-x5 in January-February when the Argentinians migrate en masse), José Ignacio (30 min from Punta del Este, more discreet and smarter, haunt of magnates and celebrities, La Mansa beach), Colonia del Sacramento (UNESCO, 17th-century Portuguese colonial town on the Rio de la Plata, accessible in 1h by ferry from Buenos Aires Argentina), Cabo Polonio (isolated village without official electricity or road, accessible only by 4x4 on the beach, bohemian/hippie atmosphere, sea lion colonies), Punta del Diablo (bohemian surf fishing village), Carmelo (Uruguayan vineyards, Tannat signature grape). Try chivito ("national sandwich" beef-ham-cheese-egg-bacon), parrillada (Uruguayan asado), dulce de leche, mate (national shared drink, more consumed than in Argentina), Tannat wine (emblem Uruguayan grape).
montevideo:en: Montevideo, capital of Uruguay: 1.3 million inhabitants (40% of the country's population, which has 3.4 million on 176,000 km² — one of the least populated countries in South America), on the Río de la Plata facing Buenos Aires (200 km by hydrofoil 2h15). Uruguay = the "Switzerland of Latin America" (politically stable, developed middle classes, security, progressive legalisations: the 1st country to legalise marijuana 2013, gay marriage 2013, divorce 1907). January is my favourite month (9.0/10): 20-26°C, 27% rain, this is the austral summer, ideal temperatures thanks to Atlantic breezes. From November to March, it's the high season: 16-26°C. From April to May and September-October are the transitions: 11-22°C. From June to August, it's the austral winter: 9-15°C, 25-29% rain, mild but windy. Highlights: Ciudad Vieja (18th-19th century old town, maze of alleys, Puerta de la Ciudadela former fortified gate, Mercado del Puerto = central market converted into asado parrillas restaurants), Plaza Independencia (main square that separates the colonial upper town and the modern lower town, statue of national hero José Artigas + his underground mausoleum, Palacio Salvo 1928 = former tallest building in South America 100m), Teatro Solís (1856, historic opera hall), Catedral Metropolitana, Rambla de Montevideo (22 km of promenade along the Río de la Plata, one of the longest urban seaside promenades in the world), Estadio Centenario (1930, place of the 1st Football World Cup won by Uruguay on its lawn, football museum), Pocitos and Carrasco (smart residential quarters with river urban beaches). Excursions: Punta del Este 2h away (the great smart Latin American beach resort, see dedicated review), Colonia del Sacramento 2h away (UNESCO, 17th-century Portuguese colonial town), Cabo Polonio 4h away (off-grid village by the ocean, sea lions). Try asado (real Uruguayan asado, slow long hours), chivito (Uruguayan signature sandwich with steak/ham/egg/bacon/lettuce/tomato/mayonnaise), dulce de leche (controversial origin Uruguay/Argentina), mate (national Uruguayan mate more than anywhere, Uruguayans walk everywhere with their thermos of hot water and calabaza), Tannat wines (signature Uruguayan grape).
punta-del-este:en: Punta del Este, smart beach resort of Uruguay: 10,000 permanent inhabitants (population x10-x20 in high season from late December to mid-February). "Saint-Tropez of South America", summer haunt of Argentinian, Brazilian and Uruguayan elites (wealthy Argentinians have their secondary residences here, it's one of the most expensive beach destinations in Latin America). January is my favourite month (9.0/10): 19-26°C, 25% rain, this is the peak of the austral summer. From December to February, it's full high season: hotel prices x5-x8 (Conrad Hotel & Casino 800-3000$/night), full restaurants, packed beaches. From March to May, it's the ideal shoulder season (sea still warm, prices divided by 3, crowds disappeared, it's one of the best moments). From June to October, it's the austral winter: 8-21°C, many establishments close, near-deserted town (5000 permanent inhabitants on site). Highlights: La Mano de Punta del Este ("Los Dedos", the most famous sculpture in Uruguay, 5 giant fingers emerging from the sand of Playa Brava, created 1982 by Mario Irarrázabal), Casapueblo (at Punta Ballena 15 km, hotel-museum-residence created by the artist Carlos Páez Vilaró 1958, Mediterranean architecture in white lime cubist built intuitively without plans, "Ceremonia del Sol" ritual = poetic reading at sunset on the terrace), Punta del Este Lighthouse (1860, on the point between the 2 oceans "Playa Brava" Atlantic with strong waves and "Playa Mansa" calm Río de la Plata), Casa Pueblo. Beaches: La Brava (Atlantic ocean side, waves, surfers), La Mansa (Río de la Plata side, calm, families), José Ignacio 30 min to the north (the smarter and more discreet "hidden gem", frequented by Shakira and other celebrities). Nightlife: Conrad Hotel & Casino, Ovo Beach Lounge. Excursions: Isla de Lobos by boat (250,000 sea lion island), La Barra smart hippie village, Punta Ballena. Try chivito, asado, Tannat wines, dulce de leche, calamares fritos.
antigua-guatemala:en: Antigua Guatemala, former colonial capital UNESCO: 1500m altitude, baroque ruins of churches (18th-century earthquakes), active volcanoes in the background (Fuego, Acatenango, Agua). February-March are my favourite months (9.1/10): 13-27°C, 12-19% rain, ideal dry season, clear sky to see Fuego eruptions. From May to October, it's the rainy season: 78-92% rainfall in afternoon showers, but mornings often sunny. From November to April, dry season, jacarandas in bloom (March-April). Highlights: Plaza Mayor and Cathedral Santiago (in ruins), Iglesia de la Merced (yellow coloured), Ruinas Iglesia de San Francisco, Convento de Capuchinas, Cerro de la Cruz (panoramic view of the town and Agua volcano). Volcano excursions: Acatenango (3976m, 6h ascent, bivouac, view of Fuego in permanent eruption), Pacaya (2500m, 2h ascent, flowing lava, grilled marshmallows). Markets: Mercado Municipal and craft markets (Chichicastenango 2h away on Thursdays/Sundays). Lake Atitlán 2h away (one of the most beautiful in the world according to Aldous Huxley, 3 volcanoes in the background, Mayan villages). Try pepián (national stew), tamales, Guatemalan coffee (among the best in the world).
monteverde:en: Monteverde (Cloud Forest Reserve), nature reserve in the centre of Costa Rica on the Tilarán Cordillera: 7000 inhabitants at 1300-1850m altitude. Tropical cloud forest = unique ecosystem where the clouds remain constantly hooked to the canopy creating a permanently humid microclimate that allows exceptional epiphytes and orchids. Reserve founded in 1972 by an American Quaker community come seeking a peaceful life away from US military service (Costa Rica has no army), today one of the most famous natural destinations in the world. March is my favourite month (8.0/10): 13-21°C, 27% rain, this is the ideal dry season at altitude. From December to April, it's the dry season "verano": 12-22°C with 21-46% rain. From May to November, it's the green season: 67-89% rainfall, but constant fogs and mists typical of the cloud forest, unique atmosphere. Permanent climate: Monteverde never tops 22°C in maximum average and rarely descends below 12°C in minimum average thanks to altitude 1300-1850m, ideal tropical temperate climate, cooler than the Pacific coast 30 min by car downhill (15°C difference in 30 min). Highlights: Monteverde Cloud Forest Biological Reserve (main reserve 10,500 hectares, accessible by paths, signature wildlife observation: resplendent quetzal = sacred bird of the Mayas-Aztecs with long green-red-blue feathers, signature of Monteverde, observable especially February-June during nesting, limited geographic zone Central America, considered one of the most beautiful birds in the world, presence guaranteed 30-50% of morning outings with guide), Sky Walk or Selvatura Park (suspension bridges through the canopy at 60m height, signature canopy experience of Monteverde), Zipline ("zipline", Monteverde is the cradle of the tourist canopy zipline concept invented here in 1979 by the American biologist Donald Perry to observe the canopy, today dozens of operators in Monteverde Selvatura/100% Aventura/Sky Trek, zip lines among the longest in the world 1500m+ with "Tarzan swing" descent), Curi-Cancha Reserve (more peaceful alternative with fewer tourists), Bat Jungle (interactive centre on bats), Frog Pond and Butterfly Garden (centres with local species), Selvatura Hummingbird Garden (gardens with hummingbirds in large numbers, several species including brilliant hummingbirds, facilitated photography). Sister forest: Santa Elena Cloud Forest Reserve (smaller municipal reserve but less frequented), Children's Eternal Rainforest (the largest private reserve in Costa Rica 23,000 hectares, founded 1988 by Swedish children to save the Costa Rican jungle, one of the most beautiful conservation initiatives in the world). History of the Quakers: the American pacifist Quaker community founded in Monteverde in 1951 (44 people initially) founded the dairy culture on the plateau (Monteverde Cheese factory institution since 1953, gouda and other cheeses = visit + tasting). Try Monteverde cheese, café Monteverde Coffee (signature Don Juan Coffee Tour plantation visit with demonstration of production stages), gallo pinto, casado, batidos. Logistics: Monteverde 4h from San José by car (partially rough road, ends in gravel track for the last 30 km = voluntary experience to preserve authenticity), no airport on site.
cuenca-equateur:en: Cuenca, 3rd city of Ecuador in the Andean sierra: 330,000 inhabitants at 2560m altitude, UNESCO listed 1999 for its well-preserved Spanish colonial historic centre. Considered the most European city in Ecuador (more than Quito), renowned for its quality of life (one of the favourite destinations of American and Canadian retirees in Latin America, expat community of 5000+ people). August is my favourite month (7.3/10): 8-19°C, 66% rain. Cuenca has a constant climate year-round (stable temperatures 8-20°C thanks to altitude, little seasonal variation), main variations on rainfall. From August to January, it's the relative "dry" season: 8-20°C, 66-83% rain, sun in the morning/afternoon storms typical of the equatorial Andean sierra. From February to July, it's the wetter season: 8-19°C, 73-93% rainfall. Highlights: Centro Histórico (UNESCO, maze of paved alleys, 16th-18th century Spanish colonial architecture), New Cathedral ("Catedral Nueva", Inmaculada Concepción 1885-1967, blue and white domes signatures of Cuenca, panoramic view from the terraces), Old Cathedral (1557, become museum), Plaza Calderón (main square between the 2 cathedrals), Mercado 10 de Agosto (central market, top floor = food court with hornado specialty of Cuenca), Río Tomebamba (river that crosses the city, El Barranco path along with colourful houses suspended on the cliff), Barrio Las Herrerías (blacksmiths' quarter), Museo del Banco Central Pumapungo (Inca-Cañari ruins, ethnobotanical garden), Museo de las Conceptas. Panama Hat industry: Cuenca is in reality the true world capital of the Panama Hat (the famous toquilla straw hat, despite its name is manufactured in Ecuador since the 16th century, exported via the Panama Canal in the 19th century = origin of the misleading name), visits to the Homero Ortega and K. Dorfzaun factories. Excursions: Cajas National Park 30 min away (Andean paramo, 270 glacial lakes, hiking altitude 3000-4500m, llamas), Ingapirca 2h away (the most important Inca ruins in Ecuador, but modest compared to Peru), Cordillera Real. Try cuy (roast guinea pig, traditional Andean dish), hornado (roast pork à la Cuenca, the most famous in Ecuador), locro de papas (potato soup), morocho (white maize drink).
namibie:en: Namibia, country in southern Africa: 2.5 million inhabitants on 825,000 km² (density among the lowest in the world), capital Windhoek. The most arid country in sub-Saharan Africa, former German then South African colony, independence 1990 (the youngest country in Africa). Unique biodiversity: Namib Desert (the oldest in the world, 80 million years) and Kalahari. September is my favourite month (9.5/10): 13-29°C, 2% rain, this is the end of the dry season, wildlife visible at watering holes. From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 8-31°C, 0-6% rain, dry climate ideal for safari and desert. From June-July, expect 8-22°C: glacial nights at altitude (sometimes below 0°C in the Kalahari), ideal for wildlife observation in the morning. From December to March, it's the relative rainy season: 25-33% rainfall, but it's the "green season" with re-greened landscapes and animal births. Regions to know: Sossusvlei (orange dunes of the Namib among the highest in the world — Big Daddy 325m, Dune 45 photogenic, Deadvlei "dead valley" with black fossilised acacia trees on white and orange background = the most signature landscape of Namibia), Skeleton Coast (Skeleton Coast, 500 km of desert + misty ocean, boat wrecks and whale skeletons, desert lions, Cape Cross sea lions 100,000 individuals), Etosha National Park (the largest national park in Namibia, 22,900 km² with a central salt "pan" that attracts wildlife in the dry season: elephants, lions, black and white rhinos, oryx, springboks), Damaraland (red rocky landscapes, San rock engravings of Twyfelfontein UNESCO, desert elephants), Fish River Canyon (the 2nd largest canyon in the world after the Grand Canyon, 160 km long, 27 km wide, 550m deep, 5-day hike), Walvis Bay and Swakopmund (Atlantic coast, kayaking with seals and pink flamingos, German atmosphere colonial architecture). Try biltong (dried meat), kapana (street-food grilled meat), oryx steak, Windhoek lager (German heritage).
windhoek:en: Windhoek is above all the logistic base for Namibia. At 1700m altitude on the central plateau, temperatures stay surprising: from May to August, nights drop to 8°C while days stay at 22-25°C. September is my favourite month (9.5/10): 13-29°C, 2% rain, clear sky to reach Sossusvlei, Etosha or the Skeleton Coast. From December to March, the rainy season brings 25-33% stormy showers (often in late afternoon) and temperatures climb to 30-32°C. The tracks can become difficult, some lodges close. It's the only period when the Namib re-greens: a rare and photogenic face of the desert. April-May are a good transition: ending rains, golden light, temperatures back to mild.
etosha:en: Etosha is the reference safari park in Namibia: 22,000 km² around an immense dried-up salt lake that becomes blinding white. May is my favourite month (9.4/10): 15-29°C, 1% rain, animals concentrated around the permanent watering holes (Okaukuejo, Halali, Namutoni). The whole dry season from April to August is excellent for safari: maximum visibility, animals predictable at the waterholes. The nights at Okaukuejo waterhole (illuminated until midnight) remain a rare experience: elephants, black rhinos, lions parading at 30m. From September to December, expect 18-36°C: the heat becomes crushing in midday (3 safari outings: dawn, end of day, night). From January to March (rainy season), animals dispersed, some tracks impassable, but green photogenic landscapes and baby season. Accommodation: 3 NWR camps inside the park (to book 6 months ahead in high season) or private lodges on the periphery.
kigali:en: Kigali surprises: the cleanest city in Africa, high security, advanced digital services. At 1500m altitude, mild climate year-round (15-27°C constant). July is my favourite month (9.4/10): 4% rain, clear sky. From March to May and October-December, these are the marked rainy seasons (43-76% showers, especially in April), often in late afternoon. Morning visits remain workable. The Gisozi Genocide Memorial remains the obligatory passage to understand the country. The Kimihurura quarter concentrates the café and restaurant scene. Kigali is above all the base to reach the Volcanoes National Park (3h by road) where the gorilla treks take place (1500$ permit, to book 6+ months ahead). Lake Kivu at Gisenyi for relaxation. Hotel rates aligned with European capitals.
addis-abeba:en: Addis Ababa, capital of Ethiopia: 5 million inhabitants at 2355m altitude, one of the highest capitals in the world. Cultural and political capital of Africa (seat of the African Union since its creation in 2002, heir of the OAU founded in Addis in 1963). Ethiopia = only country in Africa never colonised (except briefly by Italy 1936-1941), preserves an Orthodox calendar with 13 months and is in the year 2018 compared to the Gregorian calendar (8-year offset). January is my favourite month (8.7/10): 8-22°C, 5% rain, end of the dry season, clear sky. From November to February, it's the tourist high season: 8-22°C, 2-19% rain, mild and dry climate ideal for visiting the country (notably northern historic route). From July-August drop to 4.6/10 due to the rainy season ("kremt"): 98-100% rainfall, tropical deluges, cut tracks. From October, expect 8-20°C with 19% rain: excellent period for the green highlands post-rains. Highlights: National Museum of Ethiopia ("Lucy" alias Dinkenesh = Australopithecus afarensis skeleton 3.2 million years discovered in 1974 by Donald Johanson in the Awash valley, one of the most complete ever discovered), Holy Trinity Cathedral (Holy Trinity, Ethiopian neo-baroque, tomb of Emperor Haile Selassie died in 1975), Mount Entoto (3200m, former site of Menelik II, panorama, monasteries), Mercato (one of the largest open-air markets in Africa), Red Terror Martyrs Memorial (memorial of the Derg communist regime 1974-1991, about 500,000 deaths), Shola Market, Fendika Cultural Center (traditional azmari music-dance). Excursions: Lalibela (UNESCO, northern historic route, see dedicated review), Aksum (UNESCO, ancient kingdoms), Gondar (UNESCO, see dedicated review), Rift Valley and Lake Tana source of the Blue Nile, Omo Valley (Mursi tribes, Hamer = last "first" peoples of Africa). Try injera (sour spongy flatbread based on teff, base of all meals), wat (spicy sauces), tibs (sautéed meat), kitfo (local steak tartare), Ethiopian coffee (origin of coffee in the world, traditional ceremony mandatory).
lalibela:en: Lalibela, holy town of Ethiopia in the Amhara region: 18,000 inhabitants at 2500m altitude. UNESCO 1978 for its 11 monolithic churches dug in the rock in the 12th-13th century under the reign of King Lalibela (who wanted to create a "New Jerusalem" in Africa for Ethiopian pilgrims deprived of access to the Holy Land by the Arab conquest). One of the most impressive religious sites in the world. January is my favourite month (8.9/10): 10-23°C, 4% rain, end of the dry season, clear sky. From December to March, it's the tourist high season: 10-25°C, 2-13% rain, ideal dry climate. On 7 January, it's Genna (Ethiopian Orthodox Christmas according to the Julian calendar) = massive pilgrimage to Lalibela, exceptional religious atmosphere, Saint George's nocturnal ceremonies, thousands of pilgrims in white. From July-August drop to 4.6/10 due to the rainy season: 99-100% rainfall, cut tracks to Lalibela. The 11 churches divided into 2 groups by the symbolic "Jordan River": Bete Medhane Alem (the largest, 33.7m × 23.7m × 11.5m deep, "Saviour of the World"), Bete Maryam (Holy Mary, original frescoes), Bete Golgotha (women forbidden, supposedly contains the tomb of King Lalibela), Bete Giyorgis (Saint George, the most famous and best preserved, in the shape of an irreproachable Greek cross, 12m deep, signature photo of Ethiopia). All are dug from top to bottom in the basaltic rock ("top-down" unique technique), connected by tunnels and trenches. Still in religious activity, Ethiopian Orthodox priests officiate daily, pilgrims lodge nearby. Logistics: Lalibela 1h by plane from Addis Ababa (daily direct flight), 2-3 days minimum recommended. Hotels: Mountain View, Maribela, Tukul Village (traditional cabin style). Try injera, doro wat (spicy chicken festival dish, Ethiopian signature), shiro (legume puree), Ethiopian coffee.
victoria-falls:en: Victoria Falls, on the Zimbabwean side, offers the most famous access to the falls: Knife Edge bridge, panoramic view from Main Falls. May is my favourite month (9.4/10): 14-27°C, 0% rain, period when the falls just come out of their peak and stay powerful. From April to July, it's the high water season: maximum volume (10 million litres/second), mist so dense that the view can be totally obstructed some days. Devil's Pool closed. From August to October (low water), the flow drops but visibility becomes maximum: it's the period of Devil's Pool on the Livingstone side (but most of the flow is on the Zimbabwe side). From November to March, expect 20-31°C and 31-68% rainfall: humid heat, but re-greened landscapes. The Zimbabwean economic situation remains complicated: plan cash USD. Double-entry visa recommended to easily cross to Zambia side. Hwange National Park 2h away for elephants in abundance.
livingstone:en: Livingstone, Zambian side of Victoria Falls, offers calmer and more natural access than Victoria Falls (Zimbabwe side). May is my favourite month (9.4/10): 14-27°C, 0% rain, just after the rains where the falls are at full power. From April to July, it's the high water season: maximum volume (10 million litres/second at peak), but mist so dense that you sometimes see nothing from the edge. The Devil's Pool on Livingstone Island is closed during this period. From August to October (dry season, low water), it's the opposite: maximum visibility, bath in the Devil's Pool (natural pool at the edge of the precipice, 100€), but flow visually less impressive. From November to March (rainy season), expect 20-31°C and 35-69% rainfall: humid heat, some lodges close, but re-greened landscapes. Chobe National Park in Botswana 2h away for elephants in abundance. Flight from Lusaka, Johannesburg or Nairobi.
san-antonio:en: San Antonio, in Texas: 1.5 million inhabitants (7th largest city in the USA), founded by the Spanish in 1718, the most historic "Hispanic" city in the USA after Saint Augustine. Place of the Battle of the Alamo (23 February-6 March 1836, the founding event of Texan identity, 187 Texan defenders killed by Santa Anna's Mexican troops). October is my favourite month (8.7/10): 19-29°C, 25% rain, ideal temperatures after the summer heatwave. From April to June and October-November are the best seasons: 13-30°C. From July-August drop to 5.8-5.9/10 due to the extreme heat (25-36°C, sometimes 40°C+), oppressive humidity typical of Texas. From December to February, expect 6-21°C: mild for the south, but brutal variations possible (norther winds that can drop the temperature 20°C in a few hours). Highlights: The Alamo (Spanish Franciscan mission 1718 transformed into fortress 1835, restored as monument since 1905 by the Daughters of the Republic of Texas, 2.5 million visitors/year, one of the most emotionally charged sites in American history, free), San Antonio Missions National Historical Park (UNESCO 2015, 4 Spanish missions + the Alamo, 12 km to cycle along the San Antonio River: Concepción, San José, San Juan, Espada), River Walk ("Paseo del Río", 24 km of developed promenade along the river 1 level below the street, restaurants, bars, gondolas, opened 1939-41 as part of the New Deal, one of the most successful urban projects in the USA), Tower of the Americas (227m, panoramic view), Pearl District (former brewery converted into trendy gastronomic zone), Mercado (Market Square, the largest Mexican market outside Mexico). Fiesta San Antonio (10 days late April, annual festival since 1891 celebrating Hispanic heritage). Excursions: Hill Country to the north-west (hilly landscapes, Texan wines, lavender fields, Fredericksburg town founded by Germans), Austin 1h30 away. Try Tex-Mex (origin of the genre), Texan barbecue, breakfast tacos, margaritas (the frozen "Texan margarita" allegedly invented in San Antonio in 1971).
anchorage:en: Anchorage, largest city in Alaska: 290,000 inhabitants (40% of the state population, which has 730,000 on 1.7 million km² — a density comparable to Siberia). Located at the bottom of the Cook Inlet, surrounded by the Chugach Mountains and the Knik Arm. Air hub, base for exploring wild Alaska. June-July are my favourite months (7.9/10): 10-20°C, 30-37% rain, this is the "endless day" (19h of light at the 21 June solstice, never complete night), hiking, kayaking, whale watching, salmon fishing. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 7-20°C, hotel prices x3-x4 (Inside Passage cruises, Denali NP). From November to March, expect -10/2°C and 30-44% precipitation often as snow: this is the high season for the Northern Lights (visible 200 nights/year, but Anchorage is less well placed than Fairbanks further north), short polar night (5-6h of light in December). Iditarod (dog sled race, 1600 km Anchorage-Nome, departure early March). Highlights: Anchorage Museum (Alaskan history, indigenous art), Tony Knowles Coastal Trail (18 km of coastal cycle path, moose and beluga observation), Earthquake Park (memorial of the Good Friday earthquake 1964, magnitude 9.2, the 2nd most powerful ever recorded). Excursions: Denali National Park 4h by road (Mt. Denali 6190m, the highest summit in North America, grizzly bears, moose), Kenai Fjords National Park 2h30 away (cruise from Seward, glaciers, humpback whales), Wasilla (Sarah Palin's hometown), Talkeetna (Denali expedition base village). Try wild salmon (king salmon the most prestigious), halibut, reindeer sausage, Alaska beer. Direct flight from Seattle/Portland (3h30).
saint-augustine:en: Saint Augustine, in Florida: 14,000 inhabitants, founded by the Spanish in 1565 ("San Agustín"), the oldest continuously occupied European-founded city in the USA (42 years before Jamestown 1607 and 55 years before Plymouth 1620). Preserved historic centre, unique colonial Spanish atmosphere in North America. May is my favourite month (8.4/10): 21-27°C, 28% rain, end of spring with ideal temperatures. From April to June and October-November are the best seasons: 18-29°C, mild climate. From June to September, expect 24-30°C: heavy and humid heat, real cyclone risk (east coast of Florida exposed), reduced hotel prices. From December to February, expect 11-22°C: mild for Florida, numerous American snowbirds (Canadians and inhabitants of the US north-east flee winter). Highlights: Castillo de San Marcos (the oldest stone fortress in the USA, built 1672-1695 by the Spanish in coquina = soft stone composed of shells, which absorbs cannonballs rather than cracking, intact after 33 sieges, national park), Spanish old town (Saint George Street pedestrian street, reconstituted colonial casas, Plaza de la Constitución the oldest urban square in the USA), Lightner Museum (formerly Hôtel Alcazar 1888, art deco), Flagler College (formerly Ponce de Leon Hotel 1888, Henry Flagler railroad magnate, luxurious Hispano-Moorish architecture, Tiffany stained glass, guided tour), Lighthouse Museum (1874 lighthouse, 219 steps), St. Augustine Distillery, Fountain of Youth Archaeological Park (legend of the fountain of youth sought by Juan Ponce de León 1513, first European landing on the continent). Beaches: St. Augustine Beach and Anastasia State Park (soft sand, dunes). Excursions: Daytona Beach 1h away, Orlando 2h away (Disney parks). Try minorcan clam chowder (local spicy chowder), datil pepper, fried shrimp. 1h30 from Jacksonville, 2h from Orlando.
telluride:en: Telluride is the former Colorado mining town turned isolated and smart ski resort: only Route 145 leads here, no motorway. Sunken valley at 2660m, dominated by the San Juan Mountains. June-July are my favourite months (8.1-8.3/10): 3-22°C, 30-51% rain in storms. Good for the festivals (Telluride Film Festival, Bluegrass) and the hike to Bridal Veil Falls (the highest in Colorado). From December to March, expect -12/3°C: this is ski season, dry snow, limited crowds vs Vail or Aspen, off-piste accessible from Gold Hill. The free gondola between the town and Mountain Village remains the only one in the world managed as public transport. From October-November and April-May, it's the shoulder season: everything closes, capricious weather. Direct flight Telluride Regional Airport (few frequencies) or Montrose 1h30 away. Ski-in-ski-out hotels at 500-2000$/night in high season.
steamboat-springs:en: Steamboat Springs, Colorado, is the historic ski resort: 1900m altitude, light and abundant snow (the term "champagne powder" was invented here). Real town of 12,000 inhabitants (not an artificial resort). July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 12-29°C, 11% rain, good for the hike to Fish Creek Falls and swimming in the natural hot springs (Strawberry Park, Old Town Hot Springs). From December to March, expect -10/1°C: cult ski season. The Steamboat Resort area (1300 hectares) is less extensive than Vail or Park City but the snow quality remains renowned among the best in the world. From November to April, the Strings Music Festival animates the town. Direct flight Yampa Valley Airport (Hayden) 30 minutes away or Denver 3h by road via Rabbit Ears Pass.
mammoth-mountain:en: Mammoth Mountain is the reference ski resort of the Californian Sierra Nevada: 3370m summit, season among the longest in North America (sometimes November to July in good years). July is my favourite month (9.4/10): 10-25°C, 9% rain, clear sky for hikes towards Devils Postpile, Rainbow Falls, Convict Lake. From December to March, expect -9/2°C with 30-36% precipitation (often in big storms): this is ski season. The resort belongs to the Ikon Pass area. From June to September, it's the summer season: mountain bike (Mammoth Bike Park), trout fishing (Crowley Lake), access to Mono Lake and Yosemite via Tioga Pass (road closed October to May-June depending on snowfall). From April to May, it's the transition: ski at altitude possible, but lower roads deserted. Flight from LA-Mammoth Yosemite Airport (seasonal) or 5h road from Los Angeles.
stowe:en: Stowe, historic ski resort in Vermont (USA): 4500 inhabitants at 220m altitude (Mount Mansfield summit reaches 1340m, highest point of Vermont), nicknamed "the ski capital of the American East" since the 1930s-40s. More traditional and more accessible than Vail/Aspen but less snowy. July is my favourite month (8.3/10): 16-26°C, 48% rain, Long Trail hiking (long-distance trail crossing Vermont), mild summer, festivals (Stowe Performing Arts Series). From September to October, it's the foliage season (autumn colours): 0-21°C, 30-43% rain, one of the legendary destinations in North America to observe golden and red leaves (sugar maples, birches). Peak mid-October. From December to March, expect -12/4°C and 46-51% precipitation often as snow (5-7m/season, snow conserved by glacial temperatures): ski high season. Stowe Mountain Resort pistes (managed by Vail Resorts since 2017, part of the Epic Pass): 116 pistes, 13 lifts, vertical drop 720m, 2 connected summits (Mount Mansfield + Spruce Peak). Legendary pistes: Front Four (the 4 cult difficult pistes: Goat, Lift Line, National, Starr — the most demanding in the American East). Stowe Recreation Path = 8 km of cycle/pedestrian path in the valley, one of the most beautiful in North America. Audience: Bostonians, New Yorkers (Stowe is one of the favourite resorts of well-off East Coast families), historic skier heritage (von Trapp Family of "The Sound of Music" who settled here after their flight from Austria in 1938). Hotels: Trapp Family Lodge (5*, founded by the von Trapps 1942, now brewery too), Stowe Mountain Lodge, Topnotch Resort, 200-1000$/night. Try Vermont maple syrup (the best maple syrup in the world according to Vermonters), Ben & Jerry's ice cream (origin in neighbouring Burlington), Vermont craft beers (The Alchemist Heady Topper, Hill Farmstead = the most prized beers in the world). Logistics: Stowe 5h from Boston by car, 6h from NY, Burlington airport (BTV) 50 min away.
killington:en: Killington, in Vermont, is the largest ski resort in the American East: 6 interconnected mountains, 155 pistes. Particularly long season (October to May in some years) thanks to an advanced snow cannon system. July is my favourite month (7.7/10): 11-20°C, 48% rain, good for the hike to Killington Peak and Camel's Hump. From December to March, expect -16/-2°C: ski season. The resort hosts the women's Alpine Ski World Cup in late November (Killington Cup giant slalom), 1st American stage. From April to May and October-November, it's the slow shoulder season, except for the start of the World Cup season. Vermont in autumn (foliage, mid-September to mid-October) is another reason to come: maple colours. Boston 3h by road, NYC 4h30. More accessible to New Yorkers than the Colorado Rockies.
sun-valley:en: Sun Valley, Idaho, is the oldest ski resort in North America (1936), created by Averell Harriman for his Union Pacific railway. Historic resort that invented the chairlift. July is my favourite month (9.9/10): 10-28°C, 10% rain, dry sky (semi-arid altitude climate, 1750m). Ideal for hikes in the Sawtooth Mountains and swimming in natural hot springs (Goldbug, Boat Box, Russian John). From December to March, expect -11/0°C: ski season on Bald Mountain (Baldy, 1052m vertical drop) and Dollar Mountain (beginners). Dry altitude snow, limited crowds vs Aspen or Park City. From April to May and October-November, it's the shoulder season: resort semi-closed. Ketchum, the neighbouring village (Hemingway died here), keeps a rustic-smart charm. Ernest Hemingway is buried in the Ketchum cemetery. Direct flight Sun Valley Airport (Hailey) or Boise 2h30 by road.
catane:en: Catania, 2nd city of Sicily: 310,000 inhabitants at the foot of the Etna (3357m, the highest active volcano in Europe, in near-permanent eruption). Town rebuilt after the 1693 earthquake (60,000 deaths, 17 towns destroyed in eastern Sicily) in black Etna lava stone and white limestone, giving its characteristic baroque chequered black-white appearance. UNESCO 2002 (Val di Noto). June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 21-29°C, 7% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-33°C, frequent sirocco sometimes reaches 45°C in the open air with sand wind). September stays excellent (8.9/10). From November to March, expect 8-20°C and 21-31% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, snow on Etna in winter (skiing possible December-March on 2 stations Etna south and Etna north). Catania highlights: Piazza del Duomo (central baroque square, Fontana dell'Elefante = lava elephant Liotru = symbol of the city, Sant'Agata Cathedral dedicated to the patron saint), Via Etnea (main 3 km street leading towards the Etna, perspectives on the volcano), Roman Theatre (1st century BC in black lava, surprising conservation), Pescheria Quarter (the liveliest, daily fish market in the morning), Castello Ursino (13th century, castle of Frederick II), Bellini Theatre (opera). Mount Etna (30 min by car, access Rifugio Sapienza 1900m south side, cable car towards 2500m, 4x4 jeep towards 2900m south crater, mandatory accompaniment to go to the 3357m summit, possibility of snowshoeing excursion in winter, spectacular view in eruption with night lava flows depending on the activity of the moment). Excursions: Taormina 1h away (already covered by BDW), Syracuse 1h away (UNESCO), Valley of the Temples Agrigento 2h30 away (UNESCO), Aeolian Islands by boat from Milazzo. Try pasta alla Norma (aubergine pasta with salted ricotta, signature dish of Catania), arancini (fried rice balls, in Catania they are conical against round in Palermo = internal Sicilian war), Sicilian granita (typical breakfast), cannoli, Etna Rosso wines (Nerello Mascalese grape on volcanic soils). Catania-Fontanarossa airport (CTA).
trieste:en: Trieste, port city in the north-east of Italy on the Slovenian border: 200,000 inhabitants at the bottom of the Adriatic, capital of Friuli-Venezia Giulia. Unique atmosphere mix of Italo-Slavic-Austrian (the city only became Italian in 1918 after remaining Austrian for 500 years 1382-1918, main port of the Austro-Hungarian empire for the Mediterranean). Cosmopolitan cultural centre (James Joyce, Italo Svevo, Umberto Saba, Rainer Maria Rilke lived here). June-July-August are my favourite months (9.0/10): 18-29°C, 25-30% rain, ideal temperatures. From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 9-29°C. From November to March, expect 2-14°C and 32-45% rain: mild for northern Italy, but Trieste is famous for the bora = violent cold north-east wind that can reach 130 km/h in winter, climate signature of the city (taut ropes in the streets to hold on in case of bora peak). Highlights: Piazza Unità d'Italia ("Square of Italian Unity", the largest square open to the sea in Europe, 12,500 m² surrounded by 19th-century Habsburg palaces, unique atmosphere with maritime opening, splendid night illuminations), Castello di Miramare (8 km north-west on promontory, white neo-Gothic castle 1860 built for Archduke Maximilian of Austria — brother of Franz Joseph — before he became Emperor of Mexico 1864 and was shot 1867, gardens, signature sea view), Saint Just Cathedral (San Giusto, 14th-century Romanesque on hill, former Roman forum discovered underneath), Citadel (San Giusto Castle), Roman Theatre (1st century), Canal Grande (navigable canal in the heart of the city bordered by 18th-century palaces), Trieste Synagogue (one of the largest in Central Europe 1912, important Jewish community before Holocaust), Risiera di San Sabba (former Nazi concentration camp 1943-1945, the only German extermination camp on Italian soil, memorial), Caffè San Marco (literary café 1914, James Joyce wrote here, traditional Viennese atmosphere), Grotta Gigante 15 min away (one of the largest tourist caves accessible in the world 100m × 280m × 130m), Castello di Duino (Rilke wrote the Duino Elegies here 1912). Excursions: Slovenia 30 min away (Lipica — Lipizzan stud farm, spectacular Postojna cave), Aquileia 30 min away (UNESCO, paleochristian Roman mosaics), Venice 1h30 by train. Try jota (Slovenian bean sauerkraut soup), Triestine goulash (Hungarian heritage), caffè (Trieste = Italian coffee capital with world headquarters Illy + Hausbrandt, coffee is ordered here in special terms: capo in B = cappuccino in glass, nero = espresso), prosciutto crudo, Terrano wine (intense local red wine).
livigno:en: Livigno, remote Italian alpine valley near the Swiss border, is a historic duty-free zone (alcohol, perfumes, petrol at -50%). Ski resort composed of 2 areas (Mottolino and Carosello) connected. July is my favourite month (7.7/10): 8-19°C, 47% rain, good for mountain bike (Livigno Bike Park, one of the best in Europe). From December to April, expect -12/2°C: ski season. Renowned snowparks (Mottolino hosts the X Games Real Snow). From November and May, it's the shoulder season. Access only by pass roads (Tunnel del Munt la Schera from Switzerland in winter, or via Bormio from Italy). No train station. Nearest airport: Milan-Bergamo 4h away. The valley hosted the Milan-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics for freestyle and snowboard events. Cheaper ski passes than in other Italian alpine resorts.
sestriere:en: Sestrière is part of the Milky Way (Voie Lactée), one of the largest Franco-Italian cross-border areas: 400 km of pistes connected with Montgenèvre, Sansicario and Claviere. Host of the Turin 2006 Winter Olympics. July-August are my favourite months (8.0/10): 6-19°C, 26-30% rain in afternoon storms, good for hiking and mountain bike. From December to April, expect -11/3°C: ski season, reliable snow thanks to altitude (2035m, one of the highest Italian resorts). Dry continental climate, pistes mainly north-facing. From May and October-November, it's the slow shoulder season: resort semi-closed. Turin 1h30 by car, Briançon 1h via the Montgenèvre pass. The resort has aged architecturally, less charming than Cortina or Bormio, but the skiing remains excellent and the passes affordable.
courmayeur:en: Courmayeur, ski resort and holiday destination of the Aosta Valley in Italy at the foot of Mont Blanc on the Italian side: 2800 inhabitants at 1224m. Smarter and less technical than Chamonix on the French side of the other side of the Mont Blanc Tunnel. Connected to Chamonix by the Aiguille du Midi-Pointe Helbronner (trans-alpine panoramic Skyway Monte Bianco cable car). July is my favourite month (8.7/10): 12-24°C, 33% rain, ideal temperatures for alpine hiking and mountaineering. August stays excellent (8.5/10). From June to September, it's the summer high season: 8-24°C, mountaineering, Tour du Mont Blanc hiking, paragliding, 9-hole golf course (one of the highest in Europe). From December to March, expect -6/7°C and 41-44% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 100 km Courmayeur Mont Blanc Funivie (modest compared to the 600 km of Portes du Soleil or 3 Valleys, but excellent snow quality thanks to altitude 1224-2755m, spectacular Mont Blanc-Grandes Jorasses landscapes), Plan Chécrouit (main side, accessible by 5-min cable car from Courmayeur), Val Vény (calmer north side and sunny in the afternoon). Off-piste: Courmayeur is one of the heli-ski bases in Europe with Mont Blanc Helicopters (flights from Toula, Punta Helbronner or Glacier du Géant for descents of the Vallée Blanche on the Italian Toula side 2400m vertical drop in 1 descent, 350-500€/person). Skyway Monte Bianco (panoramic 360° rotating cable car inaugurated 2015, 3 sections from Courmayeur Pontal d'Entrèves 1300m to Pavillon 2173m then Punta Helbronner 3466m in 19 min, exceptional Mont Blanc-Matterhorn-Grand Combin-Mont Rose panorama, arrival station with panoramic restaurant, geological observatory = one of the most impressive alpine attractions in Europe, high price 60€ round trip but unique experience). Highlights: Aosta Valley (the smallest region of Italy, historically Franco-Provençal people, French linguistic autonomy protected), Aosta 30 min away (provincial capital with Augustus Roman arch 1st century BC, 2000 years of history), Saint-Pierre castle (12th century), Mont Blanc Tunnel (5 min by car, 11.6 km, opened 1965, exit Chamonix France 25 min after entry to Courmayeur). Audience: Northern Italians in the majority (Milanese, Turinese on weekends), European aristocratic families, few British (prefer Chamonix), refined non-festive Italian atmosphere (unlike Mayrhofen or Ischgl in Austria). Hotels: Grand Hotel Royal e Golf (5*+ historic 1854 where Hemingway, Pavarotti stayed), Auberge de la Maison, Hôtel Pavillon, 250-1500€/night. Try fonduta valdostana (Aosta Valley fondue with Fontina DOP cheese), polenta concia, Arnad lard DOP, Saint-Marcel ham, Aosta wines (one of the smallest vineyards in Italy, indigenous grape varieties Petit Rouge, Petit Arvine), Génépy (alpine liqueur). Logistics: Courmayeur 30 min from Chamonix by tunnel, 1h from Geneva by tunnel, 2h from Turin via the Aosta Valley.
perouse:en: Perugia, capital of Umbria in central Italy: 165,000 inhabitants at 500m altitude on a hill. Former Etruscan capital then medieval town, rich in Gothic churches and palaces. Made worldwide famous by Perugina chocolate (creator of Baci Perugina 1922 = hazelnut-filled chocolates wrapped in a blue star with a small love quote). University town (Università per Stranieri di Perugia since 1925 = the oldest university for foreigners in Italy). July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 18-31°C, 15% rain, ideal temperatures. June-August stay excellent (9.0/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 6-31°C. From November to March, expect 0-13°C and 30-38% rain: mild for the altitude, few tourists (except festivals like Eurochocolate in October = the most famous chocolate festival in Italy since 1993, 1 million visitors over 9 days). Highlights: Piazza IV Novembre (central medieval square, signature Fontana Maggiore = Gothic fountain 1278 by Nicola and Giovanni Pisano), Perugia Cathedral (San Lorenzo, 14th-15th century Gothic, houses the "Sacred Ring" = relic of Mary and Joseph's marriage), Palazzo dei Priori (Gothic town hall 13th-14th century, houses the Galleria Nazionale dell'Umbria with collection of Umbrian art: Pietro Vannucci called "Il Perugino" master of Raphael — born in Città della Pieve near Perugia), Collegio del Cambio (15th-century exchange chamber with frescoes by Il Perugino 1496-1500), Rocca Paolina (papal fortress 1540 buried and accessible by escalators — underground quarter unique in the world, razed Baglioni palaces buried), Etruscan Well (3rd century BC Etruscan well 37m deep), Etruscan Arch (Arco Etrusco, 3rd century BC, one of the only Etruscan arches entirely preserved), Augustus Arch. Umbria excursions: Assisi 30 min away (UNESCO, birthplace of Saint Francis and Saint Clare, basilicas with Giotto frescoes), Spoleto 1h away (lyric festival), Orvieto 1h away (magnificent Gothic cathedral on volcanic tuff), Lake Trasimene 30 min away (4th largest lake in Italy), Marmore Falls 1h away (165m Roman artificial waterfall, the highest in Europe), Norcia (Italian capital of charcuterie, ham and black truffle), Civita di Bagnoregio ("dying town", perched eroding village). Major festival: Umbria Jazz in July (one of the largest jazz festivals in Europe since 1973). Try Norcia black truffles, prosciutto di Norcia IGP, Baci Perugina, torta al testo (Umbrian flatbread), Sagrantino di Montefalco DOCG wines, Orvieto Classico white wines.
adelaide:en: Adelaide, capital of South Australia: 1.3 million inhabitants, calmer city than Sydney/Melbourne, British urban design with green belt (Adelaide Park Lands), capital of Australian wines. January is my favourite month (9.6/10): 16-28°C, 14% rain, ideal austral summer. From June to August (austral winter), expect 8-14°C and 43-44% rain: cold and humid for Australia. December-March are the high festival season: Adelaide Festival of Arts and Fringe Festival (the 2nd largest arts festival in the world after Edinburgh) in February-March. Highlights: Adelaide Central Market (since 1869), Art Gallery of South Australia, Adelaide Botanic Garden, North Terrace (museums). Beaches: Glenelg (by tram from the centre), Henley Beach. Wine excursions: Barossa Valley 1h away (Shiraz, Penfolds Grange), McLaren Vale 45 min away (Shiraz, Cabernet), Adelaide Hills (Pinot Noir, Chardonnay), Clare Valley 2h away (Riesling). Kangaroo Island 2h by ferry (endemic wildlife, Remarkable Rocks, Seal Bay). Coober Pedy 8h away (underground opal town). Try pie floater (pie on split pea soup, local dish), German pastries (Hahndorf German village). Adelaide is less frequented by Europeans than Sydney/Melbourne, more local.
brisbane:en: Brisbane is calmer than Sydney or Melbourne: capital of Queensland without the crowds, river that meanders, subtropical climate. November is my favourite month (8.7/10): 18-28°C, 38% rain in brief showers, 13h days. From December to March, expect 20-30°C with 40-54% precipitation: subtropical wet season, sometimes severe floods (2011, 2022). From June to August (austral winter), plan 10-22°C and 17-23% rain: mild climate, ideal for South Bank, Story Bridge climb, Mount Coot-tha. Brisbane mainly serves as a base: Gold Coast 1h away (Surfers Paradise, Burleigh), Sunshine Coast 1h30 away (Noosa, Glass House Mountains), Stradbroke Island and Moreton Island by ferry for dolphins/dugongs. The Fortitude Valley and West End café scene deserves exploration. Direct flights from Singapore, Doha, LA.
whitsundays:en: The Whitsundays are 74 tropical Queensland islands at the heart of the Great Barrier Reef: Whitehaven Beach (98% silica white sand, among the most beautiful beaches in the world), Heart Reef (heart-shaped reef). October is my favourite month (8.8/10): 22-25°C, 23% rain, end of the dry season, calm sea, maximum visibility. From November to April, it's the wet season: 40-66% rainfall, possible cyclones (historic category 5), irukandji jellyfish (stinger suit or netted beach). From May to October, it's the dry season: 23-40% rain, ideal. Major activities: Whitehaven Beach (boat excursion from Airlie Beach, 7 km of white sand, Hill Inlet for the signature aerial photo of the swirls), Heart Reef (only seen by plane or helicopter, panoramic flights 200-400$), diving and snorkelling (day outings or 3-7 day liveaboard), Hamilton Island and Hayman Island (luxury resorts), Daydream Island. Airlie Beach = base on the continent (animation, artificial lagoon as no swimmable urban beach due to crocs/jellyfish). Catamaran cruises 2-3 days (300-700$) very popular. Try coral trout, mud crab, prawns. Flight from Brisbane to Hamilton Island or Whitsunday Coast (Proserpine).
uluru:en: Uluru (formerly Ayers Rock) is the signature rock of the Australian Red Centre: 348m high, 9.4 km circumference, UNESCO listed. September is my favourite month (9.4/10): 13-28°C, 8% rain, ideal temperatures before the infernal summer. From December to February, it's the desert summer: 23-38°C with sometimes 45°C, flies everywhere (head nets recommended), numerous tourist deaths from dehydration each year. From June to August (austral winter), expect 6-20°C: glacial nights (sometimes -5°C), ideal days for hiking. April-May and September-October are the real good compromises. Climbing Uluru is forbidden since 2019 (respect for Anangu Aboriginal sacred). The walking tour (10.6 km around) remains open. Highlights: Uluru at sunrise and sunset (spectacular colours), Kata Tjuta (Mount Olga 30 km away, formation of 36 domes, Walpa Gorge or Valley of the Winds hike), Field of Light (Bruce Munro installation, 50,000 lights), Aboriginal Cultural Centre. Logistics: Ayers Rock Airport, shuttle or car to the Yulara resort (only accommodation, no free accommodation in the park). Plan 1500-3000$ for 3-4 days all-inclusive from Sydney/Melbourne. Sacred site: do not photograph some parts (signs).
thredbo:en: Thredbo, ski resort of the Snowy Mountains of Australia in Kosciuszko National Park (NSW): 50 permanent inhabitants (3000 in ski season). One of the main ski resorts in Australia with neighbouring Perisher (the other large area, 40 min from Thredbo), accessible to Sydney and Melbourne skiers on weekends. Australian short ski season = June-October (austral winter). January is my favourite month (8.0/10): 11-21°C, 43% rain, ideal temperatures (austral summer). December-February stay excellent (7.7-7.9/10). From December to April, it's the summer season: 5-21°C, Snowy Mountains hiking, mountain bike, paragliding, ascent of Mount Kosciuszko 2228m = highest point of continental Australia (one of the Seven Summits = continental summits according to Bass classification but not according to Messner who includes Carstensz Papua 4884m), 6.5 km hike from Thredbo Top Station accessible by Kosciuszko Express chairlift = the easiest ascent of the Seven Summits (feasible in half a day, accessible to families). From June to August, it's ski season: -2/4°C and 42-52% precipitation often as snow, austral cold period. Pistes: 50 km Thredbo Resort (modest vs Alps: 600 km 3 Valleys, but one of the largest Australian areas with Perisher 100 km), altitude 1365-2037m, vertical drop 672m = the largest vertical drop in Australia. Signature pistes: Funnel Web (signature black descent), Crackenback Supertrail (the longest piste 5.9 km), Dream Run (family). Particularities: the resort hosts the FIS World Cup Aerials every year = international aerial freestyle ski competition, Australian athletes dominate this sport (Jacqui Cooper, Lydia Lassila Olympic medallists). Audience: Australians in the majority (Sydney 6h by car, Melbourne 6h by car, Canberra 2h30), New Zealand Kiwis, few foreigners (Europeans-Americans prefer Aspen or Chamonix which have 5x more snow and 10x more pistes for the same price). Cost: high prices ($200-300 AUD/day pass, $400-800 AUD/night hotel) as Australian skiing = elite sport. Hotels: Thredbo Alpine Hotel, Lake Crackenback Resort, several lodges. Excursions: Mt Kosciuszko in summer, Perisher 40 min away (other large resort), Jindabyne 30 min away (alpine lake and Snowy Mountains hub), Canberra 2h30 away (Australian capital already covered by BDW), Sydney 6h away. Try meat pie (Australian après-ski tradition), Pavlova (signature Australian-New Zealand dessert), Vegemite (signature Australian fermented spread), Tim Tam (signature chocolate biscuits), local Carlton/James Boag/Coopers beers, Australian Shiraz/Chardonnay wines. Logistics: Thredbo accessible only by car, Canberra airport (CBR) 2h30 away, Sydney airport (SYD) 6h away, Cooma airport (COA) the closest 1h30 away but few flights.
perisher:en: Perisher, largest ski resort in Australia and the southern hemisphere: no significant permanent population, 100% tourist resort in Kosciuszko National Park (NSW). Largest skiable area in Australia (in competition with Treble Cone New Zealand for the southern hemisphere crown), 4 connected villages: Perisher Valley, Smiggin Holes, Blue Cow, Guthega. Short ski season June-September. January-February are my favourite months (7.6/10): 8-19°C, 43-50% rain, ideal temperatures in austral summer. Perisher never tops 7.6/10 (austral alpine climate with narrow season). From December to April, it's the summer season: 4-19°C, Snowy Mountains hiking, mountain bike (developed network of mountain bike trails in summer), fishing in alpine lakes. From June to September, it's ski season: -3/3°C and 42-46% precipitation often as snow. Pistes: 100 km Perisher Resort (the largest area in Australia, 5x larger than neighbouring Thredbo), altitude 1605-2034m, vertical drop 429m (more modest than Thredbo). Signature pistes: Front Valley (family, ideal for beginners), Mount Perisher (summit 2034m), Olympic (red), Pretty Valley. Strengths: 4 villages connected by Skitube = unique Australian underground tunnel train 6 km long to connect the base of the mountain (Bullocks Flat) to the heart of the area (Perisher Valley) avoiding icy roads in winter, unique experience in a ski resort (similar to Funivia del Ghiacciaio at Cervinia but underground). No off-piste officially authorised in Australia (protected national park), but local skiers occasionally practise back-country with skins. Audience: Australians in the majority (Sydney 6h by car, Melbourne 6h, Canberra 2h30), families with children attracted by the Perisher Snowsports ski school (one of the largest ski schools in the world with 600 instructors), few Western foreigners (prefer Aspen Vail Park City Niseko Hokkaido for flights from Asia or Pacific). Cost: high prices ($170-250 AUD/day pass + $400-700 AUD/night hotel), often considered "expensive for what one has" compared to northern hemisphere. Hotels: no in-resort hotels (the NSW Government refused construction of hotels in the national park), all accommodation is in the neighbouring village Jindabyne 30 min away or Bullocks Flat at the foot of the Skitube, which is a major drawback of Perisher vs Thredbo which has hotels at the foot of the slopes. Excursions: Mt Kosciuszko 2228m highest point of continental Australia, Thredbo 40 min away, Jindabyne Lake (kayaking in summer), Canberra 2h30 away, Sydney 6h away. Try meat pie, Pavlova, Australian fries ("chips" in Australia), Aussie beers. Logistics: Canberra airport (CBR) 2h30 away, Cooma-Snowy Mountains airport (COA) 1h30 away the closest, access from Sydney 6h by car, from Melbourne 6h.
nouvelle-zelande:en: New Zealand is one of the most beautiful countries in the world: 5 million inhabitants for 268,000 km² (metropolitan France makes 552,000 km²), 30 times more sheep than inhabitants. January-February are my favourite months (8.1-8.2/10): 15-20°C average national, austral summer, long days, Great Walks (Milford Track, Routeburn Track, Tongariro Alpine Crossing) accessible. From June to August (austral winter), expect 4-13°C: cold with snow at altitude, this is ski season (Queenstown, Wanaka, Mt Ruapehu). March-April and October-November are the best compromises. "4 seasons in one day" climate: plan a warm/waterproof jacket even in midsummer. Top sites: Milford Sound (Fjordland), Aoraki/Mount Cook (3724m, highest summit), Lake Tekapo (Church of the Good Shepherd, Dark Sky starry sky), Franz Josef and Fox glaciers, Rotorua/Taupo (geysers), Wellington, Auckland, Bay of Islands, Hobbiton, Wanaka. Logistics: plan 3-4 weeks minimum to see the essentials (2 islands), road trip in motorhome or car (left-hand), reasonable distances. Domestic flights: Air NZ, Jetstar. Direct flight Paris-Auckland in 24-26h (via Singapore or Doha). Reversed seasons southern hemisphere.
queenstown-ski:en: Queenstown Ski ("Coronet Peak" and "The Remarkables"), ski areas near Queenstown on the South Island of New Zealand in the Otago region: no permanent population (Queenstown base-town 35,000 inhabitants 30 min from the resorts). One of the main ski destinations in the southern hemisphere with Thredbo-Perisher Australia, spectacular alpine-lake landscapes around Lake Wakatipu. Southern hemisphere ski season = June-October. February is my favourite month (7.7/10): 6-22°C, 18% rain, ideal temperatures in austral summer. January-March stay excellent (7.6-7.7/10). From December to April, it's the summer season: 1-22°C, Routeburn-Milford-Greenstone-Caples hiking = signature New Zealand Great Walks, mountain bike, jet boat on the Shotover River = Queenstown signature, bungee jumping (Kawarau Bridge bungee = birthplace of world tourist bungee 1988 by AJ Hackett), parachute jump. From June to October, it's ski season: -1/9°C and 23-43% precipitation often as snow. Pistes: 280 ha combined (Coronet Peak 280 ha + The Remarkables 220 ha + Cardrona 1h away + Treble Cone 1h away, one of the largest southern hemisphere ski areas), altitude 1187-1957m (The Remarkables summit 2381m but ski to 1957m), vertical drop 437m. Coronet Peak (the closest to Queenstown 25 min, signature night skiing Thursdays-Saturdays 4pm-9pm under floodlights), The Remarkables (40 min, more difficult and higher in altitude, spectacular panoramas over Queenstown + Lake Wakatipu). Signature pistes: Greengates ("The Big One" Coronet Peak black descent), Sugar Bowl The Remarkables off-piste accessible. Off-piste: Queenstown is one of the most respected southern hemisphere heli-ski bases, operators Harris Mountains Heliski + Methven Heliski offering virgin descents in the New Zealand Southern Alps backcountry (July-September seasons), high prices 800-1500 NZD/day. Queenstown town: nicknamed "world capital of adventure", young-festive cosmopolitan atmosphere (international clientele 70% foreign, especially British and Australians), dynamic restaurants, nightlife. Activities: Shotover jet boat on the rapids at 80 km/h in narrow canyons = signature Queenstown experience since 1965, Kawarau Bridge bungee jumping (43m, the 1st commercial in the world 1988), Queenstown skydive, TSS Earnslaw cruise (1912 steamer on Lake Wakatipu to Walter Peak Sheep Station = visit to typical New Zealand sheep farm), Lord of the Rings tours (Glenorchy filming locations Isengard-Lothlorien-Argonath). Essential excursions: Milford Sound 4h away (UNESCO Te Wahipounamu, spectacular fjord nicknamed "8th wonder of the world" by Rudyard Kipling, Milford Sound cruise + signature Stirling Falls waterfall), Wanaka 1h away (peaceful neighbouring lake, more traditional atmosphere), Mt Cook National Park 3h away (highest point Aoraki 3724m), Fox Glacier and Franz Josef Glacier 5h away (spectacular west coast). Try New Zealand lamb (the best in the world), New Zealand king salmon, lamb pie, Pavlova (national dessert), L&P (kiwi lemonade), craft beers Speights/Mac's, New Zealand wines Pinot Noir Central Otago + Sauvignon Blanc Marlborough. Queenstown airport (ZQN), domestic flights + direct Australia Sydney-Brisbane-Melbourne.
big-white:en: Big White, in British Columbia in Canada, is the ski resort known for the quality of its snow: deep dry powder, snowghosts (frost-covered firs) on the summits. At 1755m altitude. Family resort, not smart like Whistler. July is my favourite month (8.3/10): 7-21°C, 22% rain, good for mountain bike at Big White Bike Park and the hike to Rhonda Lake. From December to March, expect -12/0°C: cult ski season. The integrated resort (everything is ski-in-ski-out) makes life easier for families. Pistes rather wide and marked. From April to November, resort semi-closed. Kelowna 1h by road, the main airport. The Okanagan region in summer (July-September) compensates with lakes, vineyards and fresh fruit. Day pass at 100-130 CAD, more accessible than Whistler or Banff.
revelstoke:en: Revelstoke, ski resort in British Columbia (Canada) in the Selkirk Mountains: 7500 inhabitants at 450m, historic stop town on the Canadian Pacific transcontinental railway (1885). Modern ski resort opened in 2007, already among the best in North America for freeriders. July-August are my favourite months (8.9/10): 13-26°C, 27-35% rain, Selkirk Mountains hiking, kayaking on Columbia River, mountain bike. From June to September, expect 4-26°C: very active summer season (Revelstoke is also a summer adventure hub: mountain bike, kayak, climbing, hiking). From December to March, expect -6/6°C and 56-61% precipitation often as snow (12-15m/season, among the snowiest in North America): ski high season. Revelstoke Mountain Resort pistes: 75 pistes, 5 lifts, record vertical drop in North America 1713m (the largest on the continent), altitude 512-2225m. Cat skiing and heli skiing: Revelstoke is one of the world spots for heli-ski (off-piste from helicopter, 30,000-100,000m of vertical drop/day with guide, prices 800-2000$/day). CMH Heli Skiing = world pioneer of heli-ski since 1965. Audience: advanced freeriders from around the world, ski professionals, few beginners (Revelstoke is known for its committed black and expert pistes). Hotels: Sutton Place Hotel Revelstoke Mountain Resort, Best Western Plus, private lodges nearby, 150-600 CAD/night. Glacier National Park nearby (Rogers Pass, 4-5m of snow/year, road regularly closed for avalanches). Try bison, Sockeye salmon, micro-brewery beers (Mt. Begbie Brewing). Logistics: Revelstoke 6h from Vancouver by car, 4h from Calgary, regional airport + transfer.
marbella:en: Marbella is the smart beach resort of the Costa del Sol: international jet-set, luxury marinas, golf, evenings on Puerto Banús beach. June is my favourite month (9.8/10): 20-27°C, 3% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-30°C) and especially the crowds: hotel prices x3, full restaurants, packed beaches. From December to February, expect 10-17°C and 20-24% rain: mild for Spain, European golfers present year-round (50+ courses in the region). Highlights: Casco Antiguo (charming old town with Plaza de los Naranjos), Puerto Banús (jet-set marina, 50M€ yachts, Gucci-Prada-Cartier boutiques, expensive restaurants), Paseo Maritimo (10 km of seaside promenade), Playa de Cabopino (the most natural). Evenings: Nikki Beach, Ocean Club, Olivia Valère. Excursions: Ronda 1h away, Gibraltar 1h away, Tangier by ferry from Algeciras (1h by car + 1h by ferry). Try espetos (sardines grilled on a wooden stake on the beach), pescaíto frito, Malaga wine. May and September-October are the best crowd/weather compromises.
alcudia:en: Alcúdia, town-resort in the north-east of Mallorca in the Spanish Balearics: 21,000 inhabitants 50 km from Palma. Unique combination of a Roman and medieval fortified old town with its 1.4 km of preserved medieval ramparts, and a 12 km beach at one of the longest beaches in Europe at Port d'Alcúdia. June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 20-28°C, 14% rain, sea at 21°C, ideal temperatures before the summer heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (22-31°C) and the extreme crowds (Germans in the majority, hotel prices x3-x4, 12 km beach congested but remains workable as very long). September-October stay excellent (8.4-8.6/10). From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 21-30% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting without crowds (golf and beach resort hotels often closed). Highlights: Old town of Alcúdia ("Casc Antic" = Old City, fortified by 26 towers and 1.4 km of largely intact 14th-century medieval ramparts, accessible on foot on the ramparts, preserved Mediterranean atmosphere, maze of paved alleys with cafés-restaurants), Pollentia (vestiges of the Roman city founded 123 BC, one of the oldest towns in Mallorca still inhabited, 1st-century Roman theatre, archaeological excavations south of the ramparts), Port d'Alcúdia (modern marina with 750 mooring places, marina + ferry to Ciutadella Menorca), Alcúdia Beach (12 km of fine white sand facing north-east of Mallorca, protected dunes, European Blue Flag since 1996), Muro Beach (south-west extension of Alcúdia beach, wider white sand, naturist on some sections), Cap Pinar (to the north, wild peninsula, hikes towards Mirador de la Victòria panorama over Alcúdia bay, Alcanada beach 5*+ golf), Coves de Sant Martí (natural cave where a chapel was dug 14th century), Sant Jaume Church (13th-14th century Gothic in the centre of the Old Town). Excursions: Cap de Formentor 30 min away (extreme north of Mallorca, spectacular 300m cliffs, hairpin road, 1892 lighthouse, Formentor beach), Pollença 15 min away (neighbouring medieval town-village with 365 steps of the Calvari, viewpoint), Sa Calobra 1h away (legendary hairpin road in the Tramuntana mountains), Caves del Drach 1h30 away (at Porto Cristo, one of the largest underground lakes in the world), Soller and Valldemossa 1h30 away (UNESCO Tramuntana). Activities: beach, water sports, cycling (the island is a paradise for cycling tourism with 1200 km of cycle paths and many pros training in Mallorca March-May), Hidropark Aqua Park, golf Alcanada Golf Club 5*+ Robert Trent Jones Jr 2003. Try ensaimada de Mallorca, sobrasada (spicy black Mallorcan pork charcuterie), pa amb oli, tumbet, Binissalem DO wines, Pla i Llevant DO wines. Palma de Mallorca airport (PMI) 50 min by car.
faro:en: Faro is the gateway to the Algarve: main airport in the south of Portugal, but often crossed without really stopping. The old town (Vila Adentro) nevertheless deserves 2-3h. July is my favourite month (9.7/10): 20-29°C, 1% rain, sea at 22°C. The whole Algarve becomes workable: Praia da Marinha, Benagil cave, Lagos. From December to March, expect 10-18°C and 20-32% rain. Always portable and touristically empty, ideal for the Ria Formosa (protected lagoon, winter ornithology, pink flamingos). It has become a growing digital nomad destination (Lagos, Tavira) with coworkings and an important French community. May-June and September-October are the real compromises: 14-26°C, little rain, reduced crowds, reasonable rates. Sevilla 2h by car (Spain) complements a stay well.
alentejo:en: Alentejo, region of southern Portugal between the Tagus in the north and the Algarve in the south: 700,000 inhabitants on 31,600 km² (1/3 of Portuguese territory but 7% of the population, one of the least densely populated European regions). "Beyond the Tagus" translation of the name Alentejo = "além do Tejo", landscapes of white plains planted with olive trees + cork oaks (Portugal = world's leading cork producer) + vineyards + signature lime-whitewashed houses. Preserved and slow atmosphere, traditional alternative to the touristic Portuguese coasts. June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 14-30°C, 5% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. May-September stay excellent (7.4-8.7/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 8-32°C, ideal dry Mediterranean climate. From July to August, expect 17-32°C with possible 40°C+ heatwave. From November to March, expect 4-17°C and 23-38% rain: mild for southern Europe, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Évora (UNESCO 1986, capital of Alentejo, Roman-medieval-Renaissance museum-town preserved, unique atmosphere whitewashed in lime ochre signature, Roman Temple of Diana 1st century AD one of the best preserved on the Iberian peninsula, Évora Cathedral 13th-century Gothic-Renaissance, Bone Chapel Capela dos Ossos 17th-century macabre baroque built in human bones from 5000 skeletons signature troubling visual with inscription "We bones who are here, await yours"), Mouraria of Évora former Moorish quarter, University of Évora 1559 (the 2nd oldest university in Portugal after Coimbra), Aqueduct da Água de Prata 16th century, Marvão spectacular fortified village atop a 860m hill Spanish border, exceptional 360° panorama, preserved medieval atmosphere, classified "most beautiful village in Portugal", Monsaraz medieval fortified village on panoramic hill overlooking Lake Alqueva (the largest artificial lake in Western Europe 250 km², created dam 2002, other top world dark sky reserve = the largest star observation zone in Western Europe, certified Starlight Tourism Destination), Beja capital of lower Alentejo, Mértola preserved Moorish village, Évoramonte small picturesque fortified village, Castelo de Vide and Portalegre (northern Alentejo), Vicentine Coast south-west Atlantic wild with preserved beaches Comporta ("Portuguese Hamptons" smart resort since 2010 attracting French celebrities Carla Bruni-Christian Louboutin-Madonna has a house) + Vila Nova de Milfontes + Zambujeira do Mar + Aljezur, South-West Alentejo and Costa Vicentina Natural Reserve (the largest natural reserve in continental Portugal). Atlantic beaches of Alentejo = preserved alternative to the touristic Algarve: less frequented, cooler water thanks to the cold Canaries current (16-19°C even in summer against 22-24°C Algarve), renowned surfing, wild and romantic atmosphere. Alentejo wines = AOC Alentejo (signature powerful wines with Aragonez + Trincadeira + Alicante Bouschet + Touriga Nacional grape varieties, one of the most renowned Portuguese vineyards with Douro). Try migas alentejanas (signature herb-fried bread bread), porco preto (signature Portuguese black Pata Negra pork), açorda (bread soup), gaspacho alentejano (different from Andalusian, more rustic), queijo de Serpa AOC (signature sheep cheese), pão alentejano (traditional round bread), Alentejo wines, Aguardente.
peloponnese:en: Peloponnese (Πελοπόννησος), large peninsula of southern Greece connected to mainland Greece by the Isthmus of Corinth: 1.15 million inhabitants on 21,549 km². Cradle of ancient Greek civilisation = legendary sites of Olympia + Mycenae + Sparta + Epidaurus + Mistra. Geographically separated from the continent since 1893 when the Corinth Canal was dug (6.3 km × 24.6m × 79m deep, one of the most spectacular canals in the world, dizzying artificial gorge signature visual of modern Greece). June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 18-29°C, 16% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. May-September stay excellent (8.9-9.0/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 10-33°C, ideal Mediterranean climate. From July to August, expect 22-33°C with possible 40°C+ heatwave. From November to March, expect 4-19°C and 33-40% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, snow possible at Mt Taygetos 2407m. Highlights: Olympia (UNESCO 1989, ancient sacred town of Olympia where the ancient Olympic Games were held 776 BC-393 AD every 4 years for 1170 years = universal cultural signature, Temple of Zeus with columns collapsed by earthquake 522 AD + chryselephantine statue of Zeus = one of the 7 Wonders of the ancient world today disappeared, Ancient Stadium where the modern Olympic flame is lit by the rays of the sun in a parabolic mirror before each Olympics since 1936 = signature visual of modern Olympic ceremonies, palaestra + gymnasium + Phidias workshop), Mycenae (UNESCO 1999, capital of King Agamemnon in Homer's Iliad, one of the oldest fortified cities in Europe 16th-12th BC, Lion Gate 13th BC = signature visual, Treasury of Atreus dome tomb 13th BC one of the largest pre-Roman structures, "Agamemnon" gold mask found by Schliemann 1876 preserved at Athens National Museum), Epidaurus (UNESCO 1988, sanctuary of Asclepius 4th BC, Ancient Theatre of Epidaurus = one of the best preserved ancient theatres in the world, capacity 14,000 places, exceptional acoustics that allow you to hear from the last row a coin falling at the centre of the stage = signature legend confirmed scientifically by MIT 2007 thanks to seat structure in stone absorbing low frequencies = unique phenomenon in the world, still used for theatre in summer during the Athens-Epidaurus Festival), Sparta (the legendary city-state of the Spartans rival of Athens 5th century BC, few visible vestiges today ironically as the Spartans did not build in durable stone, more symbol than site), Mistra (UNESCO 1989, former Byzantine capital of the Despotate of Morea 13th-15th century, impressive ruins on hill with 30 preserved monasteries and palaces, one of the most beautiful Byzantine medieval cities in the world), Monemvasia ("Greek Gibraltar", medieval fortified town on islet connected by narrow strip of land, unique atmosphere in the Mediterranean, one of the postcard images of the Peloponnese), Nafplio (Nafplio, first capital of modern Greece 1828-1834 before Athens, preserved Venetian old town, Palamidi fortress on 216m hill), Mani peninsula (the wildest region of Greece, unique-in-the-world stone Maniote tower-houses, austere atmosphere, preserved beaches Vathia + Limeni), Kalamata (already covered), Mistras, Pylos and Methoni (Venetian fortresses), Voidokilia Beach (one of the most beautiful beaches in Europe according to many rankings, supernatural regular arc bay near Pylos), Patras (3rd city of Greece, ferry port to Italy). Itinerary: 7-10 days by car to cover the essentials, base at Nafplio ideal for visiting Mycenae-Epidaurus-Olympia-Sparta-Mistra-Monemvasia. Try Greek moussaka, souvlaki, gyros, IGP Peloponnesian feta, signature IGP Kalamata olives, IGP Kalamata olive oil, Mantineia AOC moscofilero white wine, Nemea AOC agiorgitiko red wine, ouzo, tsipouro, Mavroudi from the Peloponnese.
skiathos:en: Skiathos, smallest island of the Sporades archipelago in the Aegean Sea: 6000 inhabitants on 49 km², in the west of the archipelago near the continental coast. Renowned for its exceptional fine sand beaches (rare in Greece where pebbles and rocks dominate): the most famous is Koukounaries, regularly ranked among the most beautiful beaches in Europe. Main filming location of the film Mamma Mia! 2008 and Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again 2018 (Skiathos for the main scenes, Vis Croatia for the sequel). July is my favourite month (9.5/10): 25-27°C, 6% rain, sea at 23°C, ideal temperatures. August stays excellent (9.2/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 22-27°C, sea at 21-25°C, hotel prices x2-x3 (British in the majority thanks to direct charter flights from UK). From November to March, expect 13-15°C and 25-38% rain: mild but windy, many hotels close. Unique climate: Skiathos has the particularity of having low temperature variations year-round (never below 13°C even in winter, never more than 27°C even in summer thanks to the constant sea breeze). Highlights / Beaches: Koukounaries ("Pines" in Greek, golden sand beach 1.2 km bordered by a dense pine forest, European Blue Flag, one of the signature beaches of Greece, accessible by bus or car from Skiathos Town), Lalaria Beach (north coast, access only by boat, white round pebbles polished by the sea, white cliffs, Tripia Petra = natural arch in the cliff = signature visual of Skiathos), Banana Beach and Little Banana (sand, younger atmosphere), Big Banana Beach (the liveliest), Vromolimnos (water sports), Aselinos and Mandraki (north coast, wilder), Agia Eleni Beach, Megali Aselinos, Krassa ("Naturist Beach" = official nudist beach). Skiathos Town (charming capital with whitewashed alleys, marina, dynamic nightlife with rooftop bars — including Final Step Bar sunset institution). Bourtzi (peninsula in the heart of the harbour with 13th-century Venetian fortress transformed into cultural place). Monastery of Panagia Evangelistria (18th century, the Greek flag was designed and raised for the first time here in 1807 during the war of independence). Excursions: Skopelos 30 min by ferry (neighbouring island, filming location Mamma Mia! notably the church Agios Ioannis sto Kastri perched on a rocky peak), Alonissos (national marine park of the Mediterranean monk seal), Mount Athos (visible in the distance in clear weather, religious access only to Orthodox men). Try kakavia (Greek fish soup), grilled octopus, local feta, ouzo, Retsina resin wine.
