Off-piste: Gudauri is one of the emerging heli-ski bases in Europe (authorised in Georgia unlike France-Germany where heli-ski is forbidden), operators Heliski Georgia + Caucasus Heliski offer virgin descents in the Greater Caucasus 2500m+ vertical drop in 1 descent, prices 200-500€/day vs 800-1500€ Alps equivalent = considered the best value for money for heli-ski in the world, attracts international freeriders and pros. Strengths: very low prices (passes 40-50€/day vs 70-90€ Alps, 4* hotels 80-200€/night vs 300-800€), snowfall among the best in Europe (3-5m of powder in season), few crowds compared to congested Alps. Audience: Georgians in the majority (Tbilisi 2h by car), Russians (before the Ukraine war 2022), Israelis (direct flight Tbilisi-Tel Aviv 4h), Polish-Czech-Ukrainian, few Westerners. Hotels: Quadrum Resort (4*+ opened 2018 modern architecture), Hotel Marco Polo, Rooms Hotel Kazbegi (4* in Stepantsminda 30 min from Gudauri to combine ski-cultural tourism), prices 80-300€/night. Excursions: Stepantsminda-Kazbegi 30 min away (mountain village with Gergeti Trinity Church 14th-century perched at 2170m = signature visual of Georgia), Tbilisi 2h away (capital, already covered). Georgian Military Way = legendary Soviet-Tsarist road connecting Tbilisi to Vladikavkaz Russia via the Cross Pass 2379m, crossing the most spectacular landscapes of the Georgian Caucasus, to travel by car-marshrutka. Try Adjarian khachapuri (signature cheese bread, open boat version with egg), khinkali (Georgian dumplings), mtsvadi (skewers), satsivi (chicken in walnut sauce), qvevri Saperavi/Rkatsiteli wines (Georgia = cradle of wine 8000 years ago), chacha (Georgian grape eau-de-vie).
bakuriani:en: Bakuriani, ski resort and holiday destination in Georgia in the Samtskhe-Javakheti region: 2500 inhabitants at 1700m altitude on the north side of the Lesser Caucasus. One of the oldest post-Soviet ski resorts in Georgia (created in the Soviet era for the nomenklatura), recently modernised. Hosted the 2023 Junior Alpine Ski World Championships. August is my favourite month (8.3/10): 12-22°C, 36% rain, this is mild summer at altitude (escape from the Tbilisi heat 200 km below). From June to September, it's the summer high season: 8-22°C, hiking, mountain bike, horseback rides. From December to March, it's ski high season: -8/4°C, 35-52% rain often as snow (3-4m/season, snow blown by Caucasus winds). Bakuriani pistes: 4 connected zones (Didveli, Kokhta, Mitarbi, Tatra), 30 km of marked pistes, 6 modern lifts (detachable chairlifts), altitude 1700-2700m, vertical drop 1000m, ideal terrain for beginners-intermediates, lots of off-piste freeride space. Ski rates 30-50€/day (among the cheapest in Europe). Audience: essentially Georgians (national ski school), Russians wintering off-season, numerous Israelis (direct flight from Tel Aviv), more and more Europeans seeking cheap alternatives to the Alps. Local sites: Bakuriani Forest (spruces), Mount Sakvelo (3001m, summer hike), Lake Tabatskuri 30 min away (altitude lake 2000m, swimming in July-August), Borjomi 30 min away (legendary Georgian thermal station, source of Borjomi mineral water exported worldwide, Borjomi-Kharagauli National Park state park — the largest park in Europe). Logistics: Bakuriani 3h from Tbilisi by car/marshrutka (panoramic road to Borjomi then 30 km hairpin climb), historic "Kukushka" train from Borjomi 2h on narrow gauge line 1899 = unique-in-the-world nostalgic experience. Try steaming Georgian khinkali après-ski (18-pleat dumplings Bakuriani specialty), khachapuri, gomi (polenta), satsivi, Georgian wines, tchatcha (local eau-de-vie, ideal après-ski grog).
shahdag:en: Shahdag is the recent ski resort of Azerbaijan, opened in 2012 in the Caucasus at 1900m altitude. Ambition: to make the South Caucasus an affordable ski destination. July is my favourite month (5.4/10): 5-13°C, 53% rain, fresh alpine climate. From December to March, expect -15/-3°C: this is ski season. Area still limited (30 km of pistes) but in expansion. Day pass at 25-35€, 4-5 star hotels at 80-150€/night. From April to November, station almost deserted off ski season. The logistics remain basic: few direct flights, you generally have to go through Baku then 3h by road to the north. Local Azerbaijani and Russian population mainly. For travellers seeking off-the-beaten-track skiing, at unbeatable prices. Note: our climate data reflects altitude zones where summer remains very fresh (alpine climate).
astana:en: Astana (briefly renamed Nur-Sultan 2019-2022 in honour of Nazarbayev then re-renamed Astana in 2022 after his fall), capital of Kazakhstan since 1997: 1.3 million inhabitants in the heart of the Kazakh steppes at 350m altitude. Capital integrally planned created from scratch (transferred from Almaty for seismic and geopolitical reasons), futuristic architecture signed Norman Foster and other archistars — showcase city of the Kazakh petrodollar. July-August are my favourite months (8.9/10): 14-26°C, 26-34% rain, this is the short continental summer. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 12-26°C, dry and hot climate. From December to February, expect -16/-7°C: extreme continental cold with glacial steppe winds (sometimes -40°C in January-February, one of the coldest capitals in the world after Ulaanbaatar), little tourism. From September-May, temperatures fluctuating enormously: -16/+21°C depending on the season. Highlights (essentially spectacular contemporary architecture): Bayterek Tower (97m, signature of the city built in 1997 year of capital transfer, observation platform 105m with gilded mould of Nazarbayev's hand for signature photo), Khan Shatyr ("Khan's tent", Norman Foster 2010, largest tent in the world 150m high, mall+gardens+indoor tropical beach to escape the continental climate), Pyramid of Peace and Reconciliation (Norman Foster 2006, 77m, concert hall for interfaith gatherings), Hazret Sultan Mosque (the largest in Central Asia 2012, 17,000 seats), Russian Orthodox Cathedral, Ultra-modern left bank quarter. Excursions: Lake Borovoye/Burabay 250 km north ("Kazakh Switzerland", lake landscapes and rocky formations, Soviet thermal station). Logistics: Astana 3h by plane from Moscow, direct flight Astana-Paris seasonal Air Astana. Try beshbarmak (national dish: horse or mutton meat boiled on flat pasta), kazy (horse sausage), kymyz (fermented mare's milk), shubat (fermented camel's milk), black tea with milk, vodka.
ouzbekistan:en: Uzbekistan, country in Central Asia on the ancient Silk Road: 35 million inhabitants. Former Soviet republic 1924-1991, massive tourist opening since 2017 under President Mirziyoyev (e-visa, removal of mandatory tourist registration). Country-treasure of the Silk Road myth-cities: Samarkand, Bukhara, Khiva. September is my favourite month (9.4/10): 15-29°C, 5% rain, this is the ideal dry season after the strong summer heat. May stays excellent (9.2/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 8-37°C. June-August drop to 6.9/10 due to extreme heatwave (20-37°C, sometimes 45°C+ Bukhara/Khiva). From November to March, expect -2/13°C and 22-32% rain: continental cold, snow possible Tashkent. Highlights: Samarkand (UNESCO 2001, one of the most beautiful cities in the world, capital of Tamerlane's empire 14th-15th, Registan Square = ensemble of 3 madrasas Tilla-Kari/Sher-Dor/Ulugh Beg on 3 sides signature visual, Gur-i Amir mausoleum tomb of Tamerlane, Ulugh Beg observatory 15th century one of the most advanced in the medieval world), Bukhara (UNESCO 1993, former capital of the emirate of Bukhara, 7 mosques-madrasas-minarets including Kalan Minaret 47m 12th century = signature visual, Bolo Khauz Mosque, Ark fortress, Lyabi-Hauz ensemble, Po-i-Kalan), Khiva (UNESCO 1990, fortified open-air museum-city, one of the most completely preserved Silk Road cities, Itchan Kala = old town encircled by 2 km ramparts with 4 gates, unique atmosphere), Tashkent capital (3 million inhabitants, metro with Soviet decorated stations signatures, Chorsu market, Hazrat Imam mausoleum housing the oldest preserved Quran in the world 7th century), Fergana Valley (fertile agricultural region, Rishtan ceramic workshops, Margilan silks), Kyzylkum Desert (yurts in desert), Aral Sea (major ecological catastrophe, 90% disappeared since 1960 by Soviet diversion of the rivers, ghost ports Moynaq with boat carcasses in the sand). Try Uzbek plov (signature carrot-mutton rice, national dish, UNESCO 2016), shashlik, samsa (oven turnover), lagman, manti, green or black tea, kazi (horse sausage). Tashkent (TAS) and Samarkand (SKD) airports.
bichkek:en: Bishkek, capital of Kyrgyzstan: 1 million inhabitants at 800m altitude at the foot of the Tian Shan Mountains (5000m visible from the city), former Soviet capital of the Kirghiz Republic (Frunze 1926-1991, return to the historic name Bishkek after the independence from the USSR). Most accessible and bohemian city in Central Asia (free visa for Europeans 60 days, relaxed atmosphere). June-August are my favourite months (8.9-9.3/10): 16-32°C, 15-23% rain, this is the continental summer. From May to September, it's the tourist high season: 12-32°C, dry and sunny ideal climate. From December to February, expect -5/3°C and 23-28% rain: cold continental winter, but skiing possible at neighbouring Karakol and Chimgan. Highlights (Bishkek is above all a base for exploring the country, few monuments in themselves): Ala-Too Square (centre, statue of Manas national epic hero who replaced the statue of Lenin), Osh Bazaar (huge market, Central Asian specialties, dried fruits), History Museum ("Lenin Museum" historically, massive Soviet heritage), Holy Resurrection Cathedral, Panfilov Park, White House (Parliament). Major Kyrgyz excursions: Lake Issyk-Kul 4h away (2nd largest alpine lake in the world after Titicaca, 6200 km², 700m deep, never frozen despite altitude 1600m thanks to its salinity, beaches on the north side at Cholpon-Ata, hiking on the south side at Karakol), Karakol Valley and Lenin Peak (7134m, one of the only "easily" accessible 7000m summits, 3-week ascent with acclimatisation), Khan Tengri Peak (7010m), Kyrgyz nomad yurts (homestay in high jailoo pasture, milking of mares, kymyz fermented milk), Song-Kul (altitude lake 3016m, summer nomadic yurts). Try beshbarmak (national dish based on horse or mutton), laghman (sautéed thick noodles), manty (steamed dumplings), plov (pilaf rice), shashlik (skewers), kymyz, Kyrgyz vodka.
douchanbé:en: Dushanbe, capital of Tajikistan in Central Asia: 850,000 inhabitants at 800m altitude at the foot of the Pamir. Former Soviet republic of Central Asia (1924-1991), poorest country of the former USSR, bordering Afghanistan-China-Kyrgyzstan-Uzbekistan. Modernised but little touristic capital, base for Pamir Highway expeditions. September is my favourite month (9.3/10): 14-30°C, 2% rain, this is the ideal dry season. June stays excellent (8.9/10). From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 13-37°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heatwave (18-36°C). From November to March, expect -3/16°C and 26-45% rain: continental cold, snow possible. Highlights: Statue of Ismoil Somoni (the highest monument in Tajikistan, tribute to the founder of the Samanid dynasty 9th-10th century recognised as spiritual father of the Tajik nation), Dushanbe Flag (one of the highest flagpoles in the world 165m, flag 60m × 30m), National Museum of Antiquities (reclining Buddha statue 13m discovered in 1966 in a cave, signature visual), Mehrgon Bazaar (traditional central market, Pamir dried fruits, spices), Haji Yakoub Mosque, Doustii Square, Palace of Nations (presidential palace). Essential Tajikistan excursions: Pamir Highway ("M41", one of the most legendary roads in the world, 1252 km from Khorog to Osh Kyrgyzstan through the Pamir high plateaus 4655m Ak-Baital pass, lunar-Himalayan landscapes, 4x4 expedition 7-15 days), Lake Iskanderkul 2h away (alpine lake named after Alexander the Great passed here, emerald water at 2200m), Wakhan Valley (Afghan border, Hindu Kush mountains visible to the south, unique Pamiri Ismaili communities), Khujand (2nd city, 2500 years old). Try osh plov (carrot-mutton rice, Tajik national dish), laghman (noodles), shashlik (skewers), Tajik naan (patterned flatbread), green tea with jam. Logistics: Dushanbe airport (DYU), electronic visa available.
alger:en: Algiers ("El Djazaïr"), capital of Algeria: 3.5 million inhabitants in urban area on the Bay of Algiers (Mediterranean). Nicknamed "Algiers the White" for its lime-whitewashed Haussmannian buildings and casbah, perched in tiers between sea and hills. Limited tourist visit (few Western tourists in Algeria, 2 million/year total against 11 million in neighbouring Morocco). June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 18-27°C, 7% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 15-29°C, 15-22% rain. From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (22-31°C, occasional sirocco at 40°C+). From November to April, expect 7-20°C and 22-38% rain: mild, more humid. Highlights: Casbah of Algiers (UNESCO 1992, fortified old town 16th-century Ottoman, 3 km², maze of steep tier alleys, 70m vertical drop in 1 km, Dar Aziza and Dar Mustapha Pacha palaces, Ketchaoua Mosque, Casbah suffers from the slow degradation of century-old buildings not renovated since 1962), Notre-Dame d'Afrique (basilica 1872 perched 124m above the sea, signature panorama, atypical as Catholic in Muslim country and still in operation), Maqam Echahid (Martyrs' Memorial, monument 92m high 1982 commemorating the war of independence 1954-1962), Hamma Botanical Garden (1832 botanical garden, one of the most beautiful in Africa with giant ficus, Tarzan scenes filmed here), Bardo Museum (prehistory, ethnography), MAMA - Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art of Algiers (in former Galerie de France department store 1909). Excursions: Tipaza 1h away (UNESCO, Roman ruins on the seaside, much better preserved than the more famous ones in Morocco/Tunisia), Cherchell, Constantine 4h away ("city of bridges" suspended above the Rhumel gorges). Safety: Algiers north is globally safe, the south of the country (Sahara, Mali-Niger-Libya borders) remains inadvisable for Europeans. Try Algerian couscous (different from Moroccan and Tunisian), chakhchoukha, makroudh, mhajeb, mint tea.
oran:en: Oran is the 2nd city of Algeria, facing the Mediterranean. Dry Mediterranean climate. June is my favourite month (9.9/10): 18-28°C, 5% rain, regular sea breezes. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the dry heatwave (21-31°C) and the coastal humidity that makes the heat heavy. The Oranais evacuate to the beaches of Aïn El Turk or Cap Falcon. From November to March, expect 8-20°C and 22-30% rain: humid, frequent wind, but the city keeps its rhythm. The raï scene (Khaled, Mami) was born here: a few historic cabarets persist. Santa Cruz (overhanging Spanish fortress) for the panoramic view. The Abdelkader Alloula Regional Theatre and Place du 1er-Novembre concentrate the animation. International Algerian tourism remains limited: visa to anticipate, basic hotel infrastructure. For prepared travellers, the experience remains strong.
constantine:en: Constantine (Qsantina), 3rd city of Algeria 320 km east of Algiers: 450,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 1 million in urban area. "City of Suspended Bridges" = city built on a rocky plateau 600m altitude surrounded by spectacular gorges of the Rhumel 200m deep, crossed by 7 bridges of which 4 spectacular. Capital of pre-Roman Numidian civilisation under King Massinissa 2nd century BC, refounded 313 AD by Emperor Constantine I who gave it his name. Intellectual and religious centre of the Maghreb world. June is my favourite month (8.9/10): 16-29°C, 14% rain, ideal temperatures. May-September-October stay excellent (8.0-8.6/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 9-32°C. From July to August, expect 19-34°C with possible heatwave. From November to March, expect 2-16°C and 11-31% rain: relative cold with snow possible. Highlights: Sidi M'Cid Bridge (1912, the highest suspension bridge in Algeria 175m above the Rhumel = signature visual of Constantine, one of the postcard images of Algeria), Sidi Rached Bridge (1912, stone arch bridge 447m long, one of the longest masonry arch bridges in the world), Mellah Slimane Footbridge (spectacular pedestrian suspension footbridge above the gorges), El Kantara Bridge (the oldest bridge, Roman heritage rebuilt 19th century), Ahmed Bey Palace (1830, one of the most beautiful Ottoman palaces in Algeria, mosaics + Andalusian gardens + Islamic art collection), Sidi Lakhdar Mosque, Sacred Heart Cathedral (1947 French colonial heritage, transformed into Emir Abdelkader Mosque 1962 after independence), Emir Abdelkader Mosque (one of the largest mosques in Africa, 4 minarets 107m signature visual of modern Constantine, capacity 15,000 worshippers), Si El Haouès Madrasa (former 18th-century Quranic school), Constantine Medina (preserved old Arab town), University of Constantine ("Constantine 1", one of the most prestigious universities in the Maghreb, designed by Brazilian architect Oscar Niemeyer 1972 = unique modern architectural signature in Algeria), Cirta Museum (one of the best archaeological museums in Algeria, Roman-Numidian-Byzantine collection). Excursions: Timgad 2h away (UNESCO, top Roman site), Annaba 2h away, Djemila 1h30 away (UNESCO 1982, other remarkably preserved Roman city), Setif 1h30 away, Tipaza 5h away (UNESCO 1982). Safety: Constantine globally safe, France Diplomacy increased vigilance. Try Constantinian chakhchoukha (local signature), tlitli (vermicelli), Algerian couscous, makroud (semolina-date cake), tcharak (almond cakes), mint tea. Constantine Mohamed Boudiaf airport (CZL).
ouarzazate:en: Ouarzazate, town in southern Morocco: 70,000 inhabitants at 1160m altitude at the foot of the High Atlas, nicknamed "the gateway to the desert" and "Moroccan Hollywood" (Atlas Studios = the largest film studios in the world by surface area, 30+ international films shot here including Lawrence of Arabia, Gladiator, Babel, Game of Thrones, Mission Impossible). April is my favourite month (9.6/10): 11-27°C, 4% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From March to May and October-November, these are the best seasons: 7-28°C, 4-9% rain, dry and sunny climate. From July-August drop to 3.9/10 due to the extreme heat (22-38°C). From December to February, expect 2-18°C: pleasant days, cold nights, snow possible on the Atlas (skiing possible at Oukaïmeden near Marrakech). Highlights: Taourirt Kasbah (18th century, former residence of the Glaoui Berber pasha who ruled southern Morocco late 19th-early 20th with the approval of the French protectorate, partially restored), Atlas Studios (visit of the permanent sets: Tibet, Ancient Egypt, ancient Jerusalem, etc., in partnership with CLA Studios), Cinema Museum, Place Al Mouahidine. Excursions: Aït Benhaddou 30 km away (UNESCO 1987, spectacular fortified ksar 1000 years in red pisé, one of the most famous in Morocco and filming location of hundreds of films, to see at sunset), Drâa Valley in the south ("palm valley" 200 km of palm groves bordering the Drâa river, largest oasis in Morocco), Roses Valley at Kalaat M'Gouna (May rose festival = Moroccan perfumery), Dadès Valley with its spectacular gorges, Todgha Valley (300m high gorges, climbing), Tinghir, Telouet (Glaoui kasbah), Erg Chebbi/Merzouga 4-5h away (desert, see dedicated review). Logistics: Ouarzazate 4h from Marrakech by the panoramic road of the Tichka pass 2260m (one of the most beautiful roads in Morocco), regional airport. Try Berber tagine, couscous, mehrouss (local pastry).
oukaimeden:en: Oukaïmeden is the highest ski resort in Africa: 3268m at the summit, 75 km from Marrakech (1h30 by road via the Ourika valley). Modest area (10 km of pistes, 1 cable car, 6 ski tows) but unique experience. July is my favourite month (8.6/10): 14-23°C, 28% rain, good for hiking towards Toubkal (4167m, highest summit in North Africa). From January to March, expect -3/7°C in resort, snow possible but uncertain (some seasons without any snow, depending on Atlantic precipitation). From April to November, station almost deserted. Strengths: skiing then dining couscous in the evening in Marrakech, total change of scenery, low prices (day pass 15-20€). Limits: ageing equipment, uncertain snow, not suitable for confirmed skiers. For the Berber children of the valley, it's a period of complementary income (offer luge rental, lessons).
tripoli:en: Tripoli (Tarabulus), capital of Libya on the Mediterranean coast: 1.2 million inhabitants. Founded by the Phoenicians 7th century BC under the name Oea, one of the three "Tripolis" cities ("3 cities" in Greek) with Sabratha and Leptis Magna forming the Roman province of Tripolitania. Capital of Libya Ottoman then Italian 1911-1943 (Italian colonial heritage still visible) then independent 1951 under King Idris then under Muammar Gaddafi 1969-2011 = 42-year authoritarian reign ended by 2011 revolution under NATO bombings, since = chronic civil war between rival factions. June is my favourite month (9.5/10): 19-29°C, 1% rain, this is the ideal Mediterranean spring. May-October stay excellent (8.7-9.0/10). From April to June and September-October, these are the best tourist seasons: 13-31°C, sea at 22-26°C, ideal Mediterranean climate. From July to August, expect 22-32°C with possible heatwave ghibli = hot dry Saharan wind that can raise temperatures to 45°C+. From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 17-32% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, rare cold. Highlights: Medina of Tripoli (preserved Ottoman-Italian atmosphere, one of the most beautiful medinas in North Africa, labyrinthine alleys, traditional souks), Arch of Marcus Aurelius (165 AD, Roman vestige in the heart of the medina), Gurgi Mosque (18th-century Ottoman), Karamanli Mosque (18th century), Tripoli Cathedral (1923 Italian heritage transformed into Gamal Abdel Nasser Mosque 1970), Red Castle / As-Saraya Al-Hamra (Ottoman palace and seat of government, one of the best archaeological museums in Libya before the war, Roman-Punic masterpieces), Martyrs' Square (former Gaddafi Green Square renamed 2011), Arch of Septimius Severus (Roman vestige), Tripoli Beach (urban Mediterranean). Major Roman archaeological sites Libya = this is why Libya was visited before 2011: Leptis Magna 130 km to the east (UNESCO 1982, one of the most beautiful Roman cities in the world, birthplace of Emperor Septimius Severus 145-211 AD who transformed it into a monumental imperial city, theatre + amphitheatre + arch of Septimius Severus + baths + forum in exceptional state of preservation thanks to burial by Saharan sand until the 19th century = one of the largest Roman archaeological sites in the world rivalling Pompeii-Ephesus), Sabratha 70 km to the west (UNESCO 1982, other Roman-Punic city with exceptional 3-level theatre and temples), Cyrene 1000 km to the east (UNESCO 1982, Greek then Roman city in eastern Libya, in the Cyrenaica region), Ghadamès in the south-west (UNESCO 1986, Saharan oasis with unique-in-the-world troglodyte Berber architecture, abandoned and in danger). Safety: inadvisable France Diplomacy for the entirety of Libya = civil war since 2011, 2 rival governments (Tripoli vs Benghazi), armed militias, Western kidnappings, Islamist terrorism, extremely inadvisable travel. Try Libyan couscous, bazin (signature barley flour paste with spicy sauce), shorba arabia (barley soup), Tunisian-Libyan brik, baklava, mint tea. Tripoli Mitiga airport (MJI).
khartoum:en: Khartoum (Al-Khartoum), capital of Sudan at the confluence of the White Nile and the Blue Nile forming the Nile which then flows towards Egypt: 5.3 million inhabitants in metropolitan area "Triple Capital" Khartoum + Omdurman + North Khartoum. Unique geographical place in the world = the confluence of the White Nile coming from Lake Victoria + Blue Nile coming from Lake Tana in Ethiopia, the 2 rivers visibly meet in Khartoum without immediately mixing (waters of different colours for several km), forming the Nile = the longest river in the world 6695 km. Country among the poorest in Africa, marked by 30 years of Omar al-Bashir Islamist dictatorship 1989-2019 (International Criminal Court indicted him for genocide in Darfur 300,000 deaths 2003-2019), then democratic transition aborted by successive military coups, civil war since April 2023 between the regular SAF army (Burhan) and the RSF paramilitary forces (Hemedti) = major humanitarian catastrophe 12 million displaced, mass famine Darfur-Khartoum, capital partially destroyed. January is my favourite month (9.2/10): 16-32°C, 0% rain, this is the ideal dry season. December-February stay excellent (8.6-8.8/10). From December to February, it's the cool dry season: 14-32°C. From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave (haboubs = spectacular Saharan sandstorms): 20-42°C. From June to October drop to 3.9-6.7/10 due to heatwave + short Sahelian rainy season. Highlights (before the war): Confluence of the Nile ("Mogran", signature geographical point where White Nile + Blue Nile meet, observation from boat), Omdurman Central Market (one of the largest open-air markets in sub-Saharan Africa), Tomb of the Mahdi (Omdurman, mausoleum of the Mahdi Muhammad Ahmad who overthrew the Anglo-Egyptians 1885 then defeated by Kitchener 1898), National Museum of Sudan (exceptional Nubian collection, Karima statues + temples remounted), Soufi Hamed al-Nil Mosque (Omdurman, Friday Sufi whirling dervish ceremony). Major Nubian archaeological sites = Sudanese Nubia houses more pyramids than Egypt (250 vs 130 in Egypt = but they are smaller and less famous): Meroe 200 km north (UNESCO 2011, former capital of the Nubian Kingdom of Kush 3rd century BC-4th century AD, pyramid necropolis with 200 pyramids smaller but more pointed than the Egyptian ones, mystical Saharan landscape signature visual), Naga and Musawwarat es-Sufra (UNESCO 2011, other Kushite sites). Safety: inadvisable France Diplomacy for the entirety of Sudan = active civil war since 2023, capital partially destroyed, extremely inadvisable travel except experienced humanitarian missions. Try ful medames (fava beans, popular dish at breakfast shared with Egypt), kisra (signature sorghum flatbread), aseeda (sorghum porridge), grilled Nile fish, Sudanese coffee with signature ginger, hibiscus juice. Khartoum International airport (KRT) damaged by the 2023 war.
djibouti:en: Djibouti (Djibouti City), capital of Djibouti on the Gulf of Aden at the strategic entrance of the Red Sea: 600,000 inhabitants. Strategic country in the heart of the Horn of Africa = obligatory passage of 30% of world maritime traffic between Mediterranean and Indian Ocean (Suez Canal + Bab-el-Mandeb strait 100 km away), 5 foreign military bases on 23,200 km² (the highest density of military bases in the world) = USA + France + Italy + Japan + China (1st Chinese military base outside China since 2017). Former French colony "French Somaliland Coast" 1862-1977, independence 1977 under President Hassan Gouled Aptidon. Mixed Afar + Issa Somali population. January is my favourite month (9.0/10): 24-29°C, 13% rain, this is the ideal dry season. February-December stay excellent (8.7-9.0/10). From November to February, it's the relative "fresh" dry season: 23-29°C, sea at 25°C, dry and tempered tropical climate ideal thanks to refreshing trade winds. From June to August drop to 6.5-6.9/10 due to extreme heatwave (29-42°C, sometimes 47°C+ with oppressive coastal humidity = among the most uncomfortable urban climates in the world in summer). Djibouti city highlights: Place du 27 Juin (official central square, tribute to independence 1977), Djibouti Central Market (unique mixed Afar-Issa-Arab-Ethiopian atmosphere), Hamoudi Mosque, Place Ménélik (French colonial heritage), European Quarter (preserved 19th-20th century French colonial architecture), Djibouti Beach / Khor Ambado (urban beach with crystal water), Port of Djibouti (one of the most strategic ports in the world, military logistics + global maritime trade). Essential Djibouti excursions = this is why one visits Djibouti: Lake Assal 1h30 away (the lowest point in Africa -155m below sea level + the saltest lake in the world 35% salinity = 10 times more than the oceans, more than the Dead Sea 30%, spectacular crystal-white lunar landscapes signature visual of Djibouti), Lake Abbé at the Ethiopian border 4h (surreal landscapes with 50m-high signature limestone chimneys, filming location of the film "Planet of the Apes" 1968 by Franklin Schaffner = cult cinematographic experience, post-apocalyptic atmosphere unique in the world), Beaches of Plage des Sables Blancs + Khor Ambado (urban beaches), Moucha and Maskali Islands 30 min by boat (signature snorkelling and diving), Tadjourah (historic old town, former sultanate capital), Goda Mountains Day Forest National Park (microclimate altitude forest), swimming with whale sharks (October-February, the Gulf of Tadjourah is one of the world sites for whale shark observation = signature tourist of Djibouti). Safety: Djibouti = one of the safest countries in Africa thanks to massive foreign military presence, low urban crime in Djibouti city. Try sambusa (signature samosas Horn of Africa), skoudehkaris (chicken with spices), grilled fish, lahoh (Ethio-Somali pancake), Djiboutian coffee (mix of neighbouring Yemen + Ethiopia = exceptional quality), Somali tea with cardamom. Djibouti-Ambouli airport (JIB).
saint-pierre-et-miquelon:en: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, French overseas community in the North Atlantic off Canadian Newfoundland: 6000 inhabitants on 242 km² spread over 8 islands of which 2 main inhabited (Saint-Pierre 6 km² + Miquelon-Langlade 216 km²). Only French territory in North America = vestige of the French Empire of Canada lost by the Treaty of Paris 1763, kept after the other colonies for the fishing rights in the Newfoundland Grand Banks region. Unique atmosphere of Brittany-Normandy of the North Atlantic with preserved 19th-century French colonial architecture. During the American Prohibition 1920-1933, Saint-Pierre was the alcohol smuggling base to USA = legendary local enrichment (Al Capone allegedly came here). August is my favourite month (7.5/10): 12-18°C, 32% rain, ideal temperatures (North Atlantic summer). July stays excellent (7.4/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 6-19°C, mild Atlantic temperatures, sea at 12-15°C (very cold), typical Newfoundland fogs frequent. From December to April, expect -10/2°C and 38-49% precipitation often as snow: North Atlantic winter cold, snowy landscapes, difficult access (flights often cancelled for fog). Highlights: Saint-Pierre town (6000 inhabitants, the only town of the archipelago, French Normandy 19th-century atmosphere with colourful multi-coloured wooden houses typical Newfoundland style = signature visual, Place du Général-de-Gaulle town centre, Saint-Pierre Cathedral 19th-century neo-Romanesque), Arche Museum (historical museum with memory of Prohibition + great fishing), Île aux Marins (5 min by boat, former fishing village abandoned 1965 and preserved as open-air museum = unique memorial atmosphere, accessible May-October), Galantry Lighthouse (signal for ships, panorama), Pointe au Cheval and Pointe du Diamant (wild Atlantic rocky coast), Saint-Pierre Cemetery (unique memorial atmosphere with tombs of sailors of all nationalities who died at sea in the region), Miquelon-Langlade (neighbouring island accessible by 1h ferry, wild and preserved atmosphere, semi-wild horses in the Grande Barachois lagoon, observation grey seals + dolphins, wild hike 26 km), Cap aux Morts (Miquelon north cape with lighthouse). Activities: whale and seal watching (July-September, humpback whales + minke whales + dolphins + grey seals in the strait), big game fishing (cod + halibut + tuna, Grand Banks heritage), wild hiking, sea kayaking. Population: 100% French-speaking, French Hexagone atmosphere in miniature in the heart of the North Atlantic, preserved Norman-Breton accent. No special currency = uses euro since 2002, very high import prices. Try Saint-Pierraise tourtière (signature local fish pie), traditional salted cod (Grand Banks heritage), American lobster (July-August season), Saint-Pierraise blood pudding, imported French cheeses, French wines, Saint-Pierraise beer. Logistics: Saint-Pierre Pointe-Blanche airport (FSP), Air Saint-Pierre flights from Halifax Canada (1h30) or Montreal (1h45) + seasonal Paris CDG flights (5h30 charter Air France summer), passage often complicated by Atlantic fogs.
rodrigues:en: Rodrigues, smallest island of the Republic of Mauritius in the Indian Ocean: 42,000 inhabitants on 109 km², 560 km east of the main island Mauritius (1h30 by plane). Discovered 1528 by the Portuguese Diogo Rodrigues hence the name, twin sister of Mauritius all proportions kept (similar climate but more windy, similar landscapes but wild preserved from mass tourism, population less than 1% of Mauritius). October is my favourite month (8.2/10): 21-22°C, 42% rain, end of the cool season. From October to December and April-May, these are the best seasons: 20-25°C, 42-66% rain. From November to April, it's the warm season (21-26°C, sea at 26°C). From June to September, it's the cool season ("austral winter"): 19-22°C, 45-54% rain, steady trade winds. Rodrigues never tops 8.2/10 (oceanic tropical climate with trade winds). Highlights: Port Mathurin (capital, 6000 inhabitants, lively Saturday morning market with local products and craftsmanship), Mount Limon (398m, highest point, 360° panorama over the island), Beaches: Saint-François (the most beautiful in the south-west), Trou d'Argent (turquoise water creek accessible only by 1h coastal path, paradise), Anse aux Cocos (long beach with coconut trees), Pointe Cotton, Graviers, Saint-Gabriel (1936 wooden church). Île aux Cocos by boat (nature reserve with masked boobies and brown noddies, paradise beach), Île aux Sables, Caverne Patate (underground limestone caves). Activities: kitesurf and windsurf (Rodrigues is one of the world spots for the PWA thanks to constant trade winds 25-35 knots, base at Anse Mourouk), big game fishing (sailfish, marlin), cycling (island crossed east to west 18 km), diving (largest lagoon in Africa 240 km², preserved corals, green turtles nest at Pointe Cotton). Try ourite (grilled octopus local specialty), palmiste salad, lemon pickles, achards, arranged rum, Rodrigues honey. Logistics: Rodrigues 1h30 by plane from Mauritius (1-2 flights/day Air Mauritius), MV Trochetia boat 36h (little used).
sao-tome:en: Sao Tome and Principe, micro-state insular in the Gulf of Guinea off equatorial Africa: 220,000 inhabitants on 2 main islands (Sao Tome 854 km², Principe 142 km²) on the geographic equator. Former Portuguese colony 1471-1975 = first European colony to practise large-scale plantation sugar cultivation with African slaves 16th century = model that will then be exported to Brazil and Caribbean. Country among the poorest in Africa but one of the most politically peaceful. June is my favourite month (8.2/10): 22-28°C, 8% rain, this is the ideal dry season ("gravana" = dry season in local creole). From June to August, it's the dry season: 22-29°C, this is the tourist high season (Portuguese and Angolan clientele mainly, few French), turquoise waters, no rain. From September to May, it's the wet season: 19-46% rainfall, but stable temperatures 22-30°C. Unique equatorial climate: Sao Tome is on the geographic equator but receives the cold Benguela current rising from Antarctica = more moderate temperatures than expected on the equator (rare days above 30°C unlike Manaus or Singapore also on the equator). Highlights: São Tomé City capital (60,000 inhabitants, preserved Portuguese colonial atmosphere, limestone cobblestones, independence square), São Tomé Cathedral (15th-century Portuguese Gothic, one of the oldest Christian churches in sub-Saharan Africa), Fortress São Sebastião (16th-century Portuguese, houses the National Museum), Roça Agostinho Neto (former 1900 Portuguese cocoa plantation, plantation hospital reconverted, memorial atmosphere), Roça São João dos Angolares (cocoa plantation reconverted into boutique hotel with gourmet restaurant by chef João Carlos Silva = Sao Tomean culinary ambassador), Pico Cão Grande ("Great Dog Peak", 663m volcanic needle signature visual of Sao Tome rising from the jungle, extremely difficult ascent reserved for professional mountaineers), Praia Jalé Beach (south-east, sea turtle nesting), Lagoa Azul Beach (north, turquoise waters), Sete Ondas Beach ("7 Waves"), São Nicolau Waterfall (waterfall in the jungle), Obo Natural Park (primary equatorial jungle covering 30% of the island, observation of 130 endemic orchid species, endemic Sao Tome pigeon birds). Principe Island 30 min by plane (smaller wilder and more preserved island, declared UNESCO biosphere reserve 2012, Banana beach one of the most beautiful in Africa, Roça Belo Monte luxury boutique hotel on former plantation). Cocoa: Sao Tome is one of the world origins of fine chocolate (former large Portuguese producer, today premium niche for French chocolatiers like Bonnat since 1884 or Pralus). Try calulu (fish with cassava leaves), Sao Tomean feijoada (Portuguese heritage), arroz doce (Portuguese rice pudding), Sao Tome coffee (Robusta cultivated at altitude, little known but excellent), local Rosema beer, artisanal chocolate. São Tomé airport (TMS) with weekly flights from Lisbon (6h30) and Libreville (1h).
albanie:en: Albania, country in the Western Balkans on the Adriatic-Ionian coast facing Italy (Otranto 80 km to the west): 2.8 million inhabitants. Former extreme isolationist communist regime under Enver Hoxha (1944-1985 = the most paranoid dictatorship in Europe, 700,000 concrete bunkers built in the country "just in case", still visible everywhere today). Opening after 1991, EU candidate since 2014. Tourism boom 2018-2025: low prices, preserved beaches, Mediterranean atmosphere before gentrification, become "the new Croatia" for European backpackers. June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 18-28°C, 22% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. May and September stay excellent (8.1-8.7/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 13-32°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (20-32°C). From November to March, expect 3-17°C and 27-40% rain: mild for the Mediterranean. Highlights: Tirana capital (already covered by BDW, 800,000 inhabitants), Albanian Riviera (south Ionian coast between Vlorë and Sarandë, 100 km of preserved turquoise beaches rivalling neighbouring Greece for prices 50% lower: Dhërmi, Himarë, Jalë, Ksamil facing Corfu Greece with turquoise water), Sarandë (south tourist capital, ferry port to Corfu Greece 30 min), Butrint at Sarandë (UNESCO 1992, one of the most impressive ancient cities in the Balkans, Greek-Roman-Byzantine-Venetian remains superimposed over 2500 years), Gjirokastër (UNESCO 2005, "city of a thousand steps" perched with preserved Ottoman architecture, 12th-century castle, birthplace of Enver Hoxha + writer Ismail Kadaré), Berat (UNESCO 2008, "city of a thousand windows" with stair-stepped Ottoman houses on the hill + Byzantine castle and fortress, 13 mosques and 12 Orthodox-Catholic churches cohabiting), Kruja (symbol-town of the Albanian resistance against the Ottomans 15th century under the national hero Skanderbeg), Shkodër in the north (Lake Shkodër = the largest lake in the Balkans Montenegrin border), Theth and Valbona in the Albanian Alps ("Albanian Alps", "Accursed Mountains", spectacular hikes less frequented than Italian Dolomites). Tirana: Hoxha pyramid (mausoleum of the dictator reconverted), Bunk'Art (museum in Hoxha's nuclear bunker), Skanderbeg Square. Try tavë kosi (lamb in yoghurt, Albanian national dish), byrek (puff turnover with meat/cheese), fërgesë (peppers-tomatoes-cheese), Albanian raki (plum or grape eau-de-vie), Turkish coffee, baklava. Logistics: Tirana Mother Teresa airport (TIA), Sarandë Pashaliman airport (KFZ) opened 2024 for the Riviera.
gdansk:en: Gdansk (Danzig), large port city of northern Poland on the Baltic Sea: 470,000 inhabitants. Former free Hanseatic city, unique atmosphere mixing German-Polish-Jewish heritage. Birthplace of the Solidarność movement (Lech Wałęsa, electrician shipyard striker, who precipitated the fall of Polish communism 1989, prelude to the fall of the Berlin Wall). 90% destroyed by the Soviets in March 1945 in street fighting, rebuilt identically 1950-1960 according to 18th-century plans. August is my favourite month (8.3/10): 15-22°C, 36% rain, ideal temperatures. From June to August are the tourist high season: 13-22°C, Baltic swimming 18-20°C cool but possible (Brzeźno beaches, neighbouring Sopot). From November to March, expect 0-8°C and 35-43% rain: continental winter cold, snow possible, Christmas market (the most famous in Poland). Highlights: Old town (Główne Miasto, rebuilt identically after 1945 but with preserved urban planning authenticity), Długi Targ ("Long Market", main pedestrian artery between the Golden Gate and the Green Gate with colourful Hanseatic houses, 1633 Neptune fountain signature), Town Hall (Gothic-Renaissance, panorama 81m tower), Saint Mary's Church (Bazylika Mariacka, the largest brick church in the world, capacity 25,000 people, Gothic 14th-16th century), Medieval crane (Żuraw, signature visual of Gdansk, largest port crane of the Middle Ages 15th century in Europe), Mariacka Street (typical pedestrian street with characteristic Gdansk outdoor terraces "przedproża", sellers of Baltic amber = world capital of fossilised amber), European Solidarity Centre (modern museum 2014 on the history of Solidarność and the fall of communism, to see, one of the best museums in Poland), Stocznia Gdańska Shipyard (place where Lech Wałęsa and 17,000 workers went on strike 1980, memorial with 3 monumental crosses 42m erected 1980 for the workers killed 1970), Westerplatte (place where the Second World War began on 1 September 1939 at 4:45 am when the German battleship Schleswig-Holstein fired on the Polish garrison, memorial). Excursions: Sopot 25 min by train (smart Baltic beach resort, longest wooden pier in Europe 511m), Hel Peninsula (35 km arc peninsula, beaches, grey seals), Malbork 30 min away (UNESCO, the largest brick castle in the world 13th-14th century, former capital of the Teutonic Knights). Try pierogi, zurek (sour fermented rye soup), bigos (cabbage-sauerkraut meat), kaszanka (groats blood pudding), Zubrówka vodka (bison grass vodka), Sopot beer.
wroclaw:en: Wroclaw (Breslau), 4th city of Poland in the south-west in Lower Silesia: 670,000 inhabitants. Former capital of Silesia, town with complicated history (Polish in the Middle Ages, Austrian 1526-1741, Prussian-German 1741-1945, Polish since 1945 after population exchange). 70% destroyed in 1945 during the Soviet siege. Nicknamed the "Polish Venice" for its 12 river islands and 130 bridges. European Capital of Culture 2016. June is my favourite month (8.1/10): 14-24°C, 35% rain, ideal temperatures. From June to August are the tourist high season: 14-25°C. From November to March, expect -1/10°C and 30-38% rain: continental cold, snow possible. Highlights: Rynek (Market Square, one of the largest medieval squares in Europe 213m × 178m, bordered by colourful 13th-19th century burgher houses, 13th-16th century Gothic town hall in the centre, lively atmosphere terraces), Saint John the Baptist Cathedral (Ostrów Tumski, on the ecclesiastical island = cradle of Wroclaw, 13th-century Gothic), University of Wroclaw (one of the oldest in Central Europe founded 1702, Aula Leopoldina = exceptional baroque hall, mathematician Steinhaus + 9 Nobel laureates from the university), Panorama Racławicka (circular 360° painting of 114m circumference × 15m high representing the battle of Racławice 1794, one of the last great panoramic paintings in the world still exhibited), Hala Stulecia (Centennial Hall UNESCO 2006, reinforced concrete dome 1913 by Max Berg, one of the first large reinforced concrete buildings in the world, capacity 6500 people, considered an architectural masterpiece precursor of modernism), Krasnale ("Wroclaw Dwarves", 800+ statuettes of dwarves 30 cm high scattered in the city, to seek as a treasure hunt, tribute to the Pomarańczowa Alternatywa movement which drew dwarves to ridicule the communist regime in the 80s). Quarter of the 4 Religions (4 places of worship of 4 different religions — Catholic, Protestant, Orthodox, Jewish — cohabit in 4 adjacent streets). Excursions: Ksiaz Castle 1h away (3rd largest castle in Poland, basements used as Nazi bunkers), Auschwitz 3h by bus (UNESCO memorial place former extermination camp), Karkonosze National Park (Giant Mountains, skiing). Try pierogi ruskie (potato cheese dumplings), zurek, bigos, makowiec (poppy cake), Tyskie or Lech beer.
islamabad:en: Islamabad, capital of Pakistan at the foot of the Himalayas at 540m altitude: 1.1 million inhabitants intra-muros, 2.2 million with its twin city Rawalpindi. Planned capital built ex-nihilo in the 1960s (before: capital Karachi on the coast) by the Greek urbanist Constantinos Doxiadis, grid plan with quarters numbered F-G-H-I, one of the greenest and calmest capitals in South Asia. October is my favourite month (9.0/10): 16-28°C, 14% rain, ideal temperatures after monsoon. April stays excellent (8.6/10). From September to November and March-April, these are the best seasons: 8-30°C. From May to June, expect 20-35°C: pre-monsoon heatwave. From July to August, it's the monsoon: 60-61% rainfall, temperatures 24-32°C, greener landscapes. From December to February, expect 5-22°C: relative cold in Pakistan, snow possible in neighbouring mountains. Highlights: Faisal Mosque (Faisal Masjid, one of the largest mosques in the world, capacity 100,000 worshippers inside + 200,000 on the esplanade, financed by King Faisal of Saudi Arabia, modern architecture 1986 by Turkish architect Vedat Dalokay inspired by a Bedouin tent = signature visual of Islamabad), Daman-e-Koh and Pir Sohawa (panoramic hills with view over the city, mountain restaurants), Rawal Lake (artificial lake for paddleboat rides), Pakistan Monument (Shakarparian Hills, national monument in the form of flower petals, 2007), Lok Virsa Museum (Pakistani ethnological museum), Saidpur Village (preserved pre-Mughal village, traditional restaurants). Essential excursions: Taxila 1h away (UNESCO 1980, one of the oldest universities in the world 600 BC, focus of Gandhara Buddhism 2nd century BC-5th century AD), Murree 1h30 away (mountain station 2300m, temperate summer climate), Galyat (other mountain stations), Lahore 4h by bus or 1h by plane (already covered by BDW), Hunza Valley 18h by bus (legendary valley of the Karakoram mountains, K2 7th + Rakaposhi panoramas, centenarian Burusho community), Skardu and Baltoro Glacier (base camp of K2 8611m 2nd highest summit in the world, 12-15 day trek expeditions for confirmed mountaineers). Safety: Islamabad globally safe, but Pakistan in general subject to Islamist terrorism, inadvisable France Diplomacy for most regions, organised travel recommended. Try Pakistani biryani (signature with Lahore), nihari (beef stew cooked all night), karahi (wok curry), naan, samosas, lassi yoghurt, chai (spiced tea), kebab. Islamabad International airport (ISB) opened 2018.
karachi:en: Karachi lives to the rhythm of the monsoon, but its own is limited: July-August only with 21-22% precipitation against 90% in Mumbai at the same period. The climate remains majority arid. February is my favourite month (9.3/10): 17-27°C, 2% rain, sea at good temperature for Clifton Beach. From June to August, the humid heat becomes heavy (28-33°C, high humidity rate) with sometimes strong showers. The monsoon also brings smog and massive traffic jams in this 16-million megacity. From November to March, it's the good moment: blue sky, hot but portable days, pleasant evenings. It's when the open-air markets and street food (biryani, nihari) come alive: too much heat in summer for the food stalls.
dacca:en: Dhaka, capital of Bangladesh on the Ganges-Brahmaputra delta: 10 million inhabitants intra-muros, 22 million in urban area = one of the most densely populated megacities in the world (44,000 inhabitants/km² in the heart, denser than Manhattan). Bangladesh = one of the poorest and most densely populated countries in the world (170 million on the size of Iowa), but in rapid economic growth thanks to textile (2nd world exporter after China, main supplier H&M, Zara, Primark). Very little touristic. February is my favourite month (9.0/10): 16-27°C, 8% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January-November-December stay excellent (8.7-9.0/10). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 13-31°C, dry climate and cool in the morning (sometimes 14°C in January, surprising for tropical Asia). From April to May, expect 24-33°C: pre-monsoon heatwave + potential cyclones (Bangladesh = most cyclonic country in the world with Bay of Bengal). From June to October, it's the monsoon: 46-91% rainfall (Bangladesh = country in the world most at risk of flooding, 50% of the territory flooded each year in monsoon, Cyclone Bhola 1970 killed 500,000 people = deadliest natural catastrophe of the 20th century). Highlights: Old Dhaka (Mughal-chaotic-oriental atmosphere), Lalbagh Fort (17th-century Mughal fortress unfinished by Aurangzeb after the death of his daughter Pari Bibi, tomb in the centre, gardens), Ahsan Manzil ("Pink Palace", 19th-century pink stucco palace on the banks of the Buriganga, former residence of the Bangladeshi Nawab), Star Mosque (Tara Masjid, 18th century with mosaic decoration of blue stars on white façade), Baitul Mukarram Mosque (national mosque, 7th largest in the world, capacity 40,000 worshippers), Sadarghat (river port on the Buriganga, the largest passenger river port in the world, ferries to all of Bangladesh, extraordinary atmosphere with crowd of boats), Bangladesh Parliament (Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban, designed by American architect Louis Kahn 1962-1982, considered one of the masterpieces of modern 20th-century architecture), Bangladesh National Museum, Hindu Street (Shankhari Bazaar, Hindu quarter with traditional workshops). Bangladesh excursions: Sundarbans 6h away (UNESCO 1997, the largest mangrove forest in the world, Indian border, houses the Bengal tiger Royal Bengal Tiger 100 individuals, accessible only by 2-3 day boat), Cox's Bazar 10h by bus or 1h flight ("the longest natural beach in the world" 120 km of continuous sand, main beach resort of the country but important pollution), Rohingya Camp at Cox's Bazar (1 million Muslim Rohingya refugees fleeing the genocide in Burma since 2017, the largest refugee camp in the world, limited access). Safety: Dhaka = chaotic city with extreme density, high crime, frequent political demonstrations, air pollution among the worst in the world. Try Dhaka biryani (different from Indian biryani, more strong in mustard), bhuna khichuri (rice with lentils), hilsa (national fish, the most prized in Bangladesh), pitha (fermented rice cakes), shorshe ilish (hilsa in mustard sauce signature Bengal), Bengal tea. Dhaka Hazrat Shahjalal airport (DAC).
bahrein:en: Bahrain (Kingdom of Bahrain), insular micro-state of the Persian Gulf between Saudi Arabia and Qatar: 1.5 million inhabitants on an archipelago of 33 islands of which the main island (760 km² in total). Connected to Saudi Arabia by the King Fahd Causeway (25 km of bridge-causeway opened 1986). Country among the most liberal in the Arabian peninsula (alcohol authorised in hotels, flexible dress code), Al Khalifa constitutional monarchy since 1783. November is my favourite month (9.1/10): 22-28°C, 6% rain, this is the best month. March-April stay excellent (8.5-8.7/10). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 15-23°C, dry and tempered ideal climate. From May to October drop to 2.7-6.7/10 due to extreme heatwave (27-39°C, sometimes 47°C+ with oppressive coastal humidity). Highlights: Manama (capital, 270,000 inhabitants, Arab-cosmopolitan atmosphere with modern skyscrapers mixed with traditional old town), Bahrain Fort (Qal'at al-Bahrain, UNESCO 2005, vestiges over 4000 years of continuous occupation from Bronze Age to Portuguese era 16th century = one of the most complete archaeological sites in the Middle East), Bab Al-Bahrain ("Gate of Bahrain", British colonial architecture 1949 = entrance gate of the traditional souk), Manama Souk (traditional Arab souk with 22-carat gold Bahrain Gold = signature commercial of the country, Bahraini pearls historically the most prestigious in the world before the invention of Japanese cultured pearls Mikimoto 1916 which ruined the Bahraini pearl industry), Al Fateh Mosque (one of the largest mosques in the world, capacity 7000 worshippers, fibreglass dome 60 tonnes the largest of its kind, open to non-Muslims), Bahrain National Museum, Bahrain World Trade Center (signature 240m skyscraper with 3 wind turbines integrated between the 2 towers, first skyscraper in the world to integrate renewable energy production 2008), Tree of Life ("Tree of Life", 400-year-old solitary tree in the desert with no apparent water source, botanical mystery attracting many visitors, signature of Bahrain), Riffa Fort (19th century), Pearling Trail UNESCO 2012 (testimony of the historic pearling industry of the Gulf). Excursions: Hawar Islands (south-west archipelago, preserved beaches, 1h boat access), Royal Camel Farm (royal camels), F1 Sakhir circuits (Bahrain International Circuit host of the Bahrain Grand Prix in March since 2004 = 1st F1 race of the Middle East). Try machboos (Bahraini spicy rice, Arab national dish), muhammar (sweet rice with dates, Persian heritage), harees (spicy porridge for Ramadan), Arabic coffee with dates, fresh fruit juices, alcohol available in international hotels. Bahrain airport (BAH).
koweit:en: Kuwait City (Madinat Al Kuwait), capital of Kuwait at the extreme north of the Persian Gulf between Saudi Arabia and Iraq: 4.5 million inhabitants in Kuwait of which 70% expatriates (Asian mainly). Ultra-rich emirate thanks to oil (8% of proven world reserves), Al Sabah constitutional monarchy since 1752. Marked by the Iraqi invasion of 2 August 1990 which triggered the Gulf War and the liberation by US-led coalition February 1991 (Operation Desert Storm), 700 oil wells voluntarily set on fire by Saddam Hussein's army during its retreat = one of the worst ecological disasters of the 20th century. November is my favourite month (8.0/10): 18-26°C, 12% rain, this is the ideal dry season. October-March stay excellent (6.1-8.4/10). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 11-31°C, pleasant dry and hot climate. From May to September drop to 3.1/10 due to extreme heatwave (26-45°C, sometimes 53°C+ = among the highest temperatures in the world, Kuwait and neighbouring Iraq regularly dispute the world temperature record, reached 54°C June 2016 at Mitribah Kuwait). Highlights: Kuwait Towers (signature visual of Kuwait = 3 towers with spheres 1979 by Danish architects Lindstrom + Bjorn = symbolise the blue water, main height 187m with panoramic rotating restaurant, large sphere water reservoir 1 million gallons = essential need in desert country), Grand Mosque of Kuwait (the largest mosque in the country, capacity 10,000 worshippers), Sheikh Jaber Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah Mosque, Kuwait National Museum (national museum destroyed-looted during the Iraqi invasion 1990-91, restored since), Tareq Rajab Museum (exceptional private collection of Islamic art, including calligraphies and jewellery), The Avenues (one of the largest shopping centres in the Middle East, signature of Kuwait), Souk Al-Mubarakiya (traditional souk dating 200 years), Liberation Tower (372m, 5th tallest telecommunications tower in the world, tribute to the liberation 1991), Failaka Island 30 min by ferry (historical island with Hellenistic ruins + village abandoned after the Iraqi invasion, memorial atmosphere), Sadu House (museum of traditional Bedouin weaving). Safety: Kuwait = one of the safest countries in the world, near non-existent crime, but unstable northern border with Iraq. Try machboos (Arab national dish), margoog (Bedouin stew), gers ogaily (Kuwaiti cardamom-saffron cake), darabeel (porridge), Arabic coffee with dates. Alcohol strictly forbidden in Kuwait (one of the strictest countries in the Gulf, prison sentences for possession). Kuwait International airport (KWI).
salalah:en: Salalah, 2nd city of the Sultanate of Oman in the Dhofar province in the extreme south, 1000 km south of Muscat near the Yemeni border: 200,000 inhabitants. Unique climate in the Middle East thanks to the Khareef = Indian Ocean monsoon that transforms the usually arid region into a lush green jungle from June to September, making Salalah the "Arab Switzerland" for Gulf inhabitants fleeing the heatwave in Riyadh-Doha-Dubai. January-February are my favourite months (8.6/10): 18-27°C, 2-4% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From November to March, it's the traditional dry season: 18-30°C, classic temperate Arab climate. From June to September, it's the Khareef = paradoxical tourist high season: 24-31°C, 27-41% rain (rare in the Middle East), constant fogs ("fog of Khareef"), spectacular temporarily verdant landscapes (rare worldwide phenomenon: land becomes jungle, waterfalls appear in the wadis, grass covers the usually desert mountains), Khareef Festival July-August very popular for Gulf families, hotel prices x4-x5. From April to May and October, expect 23-33°C: hot but no monsoon, few tourists. Highlights: Salalah Beach (Al-Mughsayl, golden sand beach 12 km, palm trees, calmer than the Muscat beaches), Marneef Cave and Al-Mughsayl Blowholes 50 km away (natural geysers where the sea is projected 20m high through holes in the limestone rock = signature spectacular phenomenon of Salalah, especially in June-September when the sea is rough), Taqa Fortifications (19th century), Ruins of Al-Baleed (UNESCO, former city of Zafar = incense trading port 9th-16th century, archaeological vestiges on the seafront), Land of Frankincense Museum ("Land of Incense", modern museum on the Frankincense Route = the Dhofar is one of the only regions in the world where the frankincense tree Boswellia sacra grows, which produces the olibanum incense resin used since Antiquity by Egyptians, Romans, Hebrews, Magi of the Gospels, Omani incense was more precious than gold at the time of Jesus), Wadi Darbat 30 km away (oasis with lake and waterfall in Khareef season June-September, improbable tropical landscapes in the Middle East), Tomb of the Prophet Job (Nabi Ayyub, place of Muslim and Christian pilgrimage according to Quranic tradition, on hill with panorama), Sumhuram 35 km away (UNESCO, ancient port of Ubar = "Atlantis of the sands" mentioned by Pliny the Elder, ruins 2nd century BC), Banana and coconut plantations around Salalah (unique in Arabia thanks to the Khareef climate), Wadi Hinna and Ayn Hamran (natural springs in the Dhofar mountains). Excursions: Empty Quarter 4h away (Rub al-Khali, "Empty Quarter" = the largest continuous sand desert in the world 650,000 km², Saudi Arabian border, dunes 250m high, organised 4x4 expeditions 2-3 days), Mirbat 1h30 away (historic 19th-century whaling port, former capital of the Omani Mahra state), Dragon's Blood Trees around Mughsayl (signature endemic tree). Try harees (signature Arab spicy porridge), shuwa, Omani halwa, Arabic coffee with local Salalah Mabsali dates, fermented camel milk. Salalah airport (SLL) with domestic flights from Muscat (1h30) and international Doha, Dubai in Khareef season.
costa-rica:en: Costa Rica, country in Central America between Nicaragua and Panama: 5 million inhabitants. "Pura Vida" = national motto that sums up the Costa Rican philosophy. Country unique in the world: no army since 1948 (constitutional abolition, savings redirected to education and health), stable democracy since 1948 (one of the oldest in Latin America), world capital of ecotourism (28% of the territory in national parks and protected reserves, 6% of world biodiversity on 0.03% of emerged lands, 500,000 species recorded). February is my favourite month (8.9/10): 16-25°C, 6% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January and March stay excellent (8.6/10). From December to April, it's the dry season "verano": 14-26°C, this is the tourist high season, hotel prices x2-x3. From May to November, it's the green season "invierno": 71-89% rainfall in San José + central Pacific, but Caribbean east coast = inverted climate (dry in May-October, humid in November-February = stop "winter" "summer" and prefer "dry"/"green" season), greenest landscapes for photo, reduced hotel prices. Highlights / Regions: San José capital (see dedicated review), Volcán Arenal 3h from San José (the most active volcano in Central America until 2010 with spectacular night lava flows, dormant since 2010 but spectacular landscape intact, Tabacón hot springs nearby, hiking with view of ideally conical volcano 1670m), Monteverde (see dedicated review), Manuel Antonio National Park (central Pacific coast, the most visited of the Costa Rican parks, sloths + capuchins + iguanas easily accessible, turquoise beaches in the heart of preserved jungle), Tortuguero National Park (north Caribbean coast, accessible only by boat-plane, green turtle nesting July-October = signature of the Caribbean of Costa Rica), Corcovado National Park (Osa peninsula south Pacific, one of the last primary jungles in Central America, jaguars-tapirs, accessible with guide from Drake Bay, extreme experience), Guanacaste in the north-west Pacific (more accessible beaches Tamarindo Nosara Santa Teresa surf, Santa Rosa national park, drier climate), Caribbean Coast (Puerto Viejo, Cahuita, distinct Afro-Caribbean atmosphere, reggae, jerk chicken, snorkelling), Cordillera de Talamanca (mountains 3819m highest point Cerro Chirripó, hiking, indigenous Bribri people). Activities: zipline (Costa Rica = inventor country of the tourist canopy zipline concept in 1979), rafting (Río Pacuare one of the top 10 worldwide), surfing (Pavones south Pacific coast = 2nd longest left wave in the world 800m), diving (Coco Island UNESCO marine park, hammerhead sharks by the hundred), wildlife observation (sloths, monkeys, quetzal, jaguar, humpback whales south Pacific coast July-October). Try gallo pinto (rice black beans national breakfast), casado (traditional platter), olla de carne (beef stew), ceviche, Costa Rican coffee (one of the best in the world, Tarrazú altitude region), Imperial beer (national beer), guaro (cane eau-de-vie).
montenegro:en: Montenegro (Crna Gora = "Black Mountain"), small Balkan country on the Adriatic: 620,000 inhabitants. Peaceful independence from Serbia 2006 by referendum (the last Yugoslav separation), EU candidate since 2010, uses the euro despite non-EU member. Atmosphere mix of Serbian Orthodox + Venetian Dalmatian + Balkan mountain culture. June is my favourite month (9.1/10): 21-28°C, 29% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. September stays excellent (8.3/10). From May to September, it's the tourist high season: 12-31°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-31°C). From November to March, expect 5-17°C and 34-49% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, snow in the Durmitor mountains 2522m. Coast highlights: Bay of Kotor (Boka Kotorska, Adriatic fjord unique in the Mediterranean, considered one of the most beautiful fjords in the world, UNESCO 1979), Kotor (UNESCO, fortified Venetian medieval old town 12th century at the foot of Mt Lovćen, 1350 stairs to the panoramic San Giovanni fortress 280m signature), Perast (delightful baroque village on the bay edge facing 2 islets including Our Lady of the Rocks = artificial islet built by the Kotor sailors 15th-18th centuries on voluntarily sunken wrecks, signature church), Budva (15th-century fortified old town + main beach resort of Montenegro, dynamic Russian-Eastern European tourist atmosphere), Sveti Stefan (14th-century medieval fortified peninsula transformed into ultra-exclusive 5*+ Aman hotel-village 1960-2007, signature visual of Montenegro = postcard image of the country, currently limited access), Herceg Novi (at the entrance of the Bay of Kotor, fortification town, tropical palm trees), Petrovac and Bar (calmer beach resorts in the south), Ulcinj (extreme south Albanian border, Albanian-speaking community, 12 km of beach Velika Plaža = the longest beach in Montenegro). Mountain sites: Mt Lovćen (national park, mausoleum of poet-prince Petar Petrović-Njegoš 19th century at 1657m), Cetinje (former royal and religious capital of Montenegro pre-1918, Cetinje monastery 15th century), Durmitor National Park (UNESCO 1980, mountains 2522m, glacial Black Lake Crno jezero, Tara gorges = one of the deepest gorges in Europe 1300m, Tara River rafting), Žabljak (winter ski resort). Excursions: Dubrovnik 30 min away (Croatia, simple border), Mostar 3h away (Bosnia-Herzegovina), Tirana 4h away (Albania). Try kacamak (Montenegrin polenta), pršut (signature Montenegrin dried ham), kajmak (Balkan fermented butter), riblja čorba (fish soup), Vranac signature Montenegrin red wine (one of the great little-known Balkan wines), rakija (eau-de-vie). Tivat (TIV) and Podgorica (TGD) airports.
chypre:en: Cyprus, island-country in the north-east of the Eastern Mediterranean between Greece, Turkey, Lebanon, Israel: 1.2 million inhabitants on 9251 km², 3rd largest Mediterranean island after Sicily and Sardinia. Island divided since 1974: 60% in the south = Republic of Cyprus (EU and eurozone member), 35% in the north = Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus (recognised only by Turkey), Green Line crosses the capital Nicosia (the last divided capital in the world after the fall of the Berlin Wall 1989). June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 16-30°C, 4% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. May and October stay excellent (8.7-9.0/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 16-32°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-35°C). From November to March, expect 6-16°C and 18-43% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, snow possible in the Troodos mountains (1952m, skiing possible Mt Olympus December-March), ideal for visiting without crowds. Cyprus South highlights: Nicosia (Lefkosia, divided capital, crossing of the Green Line at the Ledra Street point on foot with passport, 16th-century fortified Venetian old town with 11 bastions, Selimiye Mosque = former Saint Sophia Cathedral 13th-century Gothic on Turkish side), Paphos (see dedicated review), Larnaca (see dedicated review), Limassol (Limasol, dynamic beach resort with smart marina, Carnival), Ayia Napa (festive youth resort), Akamas Peninsula (wild peninsula at the extreme west, Lara beach protected for the nesting of green sea turtles Caretta caretta), Petra tou Romiou ("Aphrodite's Rock", beach with rocks where according to Greek mythology the goddess Aphrodite was born from the foam of the sea, signature visual of Cyprus), Choirokoitia (UNESCO, Neolithic site 7th millennium BC, one of the oldest human dwellings in the Mediterranean), Painted Churches of the Troodos UNESCO 1985 (10 12th-16th century Byzantine churches in the Troodos mountains with exceptional frescoes), Kykkos Monastery (richest monastery in Cyprus 11th century, where a young Tassos Papadopoulos = future president — was a pupil). Cyprus North sites (with Green Line passage): Famagusta (Gazimagusa, fortified Venetian old town, Othello tower where Shakespeare set his play), Salamis (spectacular Greco-Roman vestiges), Karpaz Peninsula (wild extreme east peninsula, wild donkeys, preserved beaches). Try Cypriot meze (assortment of 20-30 traditional small dishes local institution), souvla (skewers), halloumi (Cypriot IGP cheese, to grill), sheftalies (sausages), kleftiko (oven-baked lamb), commandaria (medieval sweet wine, one of the oldest wines still produced in the world, mentioned by Richard the Lionheart 12th century). Larnaca (LCA) and Paphos (PFO) airports.
republique-dominicaine:en: The Dominican Republic, Caribbean country of the island of Hispaniola shared with Haiti: 11 million inhabitants on 49,000 km² (the eastern 2/3 of Hispaniola). 1st Caribbean beach tourism destination (popular all-inclusive packages since the 90s), especially around Punta Cana. January is my favourite month (8.5/10): 21-28°C, 36% rain, end of the dry season, sea at 26°C, ideal temperatures. From December to April, it's the tourist high season: 21-29°C, 36-63% rain, hotel prices x2 (resorts), bookings 3-6 months ahead for Christmas/New Year. From June to November, it's the cyclonic season: 66-77% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Maria and Irma in 2017 hit). Regions to know: Punta Cana (east coast, 50+ all-inclusive resorts, Bávaro beach ranked among the best in the world, golf 6 signature courses Tom Fazio/Jack Nicklaus/Pete Dye, direct Paris-Punta Cana flight), La Romana and Bayahibe (south-east coast, Casa de Campo exclusive resort, Saona island boat-paradise beach excursion), Santo Domingo (capital, Zona Colonial UNESCO 1990 = 1st European city of America founded in 1496 by Bartolomé Colón brother of Christopher, first cathedral 1512, first university 1538, first street Calle de las Damas), Puerto Plata (north coast, more traditional and less touristic), Samaná (north-east peninsula, humpback whales in migration January-March, El Limón waterfall), Pico Duarte (3098m, highest point of the Caribbean, 2-3 day ascent), Lake Enriquillo (salt lake below sea level, American crocodiles and iguanas). Try mangú (morning plantain mash), sancocho (soup), la bandera (white rice + red beans + meat), mofongo, presidente beer, Brugal/Barceló rum.
perou:en: Peru, Andean South American country: 33 million inhabitants on 1,285,000 km² (3rd largest in South America after Brazil and Argentina). 3 classic regions: Costa desert (Pacific coast with Lima), Sierra Andean (Andean cordillera), Selva Amazonian (60% of the territory). Cradle of the Inca civilisation and pre-Inca cultures (Caral, Chavín, Moche, Nazca, Wari, Chimú). February is my favourite month (9.0/10) in Lima: 21-26°C, 14% rain, austral summer on the coast. From May to September, it's the tourist high season for the Andes (notably Machu Picchu): 14-19°C, 0-2% rain at altitude, clear sky. From December to March, it's the rainy season at altitude: Machu Picchu humid and foggy but green landscapes, Inca Trail closed in February for maintenance. Regions/sites to know: Lima (capital, most populated city, see dedicated review), Machu Picchu (UNESCO 1983, one of the most famous sites in the world, 15th-century Inca citadel at 2430m altitude rediscovered by Hiram Bingham in 1911, access by train from Cusco + Aguas Calientes + bus, or trek Inca Trail 4 days/3 nights, 500 visitors/day limit, booking 6 months ahead), Cusco (UNESCO, former Inca capital, base for Machu Picchu — see dedicated review), Sacred Valley (Pisac, Ollantaytambo, Moray, Salineras), Lake Titicaca (3812m, shared with Bolivia, floating Uros islands made of reeds, Taquile island), Nazca Lines (UNESCO, geoglyphs to see from plane, archaeological mysteries), Arequipa (UNESCO, "Ciudad Blanca" in white volcanic stone, base for Cañon del Colca), Iquitos (Peruvian Amazon, accessible only by plane or boat), Trujillo and Chan Chan (UNESCO, largest pre-Columbian adobe city in the world). Try ceviche (national dish, raw fish marinated in lemon), lomo saltado, ají de gallina, anticuchos (beef heart skewers), pisco sour, chicha morada.
colombie:en: Colombia, South American country in the extreme north-west of the continent: 52 million inhabitants, only South American country with access to both Caribbean AND Pacific. Considered the "most diverse" of South America (Andes + Caribbean + Pacific + Amazon + eastern Llanos plains). Image transformed since 2016 (peace agreement with the FARC, end of the longest civil conflict on the continent), strong tourism growth. September is my favourite month (7.7/10) in Andean zone: 9-18°C, 34% rain. From August to September and December-February, it's the tourist high season: climate varies by altitude (hot coastal 28°C, cool mountainous 18°C). From March to June and October-November, it's the rainy season in Andean zone. All year round, the absence of marked seasons (apart from rainy/dry) allows visiting (Colombia = country of "micro-climates" by altitude). Regions to know: Bogotá (capital 2640m, Gold Museum — see dedicated review), Cartagena de Indias (Caribbean UNESCO — see dedicated review), Medellín ("city of eternal spring" 1500m, transformed since the Pablo Escobar era died in 1993, Comuna 13 graffiti and urban cable car), Cali (capital of Colombian salsa), Eje Cafetero ("coffee axis", Salento, Filandia, Cocora Valley with its giant 60m wax palm trees, coffee fincas to visit), San Andrés and Providencia (Caribbean islands 750 km off the Colombian continental coast, closer to Nicaragua), Tayrona National Park (virgin Caribbean, jungle hike to Cabo San Juan), Colombian Amazon (Leticia, Brazil-Peru border). Safety: very improved situation since 2016, but avoid the rural zones of the south (Putumayo, Cauca, Nariño) where some dissident groups persist. Bogotá and Medellín = standard urban vigilance. Try bandeja paisa, ajiaco, arepa, lechona, Geisha coffee, aguardiente.
bagdad:en: Baghdad, capital of Iraq on the Tigris River: 7 million inhabitants. Former capital of the Abbasid Caliphate 762-1258 = one of the largest cities in the medieval world with 1.2 million inhabitants in the 9th century, cultural and scientific capital of classical Islam ("House of Wisdom" Bayt al-Hikma translating Greek texts into Arabic and preserving ancient knowledge for later European Renaissance). Destroyed by the Mongols 1258, never regained its historical rank. Major Mesopotamian archaeological sites: Babylon, Ur, Uruk, Nineveh, Nippur = cradle of writing (3500 BC), mathematics, law (Hammurabi Code 1750 BC), astronomy. April is my favourite month (9.0/10): 16-30°C, 7% rain, this is the ideal dry season. March-October-November stay excellent (7.6-8.5/10). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 4-23°C, dry and tempered climate. From May to September drop to 3.9-6.0/10 due to extreme heatwave (24-46°C, sometimes 53°C+ = among the highest temperatures in the world, extreme desert climate). Highlights: Mausoleum of Imam Kadhim ("Al-Kadhimiya Mosque", one of the 4 largest Shia mosques in the world, signature golden dome and minarets, sacred place for Shias), Tahrir Square ("Liberation Square", historic central square), National Museum of Iraq (one of the most important archaeological museums in the world, Sumerian-Akkadian-Babylonian-Assyrian civilisations, partially looted in April 2003 during the US invasion, some pieces recovered later), Mutanabbi Street ("booksellers' street", traditional book market, Baghdad cultural signature, every Friday). Major archaeological sites = this is why one visits Iraq: Babylon 90 km south (UNESCO 2019, former capital of the Babylonian empire of Hammurabi 1792-1750 BC + Nebuchadnezzar 605-562 BC who built the Hanging Gardens = one of the 7 Wonders of the ancient world today disappeared, partially rebuilt by Saddam Hussein 1980 controversially with bricks marked "Saddam Reconstructor of Babylon"), Ur in the south (former Sumerian city 3rd millennium BC, preserved signature ziggurat, considered the birthplace of Abraham according to biblical tradition), Najaf and Karbala (the 2 most sacred Shia cities in the world, sanctuaries of Imam Ali and Imam Hussein = places of pilgrimage for 25 million Shias annually during Arbaeen which rivals the Hajj of Mecca by its massive attendance). Safety: inadvisable France Diplomacy for the entirety of Iraq except autonomous Iraqi Kurdistan (Erbil, Sulaymaniyah, Dohuk), residual ISIS terrorism + Shia/Sunni sectarian tensions + post-2003 US invasion political violence. Very inadvisable travel except professional accompaniment and expert organisation. Try masgouf (grilled Tigris carp signature of Baghdad), Iraqi kebab, dolma (stuffed vegetables), kubba (meatballs), tea with cardamom, tamarind juice, Iraqi dates (the best historical world producer). Baghdad International airport (BGW).
lagos:en: Lagos, in Portuguese Algarve: 22,000 inhabitants, beach resort at the extreme south-west of Portugal. Sculpted limestone cliff beaches (the most famous in Portugal), marine caves (Ponta da Piedade), more traditional atmosphere than the concrete-bedded resorts of central Algarve (Albufeira, Vilamoura). August is my favourite month (6.9/10): 24-28°C, 71% rain. Lagos never tops 6.9/10 (temperate oceanic climate, more humid than the neighbouring Canaries). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 24-30°C, sea at 21°C (cool, Atlantic current), hotel prices x2-x3, packed beaches. From November to March, expect 25-32°C and 18-60% rain: mild for Portugal, European snowbirds in wintering (golf courses open year-round). Highlights: Ponta da Piedade (signature of Lagos, cliffs and caves accessible by boat or kayak from the port, 30 min coastal walk from the centre), Praia Dona Ana (one of the most beautiful beaches in Portugal according to many rankings), Praia do Camilo (200 steps to descend), Praia do Pinhão, Meia Praia (long 4 km beach to the east, calmer), Fortified old town (16th century, Mercado Municipal, Igreja de Santa Maria, Forte da Ponta da Bandeira), Cape Saint Vincent 40 min away (extreme south-west of continental Europe, lighthouse, 75m cliffs). Sagres 35 min away (Henry the Navigator's School of Navigation cape, surf, bohemian atmosphere). Try cataplana de marisco (seafood cassolette), bacalhau à brás, pastéis de nata, Algarve wines (Negra Mole reds), ginja (cherry liqueur). Logistics: Lagos 1h from Faro (main Algarve airport), 4h from Lisbon by train or bus.
abuja:en: Abuja, capital of Nigeria in the centre of the country: 800,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 3 million in urban area. Planned capital built ex-nihilo in the 1980s-1991 (previous capital = Lagos south coast) for geopolitical reasons (neutral central position between the 3 majority ethnicities Yoruba south-west + Igbo south-east + Hausa north, more accessible, more defendable). Crescent urban plan inspired by Brasilia + Canberra, calmer and cleaner atmosphere than chaotic Lagos. Official capital since 1991. November is my favourite month (8.2/10): 19-32°C, 9% rain, this is the ideal dry season + refreshing Saharan harmattan. January-February-March-October-December stay excellent (6.1-7.3/10). From November to February, it's the dry season: 19-34°C, pleasant dry and hot climate. From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 23-36°C. From June to September, it's the West African monsoon: 47-78% rainfall.
